text: In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.
reflection: Before anything existed, God was already there. He did not need the world, but He made it because He wanted to. The same God who spoke the stars into place knows you by name and chose to create you.
question: What does it mean to you that the God of all creation wanted you here?

text: Yahweh God said, 'It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make him a helper comparable to him.'
reflection: God did not create you from far away. He came close, formed you with His own hands, and then breathed His very life into you. You are alive right now because God shared something of Himself with you.
question: How does knowing that God personally breathed life into you change the way you see yourself today?

text: I will put hostility between you and the woman, and between your offspring and her offspring. He will bruise your head, and you will bruise his heel.
reflection: At the very moment everything fell apart, God did not walk away from you. Before He spoke one word of punishment, He announced that someone was coming to set things right for you.
question: What does it mean to you that when you are at your worst, God is already planning your rescue?

text: If you do well, won't it be lifted up? If you don't do well, sin crouches at the door. Its desire is for you, but you are to rule over it.
reflection: God saw the anger building in Cain and did not stay silent. He came to him and said the danger was right at the door. God does the same for you. He does not watch you drift toward trouble without speaking up.
question: How have you sensed God warning you recently?
hook: Sin Is Crouching at Your Door

text: Enoch walked with God, and he was not found, for God took him.
reflection: God made you in His likeness. That was not an accident or an afterthought. It was His deliberate choice. Even now, through all the brokenness in the world, God's original design for you still stands.
question: How does knowing you were made in God's likeness shape the way you see yourself today?
hook: Made in the Image of God

text: But Noah found favor in Yahweh's eyes.
reflection: God sees everything, including the darkest thoughts you try to hide. But this is an invitation to honesty. God already knows your heart, and He still reaches for you.
question: What would it look like to stop hiding and let God into the parts of your heart you keep to yourself?

text: Yahweh said to Noah, 'Come with all of your household into the ship, for I have seen your righteousness before me in this generation.'
reflection: God did not say "go into the ark." He said "come." He was already there, inviting Noah in. God does the same with you. He never asks you to walk into something alone. He goes first and then calls you to follow.
question: Where is God inviting you to come to Him right now?

text: God remembered Noah, all the animals, and all the livestock that were with him in the ship; and God made a wind to pass over the earth. The waters subsided.
reflection: After the worst disaster the world had ever seen, Noah stopped and worshiped God before doing anything else. That is a challenge for you too.
question: When hard things end in your life, do you rush forward, or do you stop and thank God for carrying you through?

text: I set my rainbow in the cloud, and it will be a sign of a covenant between me and the earth.
reflection: God says your life is sacred because you are made in His image. That is not something you earned. It is something God gave you the moment He created you.
question: How does knowing that God stamped His own image on you change the way you treat yourself and the people around you?

text: These are the families of the sons of Noah, by their generations, according to their nations. From these, the nations divided in the earth after the flood.
reflection: Nimrod built his kingdom on his own ambition, without any thought of God. You have dreams and goals too.
question: Are you building your life with God at the center, or could you be building something that leaves Him out?

text: Therefore its name was called Babel, because there Yahweh confused the language of all the earth.
reflection: Terah started the journey toward Canaan but never made it there. He stopped halfway and stayed. That can happen to you too.
question: Are there places in your life where God has been calling you forward, but you have settled and stopped moving?

text: I will make of you a great nation. I will bless you and make your name great. You will be a blessing.
reflection: God promised to bless Abram and make him a blessing to others. God has given you gifts and opportunities too.
question: How could you use what God has placed in your life to become a blessing to the people around you?

text: Abram said to Lot, 'Please, let there be no strife between you and me, and between my herdsmen and your herdsmen; for we are relatives.'
reflection: God promised Abram a future he could not yet see. You may be waiting for something God has spoken over your life.
question: Can you trust that what God promises you, He will deliver, even when the timing is not yours?
hook: God Gives More Than You Lost

text: 'Blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand.' Abram gave him a tenth of all.
reflection: Right after the battle, God sent someone to refresh Abram with bread and wine. God does the same for you.
question: When was the last time you paused after something hard and let God meet you with His provision and His peace?

text: He believed in Yahweh, and he credited it to him for righteousness.
reflection: God told Abram not to be afraid, and He said the same thing to you. He is your shield and your reward.
question: What fear are you carrying right now that you need to hand over to the God who promises to protect you and provide for you?

text: She called the name of Yahweh who spoke to her, 'You are a God who sees,' for she said, 'Have I even stayed alive after seeing him?'
reflection: Hagar was alone and afraid, but God heard her. He sees you the same way.
question: When you feel invisible or forgotten, do you believe that God is paying attention to your pain and that He knows exactly what you are going through?

text: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, Yahweh appeared to Abram and said to him, 'I am God Almighty. Walk before me and be blameless.'
reflection: God gave Abram a new name before his life matched it. God sees who you are becoming, not just who you are now.
question: What name do you think God is calling you, and are you willing to trust His vision for your life?

text: Is anything too hard for Yahweh? At the set time I will return to you, when the season comes around, and Sarah will have a son.
reflection: God asked if anything was too hard for Him. That question is for you too.
question: What impossible situation are you facing right now, and do you believe that the God who kept His promise to Sarah can keep His promises to you?
hook: Nothing Is Too Hard for God

text: When God destroyed the cities of the plain, God remembered Abraham, and sent Lot out of the middle of the overthrow.
reflection: Lot's wife was being rescued, but she looked back at what she was leaving behind. God is often pulling you away from something harmful.
question: Is there anything in your past that you keep looking back at instead of trusting God and moving forward?

text: God said to him in the dream, 'Yes, I know that in the integrity of your heart you have done this, and I also withheld you from sinning against me.'
reflection: Even after Abraham failed, God called him a prophet and used him. Your failures do not cancel what God has planned for you.
question: How does knowing that God still calls you His even after you mess up change the way you see yourself today?

text: Yahweh visited Sarah as he had said, and Yahweh did to Sarah as he had spoken.
reflection: God did exactly what He said He would do, at precisely the right time. If you have been waiting for God to come through on something He promised, do not lose heart. He has not forgotten you.
question: What promise are you holding onto, and can you trust God with the timing?

text: Abraham called the name of that place 'Yahweh Will Provide.' As it is said to this day, 'On Yahweh's mountain, it will be provided.'
reflection: After the hardest moment of Abraham's life, God poured out His greatest blessing. God does not waste your pain. The things that cost you the most are often the doorway to what God has been preparing for you.
question: What hard thing might God be using in your life right now to bring about something greater?

text: 'I am a stranger and a foreigner living with you. Give me a possession of a burying-place with you, that I may bury my dead out of my sight.'
reflection: Abraham called himself a stranger even after decades with God. If you have ever felt like you do not quite belong in this world, that feeling might be closer to the truth than you realize. God made you for more.
question: Where does your sense of not belonging point you toward God?

text: 'Blessed be Yahweh, the God of my master Abraham, who has not forsaken his loving kindness and his truth toward my master.'
reflection: Every good thing Abraham had came from God, not from his own cleverness. He trusted, and God blessed him beyond anything he could have planned. God wants to do the same for you.
question: What would change if you stopped relying on your own plans and let God lead you?

text: Yahweh said to her, 'Two nations are in your womb. Two peoples will be separated from your body. The one people will be stronger than the other people. The elder will serve the younger.'
reflection: Abraham did not finish his life scrambling for more. He died full, at peace. That kind of ending comes from trusting God through every season of your life.
question: What kind of ending are you building toward right now, and does the way you live today reflect trust in God?

text: Yahweh appeared to him the same night, and said, 'I am the God of Abraham your father. Don't be afraid, for I am with you, and will bless you, and multiply your offspring for my servant Abraham's sake.'
reflection: When everything around Isaac was falling apart, God said stay where you are and I will bless you. God does not always take you out of the hard place. Sometimes He meets you in it.
question: Where is God asking you to stay and trust Him instead of running?

text: God give you of the dew of the sky, of the fatness of the earth, and plenty of grain and new wine.
reflection: This blessing was part of God's plan before Jacob was even born. No trick put it in motion, and no mistake could have stopped it. What God has promised for your life cannot be stolen from you.
question: Are you trusting His plan, or are you still trying to make things happen on your own?

text: 'Behold, I am with you, and will keep you, wherever you go, and will bring you again into this land. For I will not leave you until I have done that which I have spoken of to you.'
reflection: God spoke these words to a man running from the mess he made. Jacob did not deserve this promise. But God met him anyway and said I will not leave you.
question: If God speaks this kind of love to a runaway, what do you think He is saying over your life right now?

text: Jacob served seven years for Rachel. They seemed to him but a few days, for the love he had for her.
reflection: Leah was unloved and she knew it. But God looked at her pain and answered it. He did not fix her marriage, but He showed her she was seen. You may feel invisible to those you love most. God has already looked at your affliction.
question: What would change if you truly believed He sees you?
hook: God Sees What No One Else Sees

text: God remembered Rachel, and God listened to her, and opened her womb.
reflection: Even Laban, a man who had cheated and used Jacob for years, admitted that God had blessed him because of Jacob. God can use your presence to bless the people around you in ways you never planned.
question: Where has God placed you, and who might be receiving His blessing simply because you are there?

text: Yahweh said to Jacob, 'Return to the land of your fathers, and to your relatives, and I will be with you.'
reflection: Jacob worked twenty years under a man who cheated him at every turn. The heat, the cold, the sleepless nights. God saw every moment. Whatever you are enduring right now that feels unnoticed, God has seen your affliction and the labor of your hands.
question: How does that change how you face today?

text: He said, 'Your name will no longer be called Jacob, but Israel; for you have fought with God and with men, and have prevailed.'
reflection: God changed Jacob's name that night. The man who had schemed his way through life was broken and remade. God does the same with you. He takes your struggles and your need for control and makes you new.
question: What old identity is God asking you to let go of?

text: Jacob said, 'Please, no, if I have now found favor in your sight, then receive my present from my hand, because I have seen your face, as one sees the face of God, and you were pleased with me.'
reflection: Jacob expected punishment, but God gave him an embrace. For twenty years he feared this moment, and God turned it into reconciliation. You carry fears like that too. God can turn your deepest fear into mercy you never expected.
question: What are you dreading that God might already be preparing to resolve?

text: The sons of Jacob came in from the field when they heard it. The men were grieved, and they were very angry.
reflection: Their pain was real, but their revenge nearly destroyed the family. When you act on rage, you create more wreckage than the wound itself. God does not ask you to hide your pain, but to bring it to Him first.
question: What anger do you need to hand to God before it causes even more harm?

text: Let us arise, and go up to Bethel. I will make there an altar to God, who answered me in the day of my distress, and was with me on the way which I went.
reflection: Jacob had to clean house before he could go back to God. The idols had to go. God calls you to the same. Before you go deeper with Him, you have to let go of what you have been holding onto.
question: What is God asking you to put away?

text: Esau lived in the hill country of Seir. Esau is Edom.
reflection: Esau walked away from the promised land to settle in Seir. He chose comfort over covenant. He traded his inheritance for what he could hold in his hands. You face the same choice every day, whether to chase what feels good now or to hold onto what God has promised.
question: What are you choosing today?

text: Joseph dreamed a dream, and he told it to his brothers, and they hated him all the more.
reflection: Jacob tore his clothes and refused to be comforted. You know that kind of grief, when something you love is ripped away and nothing anyone says helps. But God was not absent in Jacob's darkest moment, and He is not absent in yours.
question: What grief are you carrying that you have not trusted God with?
hook: Grief That Only God Can Hold

text: Judah acknowledged them, and said, 'She is more righteous than I, because I didn't give her to Shelah, my son.'
reflection: Judah demanded death for a sin he himself committed. You do the same thing when you judge others for faults you carry. God brings your hypocrisy to light not to shame you, but to give you a chance to be honest.
question: What are you condemning in someone else that you need to face in yourself?
hook: The Sin You Thought Was Hidden

text: But Yahweh was with Joseph, and showed kindness to him, and gave him favor in the sight of the keeper of the prison.
reflection: Joseph was a slave in a foreign land, and yet God was with him. Your worst circumstances do not determine whether God is present. He is with you right now, in the middle of whatever you are facing.
question: Where in your life do you need to trust that God has not left you?

text: They said to him, 'We have dreamed a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it.' Joseph said to them, 'Don't interpretations belong to God?'
reflection: Joseph was in prison, but he still noticed when someone else was hurting. God calls you to the same thing. Even in your own pain, He has put people around you who need what only you can give.
question: Who near you is hurting that you have not stopped to ask about?

text: Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh, 'For God has made me forget all my toil, and all my father's house.'
reflection: God had not forgotten Joseph. Every year in prison was preparation for this single moment. If you are in a season where nothing makes sense, where your faithfulness seems invisible, God is not late. He is working in ways you cannot see.
question: What might He be preparing you for right now?
hook: From the Dungeon to the Palace

text: They said to one another, 'We are certainly guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul, when he begged us, and we wouldn't listen.'
reflection: Years had passed, but the guilt had not gone away. Joseph's brothers tried to forget, but God would not let them. If something is buried in your past that you have never brought to God, He loves you too much to let you carry it.
question: What are you still carrying?

text: 'May God Almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may release to you your other brother and Benjamin. If I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.'
reflection: Jacob prayed for mercy and then said, "If I lose my children, then I lose them." Maybe you have felt that same weight yourself. That is not giving up.
question: When you have done all you can and the outcome is out of your hands, are you willing to trust God with what you love most?
hook: When All You Can Do Is Trust

text: 'Now therefore, please let your servant stay instead of the boy, my lord's slave; and let the boy go up with his brothers.'
reflection: Judah said, "God has found out the iniquity of your servants." Maybe you have heard those words in your own heart. When God shows you what you have been carrying, it is not punishment. It is where freedom begins.
question: What would it look like for you to stop hiding and let God meet you there?

text: Now don't be grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that you sold me here, for God sent me before you to preserve life.
reflection: Joseph told the brothers who sold him, "God sent me here." Think about what that means for you.
question: If God can use the worst thing that ever happened to you for good, how should you see the people who hurt you?

text: He said, 'I am God, the God of your father. Don't be afraid to go down into Egypt, for there I will make of you a great nation.'
reflection: God called Jacob by name in the middle of the night. He still does that for you. When you are afraid about what comes next, God is not far away. He is speaking your name.
question: Are you listening for Him?

text: Jacob said to Pharaoh, 'The days of the years of my pilgrimage are one hundred thirty years. The days of the years of my life have been few and evil.'
reflection: Jacob stood before the most powerful man on earth and called his own life short and hard. He was not bitter. He was honest. Your life has been hard too, but it is taking you somewhere.
question: What would change if you saw your hard days as part of a journey God is leading?
hook: This Life Is Just a Pilgrimage

text: He blessed Joseph, and said, 'The God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, the God who has fed me all my life long to this day,'
reflection: Think about your whole life from beginning until now. God has been feeding you, keeping you, and carrying you through it all. You may not have seen it then, but he was there. He has never left you on your own.
question: Where can you see that God has been taking care of you all along?
hook: God Fed Me All My Life

text: The scepter will not depart from Judah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until he comes to whom it belongs. The obedience of the peoples will be to him.
reflection: God promised a King would come through Judah. That promise was for you. Everything he has been doing for thousands of years points to the One who came to save you. You are not waiting on an empty promise. The King has already come.
question: How does knowing this change your day?
hook: A King Is Coming from Judah

text: 'As for you, you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good, to save many people alive, as is happening today.'
reflection: Someone may have hurt you deeply, but God can use even that for your good. What was meant to destroy you did not have the final word. God did.
question: What situation in your life do you need to trust God to use for good?

text: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and the more they spread out.
reflection: When life presses down hardest, God is not absent. He takes the very thing meant to break you and uses it to grow you. No one can stop what God has decided to do in your life.
question: Where is God growing you right now through something that was meant to break you?

text: God heard their groaning, and God remembered his covenant with Abraham, with Isaac, and with Jacob.
reflection: Your pain is not invisible to God. When you cry out, he hears every word. God does not forget his promises to you. The same God who heard his people in Egypt hears you right now.
question: What promise from God are you holding onto in the middle of your hardest season?

text: God said to Moses, 'I AM WHO I AM,' and he said, 'You shall tell the children of Israel this: I AM has sent me to you.'
reflection: God did not give Moses a list of reasons to trust him. He said, I AM. That is enough for you too. The God who never changes is the same God standing with you today.
question: What area of your life needs the reminder that the unchanging God is right there with you?

text: Now therefore go, and I will be with your mouth, and teach you what you shall speak.
reflection: You do not need to be talented or gifted for God to use you. He made you and knows what he gave you. When God calls you to something, he promises to be with you.
question: What has God called you to that you have been avoiding because you feel unqualified?
hook: God Will Teach You What to Say

text: Moses returned to Yahweh, and said, 'Lord, why have you brought trouble on this people? Why is it that you have sent me?'
reflection: When you obey God and things get harder, it can feel like he has let you down. But bringing your honest questions to God is exactly what he wants you to do. He is not afraid of your pain or your confusion.
question: What honest question do you need to bring to God today?

text: 'Therefore tell the children of Israel, I am Yahweh, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians.'
reflection: God did not just promise to help a little. He said, I will bring you out, I will free you, I will redeem you. Those promises reach all the way to you today. God is not done working in your life.
question: What burden do you need God to bring you out of today?

text: 'The Egyptians shall know that I am Yahweh when I stretch out my hand on Egypt, and bring the children of Israel out from among them.'
reflection: God does not hide his power forever. He stretches out his hand so that everyone will know who he is. The same God who made Egypt see his power wants you to see it in your life too.
question: Where do you need God to show his power in your situation right now?
hook: They Will Know I Am God

text: Then the magicians said to Pharaoh, 'This is God's finger.' Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he didn't listen to them.
reflection: Even the enemies of God had to admit his power was real, but Pharaoh still refused to listen. God's power does not need anyone's permission. The same God whose finger shook Egypt is working in your life right now.
question: When has God shown you his power in a way you could not explain?

text: 'But indeed for this purpose I have raised you up, to show you my power, and that my name may be declared throughout all the earth.'
reflection: God has a purpose for every hard thing you face. Nothing in your life is random. Even the obstacles that stand against you are being used by God to show his power and make his name known.
question: What hard situation in your life might God be using to display his power to the people watching?

text: That you may tell in the hearing of your son, and of your son's son, what things I have done to Egypt, and my signs which I have done among them.
reflection: What God does in your life is not just for you. He works so that you can tell the story to your children and their children after them. Your testimony is a gift to the next generation.
question: What story of God's faithfulness in your life do you need to make sure gets passed down?

text: But against any of the children of Israel a dog won't even bark, against man or beast, that you may know that Yahweh makes a distinction between the Egyptians and Israel.
reflection: When God looks at you, he sees someone who belongs to him. He makes a distinction between his people and those who do not know him. You are set apart and protected, even when judgment falls around you.
question: How does it change the way you live to know that God has set you apart?

text: The blood shall be to you for a token on the houses where you are. When I see the blood, I will pass over you.
reflection: God did not ask if Israel deserved protection. He said, When I see the blood, I will pass over you. Your safety has never depended on your performance. It rests on the blood that was shed for you.
question: What does it mean for you that God's protection depends on his sacrifice, not yours?

text: Yahweh went before them by day in a pillar of cloud, to lead them on their way, and by night in a pillar of fire, to give them light.
reflection: God did not send Israel into the wilderness and leave them alone. He went ahead of them, every step. He does the same for you. You never walk through the unknown alone because God is already there ahead of you.
question: Where do you need to trust that God is leading you right now?

text: Yahweh will fight for you, and you shall be still.
reflection: The battle you are facing is not yours to win. God says, I will fight for you and you shall be still. You do not need to carry it by yourself. God is asking you to stop striving and let him handle it.
question: What battle do you need to hand over to God today?

text: Yah is my strength and song. He has become my salvation. This is my God, and I will praise him.
reflection: After the greatest deliverance in Israel's history, the first thing they did was sing. God had saved them in a way no one could explain. He is your strength, your song, and your salvation too.
question: When was the last time you stopped to simply praise God for what he has done for you?

text: Then Yahweh said to Moses, 'Behold, I will rain bread from the sky for you.'
reflection: Israel grumbled, and God answered with bread from heaven. He did not wait for the right attitude. He gave them what they needed, one day at a time. God does the same for you, even when your faith is weak.
question: How are you trusting God for what you need today?

text: Moses built an altar, and called its name Yahweh-Nissi.
reflection: After the battle was won, Moses built an altar and called it, The Lord is our Banner. Your victories are not yours alone. They belong to God who fights for you.
question: What battle in your life do you need to let God fight for you?

text: You will surely wear away, both you, and this people that is with you; for the thing is too heavy for you. You are not able to perform it yourself alone.
reflection: You are not meant to carry everything alone. God gave you limits so you would lean on him and on the people he has placed around you. When you try to do it all yourself, you wear out.
question: What weight do you need to share with someone God has placed in your life?

text: 'Now therefore, if you will indeed obey my voice and keep my covenant, then you shall be my own possession from among all peoples.'
reflection: God calls you his own possession, out of all the peoples on earth. That is not because of what you have done but because of who he is. He asks for your obedience not to earn his love but because you already have it.
question: What would change if you believed you are God's treasured possession?

text: You shall have no other gods before me.
reflection: Before God gave any command, he reminded Israel who he was. I am the God who freed you. He starts with love, not demand. The same God who rescued you deserves the first place in your life.
question: What in your life has quietly taken a place that belongs to God alone?

text: If you buy a Hebrew servant, he shall serve six years, and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.
reflection: God built freedom into his law. A servant could not be held forever. In the seventh year, the door opened. This is the heart of God for you. He does not want you trapped. Whatever holds you, God is working toward your freedom.
question: Where do you need God to set you free?

text: 'for that is his only covering, it is his garment for his skin. What would he sleep in?'
reflection: God sees the person everyone else overlooks. He hears the cry no one else responds to. This is who God is for you. When you feel unseen, God says he will hear, for he is gracious.
question: What need are you afraid to bring to God right now?
hook: God Hears the Cry of the Poor

text: You shall serve Yahweh your God, and he will bless your bread and your water, and I will take sickness away from among you.
reflection: God makes you a simple offer. Serve him, and he will take care of everything you need. Your bread, your water, your health. He is not holding back to punish you. He wants to fill your life with good things.
question: What would it look like for you to trust God with your needs today?

text: He took the book of the covenant and read it in the hearing of the people, and they said, 'We will do all that Yahweh has said, and be obedient.'
reflection: God sealed his covenant with blood, and nothing could undo it. That same God has made a covenant with you through Christ. His promises to you are not fragile. They are sealed and settled.
question: How does knowing you are in a sealed covenant with God change the way you live today?
hook: We Will Do All God Said

text: Let them make me a sanctuary, that I may dwell among them.
reflection: God could have ruled from a distance. But he said, make me a place, because I want to be with you. That desire drives everything God does in your life. He is always moving closer, never pulling away.
question: Where are you making room for God's presence today?
hook: Build Me a Place to Dwell

text: You shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen.
reflection: God designed his dwelling with care that should amaze you. The inner curtains were the finest linen in blue, purple, and scarlet. This was not random. God wanted beauty where he met with his people. He brings that same care into your life.
question: How do you see God's detailed hand in your story?

text: You shall command the children of Israel, that they bring to you pure olive oil beaten for the light, to cause a lamp to burn continually.
reflection: Aaron and his sons tended the lamp every evening and morning. God wanted a light that never went out. That same God keeps a light burning for you too. His attention to you never ends. He watches over you day and night.
question: What reminds you that God's presence is always with you?
hook: A Light That Never Goes Out

text: Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment on his heart, when he goes in to the holy place, for a memorial before Yahweh continually.
reflection: The high priest carried Israel's names on his heart before God. Not on a scroll. On his heart. That is how Christ carries you right now. Your name is on his heart before the Father.
question: How does knowing Christ brings you before God on his heart change your day?

text: I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.
reflection: God did not rescue Israel just to set them free. He rescued them so he could live among them. This promise is for you too. God does not watch you from a distance. He comes close and makes his home with you.
question: Where in your life do you sense God making his home with you?

text: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less, than the half shekel, when they give the offering of Yahweh.
reflection: Before God, every soul costs the same. The rich could not pay more and the poor could not pay less. Your standing before God does not depend on what you can bring. It depends on what Christ has already paid.
question: How does knowing that God values your soul equally change the way you see yourself?
hook: The Same Price for Every Soul

text: Speak also to the children of Israel, saying, 'Most certainly you shall keep my Sabbaths; for it is a sign between me and you.'
reflection: God gave the Sabbath as a sign that he is the one who makes you holy. It was not about rules. It was about relationship. Your holiness comes from God, not from your effort.
question: What area of your life do you need to let God sanctify instead of trying to fix on your own?

text: So Yahweh relented of the evil which he said he would do to his people.
reflection: Israel had broken God's covenant with a golden calf. They deserved judgment. But Moses pleaded on their behalf, and God relented. You have an even greater intercessor. Christ stands before God for you right now.
question: When have you seen God's mercy come through when you least deserved it?

text: He said, 'My presence will go with you, and I will give you rest.'
reflection: Moses did not want to go anywhere without God. Neither should you. The greatest blessing in your life is not what God gives you. It is that God himself goes with you.
question: What would you refuse to do without God's presence?
hook: My Presence Will Go with You

text: Yahweh passed by before him, and proclaimed, 'Yahweh! Yahweh, a merciful and gracious God, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness and truth.'
reflection: God proclaimed his own name by describing his heart. Merciful, gracious, slow to anger, overflowing with truth. This is God telling you who he is. When you feel like you have failed too many times, remember these words.
question: What does it change for you to hear God call himself merciful?
hook: This Is What God Is Like

text: They came, everyone whose heart stirred him up, and everyone whom his spirit made willing.
reflection: God invited his people to give toward the tabernacle. No one was forced. They came because their hearts were stirred. God works that way with you. He does not demand with threats. He stirs your heart until giving becomes joy.
question: What is God stirring in your heart to offer him right now?
hook: A Heart That Wants to Give

text: For the stuff they had was sufficient to do all the work, and too much.
reflection: The people gave so much it was too much. God had touched their hearts, and their generosity overflowed. God does the same thing in your life. When he fills you with his presence, giving comes naturally.
question: Where in your life is God producing an overflow you have not yet offered back to him?

text: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood.
reflection: The ark was the most sacred object in the tabernacle, the place where God promised to meet with his people. Bezalel built it with his own hands because God called him by name. God calls you by name too.
question: What has God placed in your hands to build for his purposes?

text: He made the basin of bronze, with its base of bronze, out of the mirrors of the ministering women.
reflection: The women gave their bronze mirrors, the things they used to look at themselves, so God could make a vessel for cleansing. They traded vanity for worship. God invites you to do the same thing.
question: What are you holding onto that God could turn into something far more beautiful for his purposes?
hook: What She Gave Up for God

text: Moses saw all the work, and behold, they had done it as Yahweh had commanded. They had done it exactly so. Moses blessed them.
reflection: They did everything exactly as God had commanded. Not one detail was changed or left out. And when Moses saw their faithfulness, he blessed them. God sees your faithfulness too. He knows when you obey him in the small things no one else notices.
question: What act of quiet obedience is God asking from you today?
hook: They Did It Just as God Said

text: Then the cloud covered the Tent of Meeting, and Yahweh's glory filled the tabernacle.
reflection: Everything they built led to this moment. The cloud came down and God's glory filled the tabernacle. God dwelling with his people was the whole point. God is doing the same in your life. He wants to fill what you offer him with his presence.
question: Where are you making room for his glory?

text: Its innards and its legs shall be washed with water. The priest shall burn the whole on the altar, for a burnt offering, an offering made by fire, of a pleasant aroma to Yahweh.
reflection: Every part of the burnt offering was consumed on the altar. Nothing was held back. The fire turned the whole sacrifice into something pleasing to God. This shows you what total surrender looks like. God invites you to bring all of yourself to him.
question: What would it look like for you to hold nothing back?

text: Every offering of your grain offering you shall season with salt. You shall not allow the salt of the covenant of your God to be lacking from your grain offering.
reflection: Salt preserves and does not fade. God required it in every offering because his covenant with you is the same way. It does not expire or weaken. God's promise to you is preserved forever.
question: How does knowing his covenant never fades change the way you trust him?

text: It shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings, that you shall eat neither fat nor blood.
reflection: The fat and the blood belonged to God alone. In the peace offering, the worshiper shared a meal with God, but God's portion always came first. God still invites you to his table.
question: What does it mean to you that God wants to sit down and eat with you?

text: All its fat he shall take away, like the fat is taken away from off of the peace offering; and the priest shall burn it on the altar for a pleasant aroma to Yahweh.
reflection: The sin offering was for sins you did not mean to commit. Even wrongs you were unaware of needed atonement. But this verse ends with freedom. He will be forgiven. God does not hold your failures against you when you come to him.
question: What have you been carrying that God is ready to forgive?

text: If anyone sins, and does any of the things which Yahweh has commanded not to be done, though he didn't know it, yet he is guilty, and shall bear his iniquity.
reflection: This verse is honest about something you may not want to hear. Not knowing you sinned does not make you innocent. But God is not saying this to crush you. He is saying it because he already made a way to lift your guilt.
question: What do you need to bring to God today?

text: Fire shall be kept burning on the altar continually; it shall not go out.
reflection: The fire on the altar was never allowed to die. It burned day and night, a picture of how faithful God is toward you. His love does not cool. His attention toward you does not wander. God invites you to keep your heart burning for him.
question: What has grown cold in your walk with God?
hook: A Fire That Never Goes Out

text: This is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which one shall offer to Yahweh.
reflection: The peace offering was voluntary. No one was forced to bring it. God wanted your thanksgiving to be free, not required. When you bring your praise to him willingly, it delights him more than any obligation ever could.
question: What has God done for you that deserves your thanks today?

text: You shall stay at the door of the Tent of Meeting day and night seven days, and keep Yahweh's command, that you don't die; for so I am commanded.
reflection: God told the priests to stay at the tent for seven days before they could serve. They had to wait and obey. God does the same with you. Before he sends you out, he prepares you. When God asks you to be still, that time is not wasted.
question: How is God preparing you right now?

text: Moses and Aaron went into the Tent of Meeting, and came out, and blessed the people; and Yahweh's glory appeared to all the people.
reflection: When God's fire fell, the people fell on their faces. The only response to his glory was worship. God still shows up in your life in ways that stop you in your tracks. His presence is just as real today.
question: When was the last time something God did left you in awe?

text: Then Moses said to Aaron, 'This is what Yahweh spoke of, saying, "I will be sanctified by those who come near me, and before all the people I will be glorified."'
reflection: Nadab and Abihu came to God on their own terms, and it cost them everything. God is not casual about how you approach him. But this is not meant to frighten you away. He invites you to come the right way.
question: Are you approaching God on his terms or yours?
hook: God Will Be Treated as Holy

text: For I am Yahweh who brought you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God. You shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.
reflection: God did not just tell you to be holy. He first reminded you who he is. I am your God. I brought you out. Your holiness flows from belonging to him, not from your own effort.
question: How does knowing you belong to God change the way you live?
hook: Be Holy Because I Am Holy

text: If she cannot afford a lamb, then she shall take two turtledoves, or two young pigeons: the one for a burnt offering, and the other for a sin offering.
reflection: If you could not afford a lamb, two small birds were enough. The price was adjusted, but the result was the same. She shall be clean. God meets you exactly where you are.
question: What has made you feel you cannot come to God as you are?

text: The leper in whom the plague is shall wear torn clothes, and the hair of his head shall hang loose. He shall cover his upper lip, and shall cry, 'Unclean! Unclean!'
reflection: The leper had to cry out his own shame. He lived alone, cut off from everyone. But God had not forgotten him. Even in your lowest moment, God still sees you. The same laws that declared you unclean also contained the path back to him.
question: What are you hiding from God that he already sees?
hook: The Weight of Being Set Apart

text: He shall sprinkle on him who is to be cleansed from the leprosy seven times, and shall pronounce him clean.
reflection: One bird died. The other was dipped in its blood and set free. The leper was sprinkled and declared clean. This is how God works with you. The cleansing you need is not something you earn. God provides it as a gift.
question: Where do you need God to declare you clean today?

text: Thus you shall separate the children of Israel from their uncleanness, so they will not die in their uncleanness when they defile my tabernacle that is among them.
reflection: God's tabernacle was right in the middle of the camp. His presence was that close. These laws were not given to push you away but to keep you near him safely. God always makes a way for you to stay in his presence.
question: What is standing between you and God today?

text: For on this day shall atonement be made for you, to cleanse you. You shall be clean from all your sins before Yahweh.
reflection: Not some of your sins. All of them. That was the promise of the Day of Atonement. One day each year, everything was dealt with. You did not carry it forward. God made the way and spoke the word. Clean.
question: What sins are you still carrying that God has already said he will take?

text: For the life of the flesh is in the blood. I have given it to you on the altar to make atonement for your souls; for it is the blood that makes atonement by reason of the life.
reflection: God says it plainly. I have given it to you. The blood on the altar was his idea and his gift. He designed it to cover your sins so you could stay near him. The cost was never yours to pay.
question: What would it mean for you to accept what God has already given?
hook: The Life Is in the Blood

text: You shall therefore keep my statutes and my ordinances, which if a man does, he shall live by them. I am Yahweh.
reflection: God does not just tell you what to avoid. He tells you where life is found. His statutes are the path to real living. God knows what is best for you and calls you to walk with him.
question: Where is he calling you to step closer to him today?

text: You shall not take vengeance, nor bear any grudge against the children of your people; but you shall love your neighbor as yourself. I am Yahweh.
reflection: God said, love your neighbor as yourself. That is not passive. It means seeking the good of the person right next to you. God ties this command to his own name. I am Yahweh. His love for you is the reason you can love others.
question: Who is God asking you to love well today?

text: You shall be holy to me, for I, Yahweh, am holy, and have set you apart from the peoples, that you should be mine.
reflection: God says you should be mine. Before any command about what to do, God claims you. He set you apart. He chose you, not because of what you have done, but because of who he is. Holiness starts with belonging, not performing.
question: How does knowing you belong to God change how you live today?

text: You shall sanctify him therefore, for he offers the bread of your God. He shall be holy to you, for I, Yahweh, who sanctify you, am holy.
reflection: The priest had to be holy because he served in God's presence. But God says, I Yahweh who sanctify you am holy. He does not just demand holiness. He provides it. You serve him not from your own strength but from his.
question: How is God making you holy right now?

text: You shall not profane my holy name, but I will be made holy among the children of Israel. I am Yahweh who makes you holy.
reflection: God says three times, I am Yahweh. His name is his guarantee. He brought you out of bondage. He makes you holy. He claims you as his own. Everything rests on who he is, not who you are. When you doubt your standing with God, remember his name.
question: How does his faithfulness steady you today?

text: 'Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day is a Sabbath of solemn rest, a holy convocation; you shall do no kind of work. It is a Sabbath to Yahweh in all your dwellings.'
reflection: God built rest into his plan for you. Six days of work, then stop. Not because there is nothing left, but because you are more than your output. The Sabbath reminds you that your life does not depend on your effort.
question: Where do you need to let God be enough?

text: 'Command the children of Israel that they bring to you pure olive oil beaten for the light, to cause a lamp to burn continually.'
reflection: God commanded that his lamp never go out. That same God keeps his light burning for you today. No darkness in your life is too deep for him to reach.
question: Where do you need to let God shine into a place you have been trying to handle alone?

text: You shall make the fiftieth year holy, and proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee to you.
reflection: God declared a year when every debt was cancelled and every person went free. That was a picture of what God wants to do in your life. He is the God who sets you free and brings you home.
question: What burden are you carrying that God is ready to lift from you today?

text: I will walk among you, and will be your God, and you shall be my people.
reflection: God did not say he would watch you from a distance. He said he would walk among you. He wants to be near you, not as a judge standing over you, but as a God who has chosen you.
question: Where in your life do you need to remember that God is walking right beside you?

text: 'All the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land or of the fruit of the trees, is Yahweh's. It is holy to Yahweh.'
reflection: God says the tithe is already his. It does not become his when you give it. It was his from the start. Giving back to him is agreeing with what has always been true.
question: What would change if you saw everything you own as a gift from God?

text: From twenty years old and upward, all who are able to go out to war in Israel, you and Aaron shall count them by their divisions.
reflection: God counted every person in Israel by name. Not one was overlooked. The same God sees you right now, wherever you are. He has never lost count of you.
question: What difference does it make for you to know that God knows you personally and has always known exactly where you are?

text: 'The children of Israel shall camp every man by his own standard, with the banners of their fathers' houses. They shall camp around the Tent of Meeting at a distance.'
reflection: God did not scatter Israel randomly in the wilderness. He placed himself at the center and arranged every tribe around his presence. That same God invites you to build your life around him today.
question: How would your daily choices look different if you arranged everything around the place where God dwells?

text: 'For all the firstborn are mine. On the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt I made holy to me all the firstborn in Israel.'
reflection: God said the firstborn belonged to him because he saved them. That same rescue now includes you. He did not simply notice you from a distance. He paid a price to make you his own.
question: How does knowing you belong to God because he saved you change the way you see yourself today?

text: 'When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all its furnishings, then after that, the sons of Kohath shall come to carry it, but they shall not touch the holy things, lest they die.'
reflection: The Kohathites carried the holiest things in Israel, yet could not touch them. God was showing you that coming near him means coming with reverence. He wants you close, but he wants you to know how holy he really is.
question: How does the way you approach God reflect how seriously you take his presence?
hook: Do Not Touch What Is Holy

text: ...they shall confess their sin which they have done; and he shall make restitution for his guilt in full, and add to it the fifth part of it, and give it to him against whom he has been guilty.
reflection: God did not ask for a quiet apology. He asked the person to confess, pay back what was taken, and add a fifth more. God wants you to be honest about your sin and make things right.
question: What would it look like for you to bring that kind of honesty to God today?

text: 'Yahweh bless you, and keep you.'
reflection: These are not just ancient words. God is speaking them over you right now. He wants to bless you and keep you. He is not distant or distracted. He is turned toward you with favor.
question: How does it change your day to hear God say he is blessing you and keeping you safe?

text: When Moses went into the Tent of Meeting to speak with Yahweh, he heard his voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat.
reflection: After all the offerings were brought, Moses walked into the Tent and heard God speak. God is not silent toward you. He still speaks to those who draw near.
question: When was the last time you came to God expecting to hear from him, and what might he want to say to you today?
hook: He Spoke From the Mercy Seat

text: 'For all the firstborn among the children of Israel are mine, both man and animal. On the day that I struck all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I sanctified them for myself.'
reflection: God did not wait for the firstborn to volunteer. He saved them and then said they were his. That is the same claim God makes on you. He rescued you, and now you belong to him.
question: How does knowing God sanctified you for himself change the way you think about your purpose today?

text: At the commandment of Yahweh, the children of Israel traveled, and at the commandment of Yahweh they encamped.
reflection: Israel did not decide for themselves when to stay or go. They watched the cloud and followed God's lead. He is asking you to do the same thing today.
question: What would it look like for you to stop making your own plans and simply wait for God to show you when to move?

text: Whenever the ark went forward, Moses said, 'Rise up, Yahweh, and let your enemies be scattered! Let those who hate you flee before you!'
reflection: When Israel set out, Moses asked God to go ahead of them. He did not try to face the road alone. God is still willing to go ahead of you.
question: What would change if you started every step of your day by asking God to go before you and clear the path?

text: Yahweh said to Moses, 'Has Yahweh's hand grown short? Now you will see whether my word will happen to you or not.'
reflection: God asked Moses a question that cuts through every doubt you carry. "Has my hand grown short?" The answer is no. Whatever you are facing right now, God's reach has not shrunk. His power has not faded.
question: What situation in your life have you been treating as too big for God, and what would it look like to trust that his hand has not grown short toward you?

text: Now the man Moses was very humble, more than all the men who were on the surface of the earth.
reflection: Moses was the most humble man on earth, and God himself came to his defense.
question: When someone criticizes you unfairly, do you rush to defend yourself, or do you trust God enough to let him speak for you?
hook: The Most Humble Man on Earth

text: Caleb stilled the people before Moses, and said, 'Let's go up at once and possess it; for we are well able to overcome it!'
reflection: Ten men looked at the obstacles and told you it was impossible. One man looked at God and said you were more than able. The difference was not courage. It was where you fix your eyes. God wants you to measure your problems against His strength, not yours.
question: What is the thing in your life that looks too big to face right now, and what would change if you measured it against God's power instead of your own?
hook: We Are Well Able to Overcome

text: 'Only don't rebel against Yahweh, neither fear the people of the land; for they are bread for us. Their defense is removed from over them, and Yahweh is with us. Don't fear them.'
reflection: God does not promise you a life without obstacles. He promises you His presence in the middle of them. When He says "do not fear," He is not pretending the danger is small. He is telling you that He is bigger.
question: What fear have you been letting stand between you and something God has promised you, and what would change if you truly believed he is right there with you in the middle of it?

text: ...so that you may remember and do all my commandments, and be holy to your God.
reflection: God knows how quickly you forget. That is not an insult. It is why He built reminders into your life. He gave Israel tassels on their garments so they would see His commands every time they looked down. He has given you reminders too.
question: What reminders has God placed in your life to keep his commands in front of you, and how often do you let the busyness of the day push them aside?

text: They fell on their faces, and said, 'God, the God of the spirits of all flesh, will one man sin, and will you be angry with all the congregation?'
reflection: When the people around you are heading toward destruction, God does not want you to stand back in judgment. He wants you on your face, begging Him for mercy on their behalf. That is what Moses and Aaron did, and God heard them.
question: When you see someone around you heading toward destruction, do you pull away in judgment or do you fall on your face and ask God for mercy on their behalf?

text: On the next day, Moses went into the Tent of the Testimony; and behold, Aaron's rod for the house of Levi had sprouted, budded, produced blossoms, and bore ripe almonds.
reflection: A dead stick blossomed overnight because God touched it. Buds, blossoms, and ripe fruit all appeared on a piece of wood that had no life in it. God does not need your situation to look promising before He can bring something beautiful out of it.
question: Where in your life has God brought something alive that you thought was completely dead, and have you stopped to thank him for the unexpected fruit he has placed in your hands?

text: Yahweh said to Aaron, 'You shall have no inheritance in their land, neither shall you have any portion among them. I am your portion and your inheritance among the children of Israel.'
reflection: God told Aaron he would receive no land and no inheritance like the other tribes. Instead, God said, "I am your portion." God Himself was the reward. That same offer stands for you today. God wants to be enough for you, more than any comfort or security this world provides.
question: If God took away every comfort you rely on and said, "I am your portion," would that be enough for you, and what does your honest answer reveal about your heart?

text: A man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer, and lay them up outside of the camp in a clean place.
reflection: God provided the ashes and the water. The person who needed cleansing did not have to create the remedy. God already prepared everything you need to be made clean. Your job is not to scrub yourself presentable. Your job is to come to Him and let Him do it.
question: When was the last time you tried to clean yourself up before going to God, instead of simply letting him do the cleansing that only he can do for you?
hook: The Ashes That Made You Clean

text: Yahweh said to Moses and Aaron, 'Because you didn't believe in me, to sanctify me in the eyes of the children of Israel, therefore you shall not bring this assembly into the land which I have given them.'
reflection: Moses was faithful in almost everything, but one moment of disobedience cost him the promised land. God does not have a category for "small sins." The way you handle the small things tells God what you truly believe about Him.
question: What area of your life have you treated as too small for God to care about, when God might be saying that how you handle it reveals what you truly believe about him?

text: Moses made a serpent of bronze, and set it on the pole. If a serpent had bitten any man, when he looked at the serpent of bronze, he lived.
reflection: God did not ask the bitten Israelites to do anything complicated. He just said look. One look at what God provided was enough to save them. That is still how God works. He does not ask you to earn your healing. He asks you to look to Him and trust.
question: When the pain of your sin is pressing in and you know you cannot fix it yourself, are you willing to simply look to God and trust that what he has provided is enough to heal you?
hook: One Look and You Will Live

text: Yahweh opened the mouth of the donkey, and she said to Balaam, 'What have I done to you, that you have struck me these three times?'
reflection: God once used a donkey to speak truth that a prophet refused to see.
question: What unexpected voice or circumstance in your life right now might God be using to get your attention, if you are willing to listen?
hook: God Used a Donkey to Speak

text: God is not a man, that he should lie, nor a son of man, that he should repent. Has he said, and he won't do it? Or has he spoken, and he won't make it good?
reflection: God is not a man. He does not change His mind, break His promises, or speak words He will not follow through on. Every promise He has made to you still stands.
question: When life makes you doubt whether God will come through, how does it change your fear to remember that God is not a man who lies, and that every word he speaks to you he will make good?

text: I see him, but not now. I see him, but not near. A star will come out of Jacob. A scepter will rise out of Israel.
reflection: Long before you needed a Savior, God was already sending one. A star was rising out of Jacob, a King was already on His way. God does not react to your crisis. He planned for it before you were born.
question: When you look at the moments in your life where God provided something you did not yet know you needed, how does that change the way you trust His plan for your future?

text: 'Phinehas, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, has turned my wrath away from the children of Israel, in that he was jealous with my jealousy among them.'
reflection: One man stood up when everyone else sat down. Phinehas honored God when the crowd around him did nothing. God noticed, and that one act of faithfulness turned away His anger from an entire nation. God sees when you stand for what is right, even alone.
question: Where in your life have you been sitting quietly when God is asking you to stand up for what is right, and what would it look like for you to honor Him in that place today?

text: For Yahweh had said of them, 'They shall surely die in the wilderness.' There was not a man left of them, except Caleb the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun.
reflection: Out of an entire generation, only two men made it to the promised land. Caleb and Joshua believed God when everyone else gave up. The rest died in the wilderness because doubt and complaining replaced their trust. God is faithful to His promises, but He asks you to hold onto your faith all the way through.
question: When you look at the promises God has spoken over your life, are you walking in the kind of faith that carries you all the way through, or have you let doubt and complaining slowly pull you off course?

text: ...who may go out before them, and who may come in before them, and who may lead them out, and who may bring them in, that the congregation of Yahweh may not be as sheep which have no shepherd.
reflection: Moses prayed for a shepherd so God's people would not wander without direction. God answered that prayer and appointed Joshua. God has never left His people without guidance. Even in seasons when you cannot see the next step, God is already preparing someone or something to lead you forward.
question: When you feel like you are walking through a season without clear direction, do you trust that God has already prepared the next guide for your life, and are you asking Him to show you who or what that is?

text: 'Command the children of Israel, and tell them, "My offering, my food for my offerings made by fire, of a pleasant aroma to me, you shall observe to offer to me in their due season."'
reflection: God asked His people to come to Him every single day with an offering. Not because He was hungry, but because He wanted them close. God still wants that from you. He is not waiting for a crisis to hear from you. He is asking you to show up daily, just because He loves your presence.
question: What would it look like for you to build a daily rhythm of coming to God, not just when you need something, but because He has asked you to bring yourself to Him each day?

text: 'On the tenth day of this seventh month you shall have a holy convocation. You shall afflict your souls. You shall do no kind of work.'
reflection: God set aside one day each year where everything stopped. No work, no distraction, just you and God and the honest weight of your own heart. He did not design that day to punish you. He designed it to free you. Sometimes the bravest thing you can do is stop running and let God see everything.
question: When was the last time you stopped everything you were doing and let yourself sit honestly before God with the full weight of what you have done wrong, with nothing to hide behind?
hook: The Day You Stop and Face God

text: 'When a man vows a vow to Yahweh, or swears an oath to bind his soul with a bond, he shall not break his word. He shall do according to all that proceeds out of his mouth.'
reflection: God takes your words seriously even when you forget them. When you make a promise to Him or to someone else, He expects you to follow through. Your integrity matters to God because His own faithfulness never wavers.
question: What promise have you made that you have not yet kept?

text: ...everything that may withstand the fire, you shall make to go through the fire, and it shall be clean.
reflection: God sent everything through fire or water before it could be used in His presence. He does the same with you. The hard seasons in your life are not random. They are how God makes you ready for what comes next.
question: What trial might God be using to purify you right now?

text: But if you will not do so, behold, you have sinned against Yahweh; and be sure your sin will find you out.
reflection: You cannot hide what you have done from God. He does not say your sin might find you out. He says be sure it will. That is not a threat to scare you. It is God loving you enough to warn you before it is too late.
question: What are you hoping will stay hidden?
hook: Your Sin Will Find You Out

text: But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then those whom you let remain of them will be as pricks in your eyes, and as thorns in your sides.
reflection: God warned Israel that the things they refused to remove would become thorns that never stopped hurting. You have things like that in your life too. The sin you keep making room for will keep wounding you.
question: What have you been tolerating that God has told you to drive out?

text: 'Command the children of Israel, and tell them, "When you come into the land of Canaan, this is the land that shall fall to you for an inheritance."'
reflection: God measured the land before Israel ever set foot in it. Every boundary was already drawn. What He promises you, He prepares in advance. You do not have to worry about whether God will come through.
question: What promise from God are you waiting on that He has already prepared for you?

text: 'You shall not defile the land which you inhabit, in the middle of which I dwell; for I, Yahweh, dwell in the middle of the children of Israel.'
reflection: God does not ask you to be holy from a distance. He told Israel not to defile the land because He lived right there with them. His presence is not a reward for getting it right. It is the reason you want to.
question: Where in your life is God asking you to honor His presence?
hook: God Lives Right Here With You

text: These are the commandments and the ordinances which Yahweh commanded by Moses to the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.
reflection: Every command God gave Israel was building toward something. He was not giving rules to burden them. He was preparing them for what was ahead. God does the same for you.
question: What instruction from God have you been resisting that might be preparing you for something you cannot yet see?

text: Behold, Yahweh your God has set the land before you. Go up, take possession, as Yahweh the God of your fathers has spoken to you. Don't be afraid, neither be dismayed.
reflection: When God opens a door for you and tells you not to be afraid, the only thing between you and the blessing is your willingness to walk forward.
question: What has God set before you that you have been too afraid to take hold of?

text: For Yahweh your God has blessed you in all the work of your hand. He has known your walking through this great wilderness. These forty years, Yahweh your God has been with you. You have lacked nothing.
reflection: God walked with Israel for forty years through a wilderness, and they lacked nothing. He knows every step you have taken too. He has seen your hard seasons and been with you through all of them.
question: What season of your life proved God was providing for you even when you could not see it?

text: 'You shall not fear them; for Yahweh your God himself fights for you.'
reflection: God fights for you. You do not have to carry every battle on your own. The same God who brought you this far goes ahead of you into whatever comes next.
question: What battle are you carrying that God is asking you to hand over to Him?

text: But from there you shall seek Yahweh your God, and you shall find him when you search after him with all your heart and with all your soul.
reflection: You do not have to stay where you are. God promises that when you seek Him with all your heart, you will find Him. No matter how far you have drifted, He is not hiding. He is waiting for you.
question: What have you been searching for in other places that only God can give you?
hook: Seek Him and You Will Find Him

text: 'Oh that there were such a heart in them, that they would fear me and keep all my commandments always, that it might be well with them and with their children forever!'
reflection: God did not ask His people for perfection. He wished they had a heart that truly wanted to follow Him. That is what God wants from you. Not a flawless record, but a willing heart.
question: What part of your heart have you been holding back from God?

text: You shall love Yahweh your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your might.
reflection: This is the most important command in all of Scripture. God asks you to love Him with everything you are. Not part of your heart, not just when it is easy, but all of it. He gave you everything and asks for everything back.
question: How fully are you giving your heart to God today?

text: Know therefore that Yahweh your God himself is God, the faithful God, who keeps covenant and loving kindness to a thousand generations with those who love him and keep his commandments.
reflection: God calls Himself the faithful God. His love for you does not depend on your performance. He keeps His promises to a thousand generations. When everything around you changes, God does not. You can trust Him because He has never broken a single promise.
question: How has God's faithfulness carried you when everything else fell apart?

text: He humbled you, and allowed you to be hungry, and fed you with manna, which you didn't know, that he might teach you that man does not live by bread alone, but man lives by every word that proceeds out of Yahweh's mouth.
reflection: God let you go hungry so He could teach you something. Every need you feel is an invitation to depend on Him. You were not made to survive on what the world provides. You were made to live on every word from God's mouth.
question: What has God been teaching you through a season of need?

text: Know therefore that Yahweh your God doesn't give you this good land to possess for your righteousness, for you are a stiff-necked people.
reflection: God does not owe you anything. Every blessing in your life is a gift, not a payment for something you earned. He gave Israel the land not because they were righteous, but because He is faithful. The same is true for you.
question: What blessing have you been treating as something you deserved?

text: Now, Israel, what does Yahweh your God require of you, but to fear Yahweh your God, to walk in all his ways, and to love him, and to serve Yahweh your God with all your heart?
reflection: God is not asking you for something you cannot understand. He tells you exactly what He wants. Fear Him, walk in His ways, love Him, serve Him with your whole heart. That is the whole of it. And it is all for your good.
question: What part of this list feels hardest for you today?

text: Therefore you shall lay up these my words in your heart and in your soul. You shall bind them as a sign on your hand, and they shall be for frontlets between your eyes.
reflection: You have access to God's words, but do they actually live inside you? God wants them so close to your heart that they shape what you do before you even think about it.
question: What would be different about your week if God's word was the first thing on your mind every morning?

text: But to the place which Yahweh your God shall choose out of all your tribes, to put his name there, you shall seek his habitation, and there you shall come.
reflection: God picked the place where He would meet His people. You do not get to decide how to approach Him. He already made the way, and when you come on His terms, joy follows.
question: Where are you trying to reach God your own way instead of the way He prepared for you?

text: You shall walk after Yahweh your God, fear him, keep his commandments, and obey his voice. You shall serve him, and cling to him.
reflection: God tells you to cling to Him. Not just believe in Him, but hold onto Him with everything you have. The tighter you hold to God, the harder it is for anything to pull you away.
question: What voice in your life is pulling you away from the God who loves you?

text: For you are a holy people to Yahweh your God, and Yahweh has chosen you to be a people for his own possession, above all peoples who are on the face of the earth.
reflection: God chose you. Not because you earned it and not because you were better than anyone else.
question: He just looked at you and said, "Mine." How does knowing you are chosen by God change the way you see yourself today?

text: You shall surely give, and your heart shall not be grieved when you give to him; because for this thing Yahweh your God will bless you in all your work, and in all that you put your hand to.
reflection: God promises to bless the hand that gives freely. He does not want your leftovers or your guilt money. He wants your open hand and your willing heart.
question: When you give, does your heart follow your hand, or are you holding back while pretending to let go?

text: They shall not appear before Yahweh empty. Every man shall give as he is able, according to Yahweh your God's blessing which he has given you.
reflection: God does not measure your giving by the number. He measures it by what He already gave you first. Everything in your hands started in His.
question: What has God given you this week that you have not stopped to thank Him for?

text: It shall be with him, and he shall read from it all the days of his life, that he may learn to fear Yahweh his God, to keep all the words of this law and these statutes, to do them.
reflection: God commanded the king of Israel to read His Word every single day for the rest of his life. Not because the king was weak, but because even the most powerful person alive needs God's voice to stay on course. If that was true for a king, it is even more true for you.
question: How often do you open God's Word, and what would change in your life if you read it every day this week?

text: Yahweh your God will raise up to you a prophet from among you, of your brothers, like me. You shall listen to him.
reflection: God promised a prophet who would speak His words directly to you. Jesus is that prophet.
question: When you read what Jesus said, are you treating His words as God speaking to you personally, or just as good advice?
hook: God Sent a Voice for You

text: Your eyes shall not pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
reflection: God takes your words seriously enough to hold you accountable for every one of them. Your mouth can protect someone or destroy them.
question: What words have you spoken about someone that you would not say to their face?

text: For Yahweh your God is he who goes with you, to fight for you against your enemies, to save you.
reflection: God does not send you into your battles alone. He goes with you. He fights for you. The enemy in front of you is not bigger than the God beside you.
question: What fight in your life have you been carrying alone that God is asking you to hand over to Him?

text: His body shall not remain all night on the tree, but you shall surely bury him the same day; for he who is hanged is accursed of God.
reflection: The one who hangs on the tree carries the curse. Jesus hung there for you. He took the weight of everything you have ever done wrong so that you would never have to carry it yourself.
question: What guilt are you still carrying that Jesus already took to the cross?

text: When you build a new house, then you shall make a railing around your roof, so that you don't bring blood on your house if anyone falls from there.
reflection: God told you to put a railing on your roof. Not because He likes rules, but because He cares about the person who might walk across it.
question: How aware are you of the ways your choices affect the safety and wellbeing of the people around you?

text: When you vow a vow to Yahweh your God, you shall not be slack to pay it; for Yahweh your God will surely require it of you, and it would be sin in you.
reflection: God does not forget the promises you make to Him, even when you do. When you told Him you would change, give, serve, or follow through, He wrote it down.
question: What promise have you made to God that you have not kept?

text: When you reap your harvest in your field, and have forgotten a sheaf in the field, you shall not go again to get it. It shall be for the foreigner, for the fatherless, and for the widow.
reflection: God provides for the helpless through your open hands. When you forget something, overlook something, or have more than you need, God turns it into provision for someone who has nothing.
question: What are you holding tightly that God is asking you to leave for someone else?

text: You shall not muzzle the ox when he treads out the grain.
reflection: God says the ox that works your field deserves to eat from it. That one small law tells you everything about God. He pays attention to how you treat those who serve you.
question: Who in your life has been working faithfully that you have not honored?

text: You shall rejoice in all the good which Yahweh your God has given to you, and to your house, you, and the Levite, and the foreigner who is among you.
reflection: God commands you to rejoice. Not just feel it quietly, but stop and celebrate everything He has given you. Your blessings are not accidents. They are gifts from a God who brought you here on purpose.
question: Have you stopped lately to thank God for what He has already placed in your life?

text: You shall therefore obey Yahweh your God's voice, and do his commandments and his statutes, which I command you today.
reflection: God says you are His. That one fact changes everything. If God has called you His own, then what He says to you is not optional. His voice is the most important thing you will ever hear.
question: Are you listening to God right now, or have you been too busy to stop?

text: All these blessings will come upon you, and overtake you, if you listen to Yahweh your God's voice.
reflection: God's blessings are not hiding from you. They are running after you. When you listen to His voice, the good things He has stored up for you chase you down.
question: What blessing might God be trying to give you that you keep running from?
