text: The secret things belong to Yahweh our God; but the things that are revealed belong to us and to our children forever, that we may do all the words of this law.
reflection: God holds some things back from you, and that is His right. You were never meant to understand everything. But what He has revealed, His Word, His promises, His direction for your life, that belongs to you and your children forever.
question: What truth has God already shown you that you have not yet acted on?

text: I call heaven and earth to witness against you today that I have set before you life and death, the blessing and the curse. Therefore choose life, that you may live, you and your descendants.
reflection: God puts life and death in front of you and says choose life. He is not hiding the answer. He wants you to live. God is not neutral about your future. He is pulling for you.
question: What choice has God placed in front of you that you already know the answer to?

text: Be strong and courageous. Don't be afraid or scared of them; for Yahweh your God himself goes with you. He will not fail you nor forsake you.
reflection: God tells you to be strong and courageous, but not because you have to face things on your own. He goes with you. Right now, whatever makes you afraid, God is already there.
question: Where in your life do you need to hear God say He will not leave you?

text: The Rock! His work is perfect, for all his ways are just. A God of faithfulness who does no wrong, just and right is he.
reflection: God is called the Rock because nothing about Him shifts or crumbles. His work is perfect and His ways are just. When your life feels unstable, you have a God whose faithfulness never moves.
question: What part of your life needs to be built on the Rock instead of something that keeps shifting?

text: The eternal God is your dwelling place. Underneath are the everlasting arms. He thrust out the enemy from before you, and said, 'Destroy!'
reflection: God is not just beside you. He is underneath you. When you feel like you are falling, His everlasting arms are already there. You do not have to hold yourself up. God holds you.
question: What weight are you carrying right now that you need to let God's arms hold for you?

text: Since then, there has not arisen a prophet in Israel like Moses, whom Yahweh knew face to face.
reflection: God knew Moses face to face. That was not just about greatness. It was about closeness. God wants to know you that way too. He does not keep His distance from you. He leans toward you.
question: How would your daily life change if you believed God wanted to be that close to you?

Therefore take diligent heed to yourselves to love Yahweh your God.

If it seems evil to you to serve Yahweh, choose today whom you will serve; whether the gods which your fathers served that were beyond the River, or the gods of the Amorites, in whose land you dwell; but as for me and my house, we will serve Yahweh.
Yahweh was with Judah, and he drove out the inhabitants of the hill country; for he could not drive out the inhabitants of the valley, because they had chariots of iron.
After all that generation were gathered to their fathers, another generation arose after them who didn't know Yahweh, nor the work which he had done for Israel.

Deborah said to Barak, 'Go! For this is the day in which Yahweh has delivered Sisera into your hand. Hasn't Yahweh gone out before you?'
'So let all your enemies perish, Yahweh, but let those who love him be as the sun when it rises in its strength.'
Yahweh looked at him, and said, 'Go in this your might, and save Israel from the hand of Midian. Haven't I sent you?'
Yahweh said to Gideon, 'The people who are with you are too many for me to give the Midianites into their hand, lest Israel brag against me, saying, "My own hand has saved me."'
Gideon said to them, 'I will not rule over you, neither shall my son rule over you. Yahweh shall rule over you.'
Thus God repaid the wickedness of Abimelech, which he did to his father in killing his seventy brothers.
They put away the foreign gods from among them and served Yahweh; and his soul was grieved for the misery of Israel.
Then Yahweh's Spirit came on Jephthah, and he passed over Gilead and Manasseh.
When I saw that you didn't save me, I put my life in my hands, and passed over against the children of Ammon, and Yahweh delivered them into my hand.
For, behold, you shall conceive and bear a son. No razor shall come on his head, for the child shall be a Nazirite to God from the womb.
But his father and his mother didn't know that it was of Yahweh; for he sought an occasion against the Philistines.
He was very thirsty, and called on Yahweh and said, 'You have given this great deliverance by the hand of your servant; now shall I die of thirst?'
Samson called to Yahweh, and said, 'Lord Yahweh, remember me, please, and strengthen me, please, only this once, O God, that I may be at once avenged of the Philistines.'
In those days there was no king in Israel. Everyone did what was right in his own eyes.
So they set up for themselves Micah's engraved image which he made, all the time that God's house was in Shiloh.
All who saw it said, 'Such a deed has not been done or seen from the day that the children of Israel came up out of the land of Egypt to this day!'

Phinehas, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron, stood before it in those days, saying, 'Shall I yet again go out to battle against the children of Benjamin my brother, or shall I cease?' Yahweh said, 'Go up; for tomorrow I will deliver him into your hand.'
In those days there was no king in Israel. Everyone did that which was right in his own eyes.
Ruth said, 'Don't urge me to leave you, or to turn back from following you; for where you go, I will go; and where you stay, I will stay. Your people will be my people, and your God, my God.'
reflection: God is not asking you for a halfhearted yes. He wants all of you, the way Ruth gave all of herself. She did not hold back one piece of her old life just in case things did not work out.
question: When you said yes to God, did you mean it with everything, or are you still keeping one foot in the life you used to live?
hook: What would it take for you to leave everything behind and follow God into the unknown?

text: May Yahweh repay your work, and a full reward be given to you from Yahweh, the God of Israel, under whose wings you have come to take refuge.
reflection: You may feel like no one has noticed what it cost you to follow God. The sacrifices you made in the quiet, the kindness you gave when no one was watching. But God has seen every bit of it. He does not miss a thing.
question: What sacrifice have you made for God that you thought went unnoticed?
hook: What if every sacrifice you have made for God has already been noticed by him?

text: He said, 'Who are you?' She answered, 'I am Ruth your servant. Spread your skirt over your servant, for you are a near kinsman.'
reflection: You do not have to hide the broken parts of your life from God. He already sees them, and he is not turning away. He is a redeemer who covers what you cannot cover yourself.
question: What part of your life have you been too afraid to bring to God, and what would it feel like to let him spread his covering over it?
hook: Have you ever been brave enough to ask God to cover the part of your life you cannot fix on your own?

text: The women said to Naomi, 'Blessed be Yahweh, who has not left you today without a near kinsman. Let his name be famous in Israel.'
reflection: You have had a season when everything felt gone. You came home empty and wondered if God noticed. But God had not left you without hope. He was holding things together for you in the background the whole time.
question: When was the last time you looked back and realized God had been carrying you all along?
hook: When you look back on the hardest season of your life, will you be able to say that God never left you without someone to hold you up?

text: For this child I prayed, and Yahweh has given me my petition which I asked of him.
There is no one holy like Yahweh, for there is no one besides you, nor is there any rock like our God.
Therefore Eli said to Samuel, 'Go, lie down. If he calls you, you shall say, "Speak, Yahweh; for your servant hears."'
She named the child Ichabod, saying, 'The glory has departed from Israel', because the ark of God was taken.
When they arose early on the next morning, behold, Dagon had fallen on his face to the ground before Yahweh's ark.
The men of Beth Shemesh said, 'Who is able to stand before Yahweh, this holy God?'
Then Samuel took a stone and set it between Mizpah and Shen, and called its name Ebenezer, saying, 'Yahweh helped us until now.'
Yahweh said to Samuel, 'Listen to the voice of the people in all that they tell you; for they have not rejected you, but they have rejected me, that I should not be king over them.'
When Samuel saw Saul, Yahweh said to him, 'Behold, the man of whom I spoke to you. He will govern my people.'
Then Yahweh's Spirit will come mightily on you, and you will prophesy with them, and will be turned into another man.
Saul said, 'Today no man shall be put to death; for Yahweh has worked salvation in Israel today.'
Only fear Yahweh, and serve him in truth with all your heart; for consider what great things he has done for you.
But now your kingdom will not continue. Yahweh has sought for himself a man after his own heart, and Yahweh has appointed him to be prince over his people.

Jonathan said to the young man who bore his armor, 'Come! Let's go over to the garrison of these uncircumcised. It may be that Yahweh will work for us; for there is no restraint on Yahweh to save by many or by few.'
Samuel said, 'Has Yahweh as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying Yahweh's voice? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to listen than the fat of rams.'

But Yahweh said to Samuel, 'Don't look on his face, or on the height of his stature, because I have rejected him; for I don't see as man sees. For man looks at the outward appearance, but Yahweh looks at the heart.'

Then David said to the Philistine, 'You come to me with a sword, with a spear, and with a javelin; but I come to you in the name of Yahweh of Armies, the God of the armies of Israel, whom you have defied.'
David behaved himself wisely in all his ways; and Yahweh was with him.
So Michal let David down through the window. He went away, fled, and escaped.

Jonathan, Saul's son, arose and went to David into the woods, and strengthened his hand in God.

David said to Abigail, 'Blessed is Yahweh, the God of Israel, who sent you today to meet me!'
Yahweh will render to every man his righteousness and his faithfulness; because Yahweh delivered you into my hand today, and I wouldn't stretch out my hand against Yahweh's anointed.

When Saul inquired of Yahweh, Yahweh didn't answer him, not by dreams, nor by Urim, nor by prophets.
Then Achish called David, and said to him, 'As Yahweh lives, you have been upright, and your going out and your coming in with me in the army is good in my sight.'

David was greatly distressed, for the people spoke of stoning him, because the souls of all the people were grieved, every man for his sons and for his daughters; but David strengthened himself in Yahweh his God.
Then Saul said to his armor bearer, 'Draw your sword, and thrust me through with it, lest these uncircumcised come and thrust me through, and abuse me.'
I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan. You have been very pleasant to me. Your love to me was wonderful, surpassing the love of women.

David grew greater and greater, for Yahweh, the God of Armies, was with him.
David danced before Yahweh with all his might; and David was clothed in a linen ephod.
Your house and your kingdom will be made sure forever before you. Your throne will be established forever.
Then David put garrisons in Syria of Damascus; and the Syrians became servants to David and brought tribute. Yahweh gave victory to David wherever he went.

David said to him, 'Don't be afraid, for I will surely show you kindness for Jonathan your father's sake, and will restore to you all the land of Saul your father. You shall eat bread at my table continually.'
Be courageous, and let's be strong for our people and for the cities of our God. May Yahweh do what seems good to him.
When the mourning was past, David sent and took her home to his house, and she became his wife and bore him a son. But the thing that David had done displeased Yahweh.
Nathan said to David, 'You are the man!'
Absalom spoke to Amnon neither good nor bad; for Absalom hated Amnon, because he had forced his sister Tamar.
For we must die, and are like water spilled on the ground, which can't be gathered up again. God doesn't take away life, but devises means, so that he who is banished is not an outcast from him.
But if he says, 'I have no delight in you,' behold, here I am. Let him do to me as seems good to him.
It may be that Yahweh will look on the wrong done to me, and that Yahweh will repay me good for the cursing of me today.
Absalom and all the men of Israel said, 'The counsel of Hushai the Archite is better than the counsel of Ahithophel.' For Yahweh had ordained to defeat the good counsel of Ahithophel.
The king was much moved, and went up to the room over the gate and wept. As he went, he said, 'My son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! I wish I had died instead of you, O Absalom, my son, my son!'

text: To him who is ready to faint, kindness should be shown from his friend, even to him who forsakes the fear of the Almighty.

text: What is man, that you should magnify him, that you should set your mind on him?

text: Behold, God will not cast away a blameless man, neither will he uphold the evildoers.

question: What part of your life feels cast aside, and can you trust that God is still holding it?

text: There is no umpire between us, that might lay his hand on us both.
reflection: You cannot stand before God on your own. But you do not have to. God himself provided the one who bridges the gap between you and him.
question: How does knowing that Jesus stands between you and God change the way you pray today?

text: You have granted me life and loving kindness. Your visitation has preserved my spirit.
reflection: Even in the middle of your hardest season, God is still the one keeping you alive. Every breath you take is a gift from the God who loves you.
question: What would it look like to thank him today, not for what he has fixed, but for the fact that he has kept you?

text: Can you fathom the mystery of God? Or can you probe the limits of the Almighty?
reflection: God is bigger than anything your mind can contain. You will never fully understand him, and that is actually the safest place to be. A God you could figure out would be too small to carry you.

text: With God is wisdom and might. He has counsel and understanding.
reflection: You do not have to carry the weight of figuring everything out. God holds all the wisdom and all the power. He is not confused by what confuses you, and his plan is already in motion.

text: If a man dies, will he live again? All the days of my warfare I will wait, until my release comes.

text: Are the consolations of God too small for you, even the word that is gentle toward you?

question: What would change in you today if you believed God's comfort is bigger than your pain?

text: Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven. He who vouches for me is on high.

text: Yet the righteous shall hold on to his way. He who has clean hands shall grow stronger and stronger.

text: You who tear yourself in your anger, shall the earth be forsaken for you? Or shall the rock be removed out of its place?

text: But as for me, I know that my Redeemer lives. In the end, he will stand upon the earth.

text: Acquaint yourself with him now, and be at peace. By it, good shall come to you.

text: But he knows the way that I take. When he has tried me, I will come out like gold.

reflection: God is not impressed by how much you know. He is looking at whether your life bends toward him with reverence and away from what would harm you.
question: Where is God inviting you to choose wisdom over cleverness today?

text: I was a father to the needy. I researched the cause of him whom I didn't know.
reflection: God gave you what you have so you could be his hands to someone who has less.
question: Who around you needs something you are able to give, and what is stopping you from stepping toward them today?

text: I cry to you, and you do not answer me. I stand up, and you gaze at me.
reflection: You have prayed and heard nothing back. God has not forgotten you. His silence is not absence.
question: What would it mean for you to trust that God is working even in the quiet?

text: Doesn't he see my ways, and count all my steps?
reflection: God sees every step you take, even the ones no one else notices. That is not something to fear. It means you are never invisible to the One who loves you most.
question: How does knowing God sees everything change the way you live today?

text: But there is a spirit in man, and the breath of the Almighty gives them understanding.
reflection: God does not require you to be the smartest person in the room before he speaks to you. His Spirit gives understanding to anyone willing to listen.
question: Where has God been trying to teach you something you have been too busy to hear?

text: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
reflection: Every breath you take is proof that God is still sustaining you. You are alive because he chose to give you life.
question: What would change in your day if you treated every breath as a gift from the God who made you?
hook: The Breath That Gives You Life

text: For his eyes are on the ways of a man. He sees all his steps.
reflection: God is watching your life right now, not to catch you failing, but because he cares about every step you take. Nothing you carry today is invisible to him.
question: How does knowing God sees your whole story change the way the way you feel about where you are right now?

text: But no one says, 'Where is God my Maker, who gives songs in the night?'
reflection: When pain keeps you up at night, God is not far from you. He is your Maker, and he gives songs even in your darkness.
question: What would it mean for you to ask God for a song in the middle of what you are facing right now?
hook: God gives songs in your darkest night.

text: Behold, God is exalted in his power. Who is a teacher like him?
reflection: God is not just powerful. He is the greatest teacher you will ever have. Every hard season and every good one is a lesson from the one who knows you perfectly.
question: What is God teaching you right now that no one else could show you?
hook: No one teaches like God does.

text: We can't reach the Almighty. He is exalted in power. In justice and great righteousness, he will not oppress.
reflection: You will never fully understand God, and that is a gift. His power is too great for you to grasp, but it is always joined with justice. He will never use his strength to crush you.
question: How does knowing God's power is always paired with justice change the way you trust him?
hook: God's power is always joined with justice.

text: 'Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth? Declare, if you have understanding.'
reflection: God laid the foundations of the earth before you drew your first breath. He was building with purpose. The same hands that set the world in place are holding your life together today.
question: What part of your life do you need to trust to the God who built the earth itself?
hook: God built the earth before you existed.

text: Is it by your wisdom that the hawk soars, and stretches her wings toward the south?
reflection: The hawk does not fly by your command. The eagle does not soar because you gave it permission. God placed the desire to fly inside every wing. The same God who designed the hawk also designed your life.
question: What has God placed inside you that only he could have put there?
hook: God designed the hawk to fly.

text: 'Behold, I am of small account. What shall I answer you? I lay my hand on my mouth.'
reflection: There comes a moment when you run out of arguments with God. Not because he silenced you, but because you finally heard him. He is not trying to win a debate. He wants you to stop fighting long enough to see that his hands are safe.
question: What would change if you let him hold you?
hook: When you finally stop talking and listen.

text: Who has first given to me, that I should repay him? Everything under heaven is mine.
reflection: God does not love you because you earned it. He does not bless you because you gave him something first. Everything under heaven already belongs to him. You cannot make God owe you anything.
question: What would change if you stopped trying to earn what God has already freely given you?
hook: You cannot put God in your debt.

text: I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear, but now my eye sees you.
reflection: There is a difference between knowing about God and actually seeing him for yourself. You can hear sermons, read books, and talk about faith your whole life without ever truly meeting him face to face.
question: What would it take for you to move from hearing about God to really seeing him today?
hook: The day you stop hearing about God and finally see him.

Make vows to Yahweh your God, and fulfill them! Let all of his neighbors bring presents to him who is to be feared.
I will remember Yah's deeds; for I will remember your wonders of old.
We will not hide them from their children, telling to the generation to come the praises of Yahweh, his strength, and his wondrous deeds that he has done.

Let them praise Yahweh's name, for his name alone is exalted. His glory is above the earth and the heavens.

The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of knowledge, but the foolish despise wisdom and instruction.
reflection: Wisdom does not start with how much you know. It starts with how you stand before God. When you approach Him with reverence, everything begins to make sense. God is not hiding knowledge from you. He is the door to it.
question: What area of your life needs wisdom that only comes from putting God first?

text: For Yahweh gives wisdom. Out of his mouth comes knowledge and understanding.
reflection: You do not have to figure out life on your own. God gives wisdom right out of His own mouth and offers it to you. The knowledge you need most comes from God.
question: What decision in front of you right now needs God's wisdom instead of just your own thinking?

text: Trust in Yahweh with all your heart, and don't lean on your own understanding.
reflection: God is not asking you for part of your trust. He wants all of it. Every doubt you carry, every fear that keeps you awake, God invites you to release into His hands.
question: What are you holding onto today that you have not fully trusted God with?

text: Keep your heart with all diligence, for out of it is the wellspring of life.
reflection: Your heart is not just where you feel things. It is where every word, every motive, and every direction for your life begins. God tells you to guard it because He knows what comes out of it shapes everything.
question: What have you been letting into your heart that God is asking you to guard against?

text: For the ways of man are before Yahweh's eyes. He examines all his paths.
reflection: God sees every step you take, not to catch you but to guide you. Nothing in your life is invisible to Him, and nothing is beyond His care.
question: How does knowing that God sees every part of your life change the way you walk through today?

text: For the commandment is a lamp, and the law is light. Reproofs of instruction are the way of life,
reflection: God gave you His Word not to weigh you down but to light your way. Every command, every correction, every instruction is God showing you where to step next.
question: Where in your life have you set down God's lamp instead of carrying it with you?
hook: A Light That Never Goes Out

text: Keep my commandments and live! Guard my teaching as the apple of your eye.
reflection: God does not say keep His commandments and survive. He says keep them and live. His Word is not a burden you carry. It is the very thing that gives your life fullness and direction.
question: What part of God's teaching do you need to guard more carefully today?

text: For whoever finds me, finds life, and will obtain favor from Yahweh.
reflection: Wisdom is not a thing you earn. It is a person you find. And when you find the God who made you, you find life itself.
question: What would change in your day if you truly believed that finding God is the same as finding life?

text: The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of wisdom. The knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
reflection: Every good thing starts here, with you standing in awe of who God is. Knowing God is not something you collect like facts. It is understanding that reaches inside you and changes everything.
question: What would it look like for you to let the fear of God be the starting place for every decision you make?
hook: The Fear That Sets You Free

text: In the multitude of words there is no lack of disobedience, but he who restrains his lips does wisely.
reflection: It is easy to let your mouth run ahead of your heart. But God tells you the wise person learns when to stop talking. When you hold your tongue, you are not losing your voice. You are finding your wisdom.
question: Where in your life do you need to speak less and listen to God more?

text: When pride comes, then comes shame, but with humility comes wisdom.
reflection: Pride will bring you low, but humility will lift you higher than you could climb on your own. When you come to God with an open heart, He fills you with wisdom no amount of striving could produce.
question: What areas of your life would change if you let go of pride and let God lead?

text: Anxiety in a man's heart weighs it down, but a kind word makes it glad.
reflection: You know what it feels like when worry sits on your heart. It slows you down and makes everything harder. But God says a kind word breaks through all of it. When He speaks to you, His words do not just encourage you. They lift you.
question: How has God spoken kindness into your heaviest moment?
hook: A Word That Lifts You Up

text: One who walks with wise men grows wise, but a companion of fools suffers harm.
reflection: God made you to grow, and the people you walk with determine the direction. When you surround yourself with people who love God, His wisdom rubs off on you. When you walk with fools, the damage follows.
question: Who are you walking with, and is that path leading you closer to God or further away?

text: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.
reflection: You can convince yourself a path is safe when it leads to ruin. Your own judgment cannot keep you from danger. God sees the end of every road you walk, and He warns you because He loves you.
question: What path are you on that feels right but has not been tested by God's Word?

text: A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
reflection: Your words carry more power than you realize. A gentle response calms a storm, and a harsh one starts a fire. God is asking you to let His gentleness shape how you speak.
question: Where does God want you to offer a gentle word to someone who needs it today?

text: Commit your deeds to Yahweh, and your plans shall succeed.
reflection: You were never meant to carry the weight of your plans alone. God wants you to bring every dream and every decision to Him first. When you commit your way to God, He makes room for you to succeed.
question: What plan are you holding onto that you need to hand over to God?

text: A friend loves at all times; and a brother is born for adversity.
reflection: God designed you for relationships that hold through hard seasons. A real friend does not disappear when life gets difficult. God Himself is that kind of friend to you.
question: Who has God placed in your life that needs you to show up for them right now?

text: Yahweh's name is a strong tower; the righteous run to it, and are safe.
reflection: God gave you His name as a place to run when life feels overwhelming. You do not have to be strong enough on your own. His name is your shelter.
question: What would change in your life if you started running to God first when trouble comes?

text: There are many plans in a man's heart, but Yahweh's counsel will prevail.
reflection: You have plans for your life, but God has something better. His counsel does not compete with your dreams. It completes them. When you surrender your plans to God, you find the path that actually leads somewhere.
question: What plan in your heart do you need to surrender to God's counsel today?

text: A man's steps are from Yahweh; how then can man understand his way?
reflection: You do not always understand why your life takes the turns it does. But God is the one directing your steps, even when the path makes no sense. Trust is not about understanding. It is about knowing that God sees what you cannot.
question: Where do you need to trust God's direction instead of demanding answers?

text: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes, but Yahweh weighs the hearts.
reflection: You feel sure about the choices you make, but God sees what you cannot. He weighs your heart, not just your actions.
question: What part of your life have you assumed was right without ever asking God to show you the truth?

text: Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.
reflection: God gave you the chance to point a child toward Him. The path you set for them now will follow them all their lives.
question: What are you doing today to plant the truth of God in the hearts of the young people around you?

text: Don't let your heart envy sinners; but rather fear Yahweh all day long.
reflection: It is tempting to look at people who ignore God and seem to have it all. But God is building something in you that no shortcut can produce.
question: What would change if you feared God more than you envied others?

text: For a righteous man falls seven times, and rises up again; but the wicked are overthrown by calamity.
reflection: God does not expect you to never fall. He expects you to get back up. The difference between the righteous and the wicked is not perfection. It is perseverance. God is with you every time you rise.
question: What failure are you carrying that God is ready to help you rise from today?

text: If your enemy is hungry, give him food to eat. If he is thirsty, give him water to drink,
reflection: God does not ask you to love your enemy because your enemy deserves it. He asks you because He loved you first when you did not deserve it either. Every act of kindness toward someone who wronged you reflects the heart of God.
question: Who is God asking you to show that same kindness to today?
hook: Love the One Who Hurt You

text: For lack of wood a fire goes out. Without gossip, a quarrel dies down.
reflection: A quarrel needs fuel to survive. Gossip is that fuel. When you stop feeding the fire with your words, God can bring peace where there used to be conflict.
question: What fire in your life would die down if you simply stopped adding to it?

text: Iron sharpens iron; so a man sharpens his friend's countenance.
reflection: God did not design you to walk alone. He put people in your life to sharpen you, and He put you in their lives for the same reason. The people who challenge you are doing the work of God.
question: Who has God placed in your life to sharpen you?

text: He who conceals his sins doesn't prosper, but whoever confesses and renounces them finds mercy.
reflection: God already knows your heart completely. He is not waiting for a perfect confession. He is waiting for an honest one. You do not have to clean yourself up before you come to Him.
question: What are you still trying to hide from God, and what would it look like to bring it to Him today?

text: The fear of man proves to be a snare, but whoever puts his trust in Yahweh is kept safe.
reflection: God sees every moment you hold back because of what others might think. He is not asking you to be brave on your own. He is asking you to trust Him more than their opinion.
question: Where are you letting fear of what others think hold you back from what God is calling you to do?

text: Remove far from me falsehood and lies. Give me neither poverty nor riches. Feed me with the food that is needful for me,
reflection: God knows exactly what you need today. Not more, not less. He is not holding back from you. He is protecting you.
question: What would change in your heart if you trusted God to give you exactly what is best for you right now?

text: Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain; but a woman who fears Yahweh, she shall be praised.
reflection: The world measures you by what it can see on the outside. God measures you by what He sees in your heart. He is not impressed by what fades. He treasures what lasts.
question: What would it look like to build your life around what God values most?

text: Vanity of vanities, says the Preacher; vanity of vanities, all is vanity.
reflection: You have chased things that promised to fill you, and every single one left you wanting more. God is not surprised by that emptiness. He made you with a longing that only he can satisfy.
question: What are you chasing right now that keeps leaving you empty?
hook: What if everything you are chasing leads nowhere?

text: For to the man who pleases him, God gives wisdom, knowledge, and joy.
reflection: You have tried building your own happiness, and it never quite works. God does not ask you to manufacture joy. He gives it freely to anyone who comes to him.
question: What would your days look like if you stopped chasing joy and started receiving it from God?
hook: Joy is not earned. It is given.

text: For everything there is a season, and a time for every purpose under heaven.
reflection: You want to rush through the hard seasons and hold on to the good ones forever. But God put a purpose inside every season you walk through, even the ones that hurt.
question: What season are you in right now, and what might God be doing in you through it?
hook: God has a reason for every season you are in.

text: If a man prevails against one who is alone, two shall withstand him; and a threefold cord is not quickly broken.
reflection: You were never meant to carry everything alone. God gave you people to stand beside you because he knows you need them. When life pushes against you, the bond between you and the people around you will not easily break.
question: Who has God given you that you have been trying to do without?
hook: You were not meant to carry this alone.

text: Don't be rash with your mouth, and don't let your heart be hasty to utter anything before God; for God is in heaven, and you on earth.
reflection: God is not impressed by big words or long prayers. He is listening for what is real inside you. When you come to him, you do not need a speech. You just need honesty.
question: What would change in your prayers if you stopped trying to say the right things and simply told God the truth?
hook: God is not impressed by big words. He is listening for what is real.

text: Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of the desire. This also is vanity and a chasing after wind.
reflection: You spend so much energy wanting what you do not have that you miss what God has already placed right in front of you. He did not give you this life by accident.
question: What if the peace you have been chasing is already here, and God is waiting for you to notice?
hook: What if the thing you keep chasing is already right in front of you?

text: In the day of prosperity be joyful, and in the day of adversity consider.
reflection: God gives you good days and hard days, and both of them come from his hand. You do not have to understand why the hard season came. You just need to trust that the same God who gave you joy is walking with you through the pain.
question: Are you trusting him in both?
hook: God gave you both the good days and the hard ones. There is a reason.

text: Though a sinner commits crimes a hundred times, and lives long, yet surely I know that it will be better with those who fear God.
reflection: God sees everything, even the things you think no one noticed. His patience is not his approval. You are always better off living with a heart that honors him.
question: What are you holding onto that God is gently waiting for you to let go of?
hook: God's patience is not permission. He sees everything.

text: Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with your might; for there is no work, nor plan, nor knowledge, nor wisdom, in Sheol, where you are going.
reflection: God gave you today and he is asking you to live it with all your heart. You do not know what tomorrow holds, but you know who holds it. The work and the love right in front of you are what matter right now.
question: What is God asking you to give your best to today?
hook: You only get one shot at today. What are you doing with it?

text: If the ax is blunt, and one doesn't sharpen the edge, then he must use more strength; but skill brings success.
reflection: God did not give you your gifts and your time so you could waste them. He wants you to be wise about how you work, how you speak, and how you live.
question: What part of your life needs sharpening right now, and are you asking God to help you with it?
hook: Working harder is not always the answer. Sometimes you need to stop and sharpen.

text: He who watches the wind will not sow; and he who regards the clouds will not reap.
reflection: God is not asking you to wait until everything lines up. He is asking you to be faithful right where you are. You do not know which of your efforts will bear fruit, but God does.
question: What are you holding back from doing because the conditions do not feel right?
hook: If you wait for the perfect moment, you will never start.

text: This is the end of the matter. All has been heard. Fear God and keep his commandments; for this is the whole duty of man.
reflection: After everything you have searched for and wrestled with in life, God brings you back to the simplest truth. Your whole purpose is to trust him and walk with him.
question: What would change in your life today if you stopped overcomplicating things and simply obeyed what God has already shown you?
hook: What if your whole purpose could be summed up in one sentence?

text: Take me away with you! Let's hurry! The king has brought me into his rooms. We will be glad and rejoice in you. We will praise your love more than wine!
reflection: God is not waiting on the other side of the universe for you to earn your way to him. He has already brought you close. His love for you is better than anything this world offers, and he delights when you love him back.
question: Where have you been searching for satisfaction that only God's presence can give?
hook: What if the love you have been looking for has already found you?

text: He brought me to the banquet hall. His banner over me is love.
reflection: God has placed a banner over your life, and that banner is love. It is not something you earned or something that can be taken from you. He is not hiding how he feels about you. He has made it public.
question: How does knowing that God has declared his love over you change the way you walk through today?
hook: What if God's love for you was not hidden but declared over your life for everyone to see?

text: I had scarcely passed from them, when I found him whom my soul loves. I held him, and would not let him go.
reflection: God has never left you, even when it felt like he did. He lets you search because the seeking deepens your love. And when you find him again, you hold on tighter than before.
question: What season of distance from God has actually drawn you closer to him in the end?
hook: Have you ever felt like God was far away, only to discover he was closer than you thought?

text: You have ravished my heart, my sister, my bride. You have ravished my heart with one of your eyes, with one chain of your neck.
reflection: You have captured God's heart. Not because you are flawless, but because you are his. One glance of faith, one step toward him, and he is moved. God does not look at you with indifference. He looks at you with delight.
question: How would you live differently today if you truly believed God is delighted by you?
hook: What if one single look of faith from you moves the heart of God?

text: His mouth is sweetness; yes, he is altogether lovely. This is my beloved, and this is my friend, sons of Jerusalem.
reflection: God is not a distant king you admire from far away. He is altogether lovely, and he wants to be your friend. Everything about him is good, and nothing in him will ever let you down.
question: When was the last time you stopped and simply told God what you love about him?
hook: What if the one your heart has been longing for is not only your God but also your closest friend?

text: I am my beloved's, and my beloved is mine. He browses among the lilies.
reflection: You belong to God, and God belongs to you. That is not a wish. It is the truest thing about your life. No failure on your part can break what God has decided about you.
question: Have you let your heart rest in the fact that you are his and he is yours?
hook: What if the most secure place in the world is knowing you belong to someone who will never let go?

text: I am my beloved's. His desire is toward me.
reflection: God is not waiting for you to chase him. His desire is already aimed at you. Before you took a single step toward him, he was moving toward you. You are not reaching for someone who is pulling away.
question: What would change if you believed that God wants you as much as you want him?
hook: What if God wants you even more than you want him?

text: Set me as a seal on your heart, as a seal on your arm; for love is strong as death. Jealousy is as cruel as Sheol. Its flashes are flashes of fire, a very flame of Yahweh.
reflection: Nothing you have walked through is stronger than God's love for you. His love is not fragile. It has survived every flood, every failure, every season you thought would be the end. No force in this world can put out what God has set on fire.
question: Do you believe his love is really that unbreakable?
hook: What if the love holding your life together is a fire nothing in this world can put out?

text: 'Come now, and let's reason together,' says Yahweh: 'Though your sins are as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow.'
reflection: God does not wait for you to clean up your life before He reaches out. He invites you, right in the middle of your mess, and promises that no stain is too deep for Him to remove.
question: What sin have you been carrying that you need to bring to God today?

text: House of Jacob, come, and let's walk in the light of Yahweh.
reflection: God does not leave you to stumble through the dark on your own. He invites you to walk with Him, in His light, where everything becomes clear.
question: Are you trying to navigate your life by your own understanding, or are you willing to walk in the light God is offering you?

text: Tell the righteous that it will be well with them, for they will eat the fruit of their deeds.
reflection: God sees every quiet act of faithfulness in your life. When the world feels chaotic and nothing seems fair, He wants you to know that your obedience is not wasted. It will be well with you because God keeps every promise.
question: How is He asking you to stay faithful today?

text: Yahweh will create over the whole habitation of Mount Zion and over her assemblies, a cloud and smoke by day, and the shining of a flaming fire by night.
reflection: God does not just forgive you and send you on your way. He stays with you, covering your life with His presence like a shelter that never comes down.
question: Where in your life do you need to stop building your own protection and rest under the covering God has already placed over you?

text: Woe to those who call evil good, and good evil; who put darkness for light, and light for darkness.
reflection: It is easy to rename the things in your life that you know are wrong. But God sees through every label you put on your choices. He loves you too much to let you stay confused.
question: What is one thing in your life that you have been calling by the wrong name?

text: I heard the Lord's voice, saying, 'Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?' Then I said, 'Here I am. Send me!'
reflection: God is not looking for perfect people to do His work. He is looking for willing ones. Before He sends you anywhere, He meets you right where you are and makes you ready.
question: What has God been preparing you for, and are you willing to say yes to Him today?

text: Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign. Behold, the virgin will conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.
reflection: God did not wait to be asked. He gave the sign Himself. The name Immanuel means God with us, and that tells you everything about who He is. He does not love you from a distance. He comes close.
question: How does knowing God is with you change the way you face today?

text: Yahweh of Armies is who you must respect as holy. He is the one you must fear. He is the one you must dread.
reflection: The world is full of things trying to make you afraid. But God says there is only one fear that matters, and it is fear of Him. When you give God that place, the threats around you lose their power.
question: What fear has been controlling you that needs to be replaced by trust in God?

text: For a child is born to us. A son is given to us; and the government will be on his shoulders. His name will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.
reflection: Every name God gives this child is for you. He is your Counselor when you do not know what to do. He is your Mighty God when you feel powerless. He is your Prince of Peace when everything is falling apart.
question: Which of these names do you need most today?

text: It will come to pass in that day that the remnant of Israel and those who have escaped of the house of Jacob will no longer depend on him who struck them, but will depend on Yahweh, the Holy One of Israel, in truth.
reflection: You may have been leaning on something that was hurting you. A habit, a relationship, or a way of thinking that promised safety but only brought pain. God is calling you to shift your weight to Him.
question: What are you leaning on today that God is asking you to let go of?

text: Yahweh's Spirit will rest on him: the spirit of wisdom and understanding, the spirit of counsel and might, the spirit of knowledge and of the fear of Yahweh.
reflection: You might be in a season where everything feels cut down to nothing. But God has always done His best work in places that look finished. He is already growing something new in what you thought was dead.
question: Where in your life does God want to bring new growth today?
hook: The Spirit of Wisdom and Might

text: Behold, God is my salvation. I will trust, and will not be afraid; for Yah, Yahweh, is my strength and song; and he has become my salvation.
reflection: You do not have to wait until your fear goes away before you trust God. Trust is what you do while the fear is still there. God is asking you to lean into Him even when everything in you wants to run.
question: What fear is God inviting you to trust Him with today?

text: 'I will punish the world for their evil, and the wicked for their iniquity. I will cause the arrogance of the proud to cease, and will humble the haughtiness of the terrible.'
reflection: God does not ignore pride. He addresses it because it keeps you from the one thing that could save you, which is honesty before Him. Pride says you do not need God, but that has never been true.
question: Where has pride been keeping you from being honest with God about what you really need?

text: For Yahweh of Armies has planned, and who can stop it? His hand is stretched out, and who can turn it back?
reflection: Nothing in your life is bigger than what God has planned for you. His hand is stretched out over your story, and no one can pull it back.
question: What situation in your life do you need to trust to God's unshakeable plan today?
hook: No One Can Stop God's Plan

text: My heart cries out for Moab! Her nobles flee to Zoar, to Eglath Shelishiyah.
reflection: God does not watch people suffer and feel nothing. Even in judgment, His heart grieves for those who turned away. He loves you too much to feel nothing when it hurts.
question: How does it change the way you see God to know that He grieves for you?

text: A throne will be established in loving kindness. One will sit on it in truth, in the tent of David, judging, seeking justice, and swift to do righteousness.
reflection: God does not sit on a throne to keep you at a distance. His rule is built on loving kindness, and every decision He makes over your life flows from tenderness, not coldness.
question: When you picture God on His throne, do you see a distant ruler or a kind King who rules with love, and how does that picture change the way you come to Him today?

text: In that day, people will look to their Maker, and their eyes will have respect for the Holy One of Israel.
reflection: God knows you were not built to carry your own life. There are things you lean on every day that were never meant to hold you. When everything else falls away, God is the only one left standing.
question: What are you depending on right now that keeps your eyes off God, and what would it take for you to look back to the One who made you and loves you?

text: In that time, a present will be brought to Yahweh of Armies from a people tall and smooth.
reflection: God does not stop at borders. His grace stretches to the farthest corners of the earth, to people no one expected to find Him. If He reaches that far, you are not beyond His hand.
question: Have you ever felt too far from God to come back, and does it change anything to know that He is already reaching for people at the very ends of the earth?

text: because Yahweh of Armies has blessed them, saying, 'Blessed be Egypt my people, Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel my inheritance.'
reflection: God looked at a nation that had been His enemy for generations and called them "my people." No history was too dark for Him to redeem. If God can turn an enemy into family, nothing in your past is beyond His grace.
question: What does that say about His willingness to welcome you back no matter what you have done or how far you have gone?

text: Those who dwell in this coast will say in that day, 'Behold, such is our expectation, where we fled for help to be delivered from the king of Assyria. And we, how will we escape?'
reflection: Every place you run to instead of God will eventually leave you standing in the open. The things that feel safe today will not hold when the real storm comes. God is the only refuge that does not crumble.
question: What are you running to for safety right now instead of God, and what would it look like to stop fleeing to things that cannot hold you and turn to the one place that can?

text: The burden of Dumah. One calls to me out of Seir, 'Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?'
reflection: You have been there. The night stretches on and you ask God, "How much longer?" He does not always answer with a schedule, but He answers with His presence. He meets you in the question.
question: What are you waiting on God for right now, and can you trust Him even before the answer comes?

text: I will lay the key of David's house on his shoulder. He will open, and no one will shut. He will shut, and no one will open.
reflection: God holds the key to every door in your life. When He opens a way for you, nothing can close it. When He shuts a door, no striving can force it open.
question: What door are you trying to force right now that God may be asking you to release to Him?

text: Her merchandise and her wages will be holiness to Yahweh. It will not be treasured nor laid up; for her merchandise will be for those who dwell before Yahweh.
reflection: God sees every season you spent chasing the wrong things. Those years are not wasted in His eyes. He can take what you poured into selfish pursuits and turn it into something that serves His kingdom.
question: What part of your past have you written off that God might be waiting to redeem?

text: Therefore glorify Yahweh in the east, even the name of Yahweh, the God of Israel, in the islands of the sea!
reflection: When everything around you shakes, God still deserves your praise. Not because the pain is not real, but because He is more real than anything crumbling at your feet. He calls you to worship even when the ground feels unsteady.
question: Where could you choose to praise God today even though nothing has changed?

text: He has swallowed up death forever! The Lord Yahweh will wipe away tears from off all faces.
reflection: God has promised you an ending where death has no power and tears are gone forever. Every ache you carry, every loss that stings, He already knows how your story resolves. He is the one who finishes it.
question: What grief are you holding that you need to trust God will one day wipe away?

text: You will keep whoever's mind is steadfast in perfect peace, because he trusts in you.
reflection: Your mind races with worry and fear. But God offers you something the world cannot. When you anchor your thoughts on Him, He wraps you in a peace nothing can shake.
question: What thought keeps pulling you away from peace, and what would it look like to hand it to God?

text: Or else let him take hold of my strength, that he may make peace with me. Let him make peace with me.
reflection: God is not pulling away from you. He is holding out His strength and asking you to grab on.
question: What would it look like for you to stop running and make peace with Him today?

text: Therefore the Lord Yahweh says, 'Behold, I lay in Zion for a foundation a stone, a tried stone, a precious cornerstone of a sure foundation.'
reflection: Everything you build your life on will be tested. Jobs shift, relationships change, even your own strength gives out. But God has laid one foundation that never cracks.
question: What are you standing on today, and what would it look like to put your full weight on Him?

text: The Lord said, 'Because this people draw near with their mouth and honor me with their lips, but they have removed their heart far from me.'
reflection: God is not counting your prayers or measuring how well you say them. He is looking for your heart.
question: Where in your life have you been going through the motions with God while your heart has drifted somewhere else?

text: Your ears will hear a voice behind you, saying, 'This is the way. Walk in it,' when you turn to the right hand, and when you turn to the left.
reflection: You will face choices today that pull you in every direction. But God has not left you to guess which way to go. He is right behind you, whispering the path.
question: What decision are you wrestling with that you need to stop and listen for His voice on?

text: As birds hovering, so Yahweh of Armies will protect Jerusalem. He will protect and deliver it. He will pass over and preserve it.
reflection: God does not protect you from a safe distance. He hovers over your life the way a mother bird covers her young, close enough to feel the danger and strong enough to shield you from it.
question: What area of your life do you need to trust Him to cover today?

text: The work of righteousness will be peace, and the effect of righteousness, quietness and confidence forever.
reflection: The peace you keep chasing is not found in better circumstances. It is the fruit of walking with God. When you live the way He designed you to live, peace follows you home.
question: What area of your life needs less striving and more surrender to Him?

text: There will be stability in your times, abundance of salvation, wisdom, and knowledge. The fear of Yahweh is your treasure.
reflection: You spend your life looking for security, something solid to stand on when everything else shakes. God says your real treasure is not comfort or certainty. It is knowing Him.
question: What would your priorities look like if you treated your relationship with God as the most valuable thing you own?

text: Search in the book of Yahweh, and read: Not one of these will be missing. None will lack her mate.
reflection: God keeps every promise He has ever made. Not one has been lost, forgotten, or left unfulfilled. When you search His word, you find a God who has never once failed to follow through.
question: What promise of God do you need to trust Him with today?

text: Then Yahweh's ransomed ones will return, and come with singing to Zion; and everlasting joy will be on their heads.
reflection: God promises a day when your sorrow will not just fade but will actually flee from you. Every tear, every ache, every sleepless night will be replaced by a joy that never ends.
question: What sorrow are you carrying right now that you need to trust God to turn into singing?

text: But if you tell me, 'We trust in Yahweh our God,' isn't that he whose high places and whose altars Hezekiah has taken away?
reflection: The enemy will always try to make your trust in God look foolish. But God does not need anyone's permission to save you.
question: What voice has been shaking your confidence, and what would change if you quietly chose to keep trusting?

text: 'Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, who is enthroned among the cherubim, you are the God, even you alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth.'
reflection: When Hezekiah was surrounded and out of options, he did not panic. He prayed. He reminded God of who God is, and he asked for help. You have the same God available to you right now.
question: What situation in your life needs you to stop and bring it honestly before God in prayer?

text: Go, and tell Hezekiah, Yahweh, the God of David your father, says, I have heard your prayer. I have seen your tears.
reflection: God told Hezekiah that He had heard his prayer and seen his tears. That same God hears you right now. He sees the things you cry about when no one else is watching.
question: What prayer have you been afraid God might not hear, and are you willing to trust that He already has?

text: Then Hezekiah said to Isaiah, 'Yahweh's word which you have spoken is good.' For he said, 'There will be peace and truth in my days.'
reflection: When God speaks a hard word into your life, it takes courage to accept it. Hezekiah did not argue with God. He accepted the correction and called it good.
question: Do you trust God enough to receive His correction and believe it is leading you somewhere better?

text: But those who wait for Yahweh will renew their strength. They will mount up with wings like eagles. They will run, and not be weary. They will walk, and not faint.
reflection: God does not promise to remove your tiredness. He promises to meet you in it and renew your strength.
question: When you feel like you have nothing left, are you willing to wait on Him and trust that His power shows up exactly when yours runs out?

text: Don't be afraid, for I am with you. Don't be dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you. Yes, I will help you. Yes, I will uphold you with the right hand of my righteousness.
reflection: God says the same thing to you that He said to His people through Isaiah. Do not be afraid. I am with you. I will help you.
question: When you feel overwhelmed today, will you let those words settle into your heart and trust that God is closer than your fear?

text: He won't break a bruised reed. He won't quench a smoking flax. He will faithfully bring justice.
reflection: God does not look at your weakness and walk away. When your faith feels like a flickering candle about to go out, He cups His hand around the flame instead of blowing it out.
question: How does knowing that God is gentle with your brokenness change the way you come to Him?

text: When you pass through the waters, I will be with you, and through the rivers, they will not overflow you.
reflection: God does not promise to keep you out of the water or away from the fire. He promises to go through it with you.
question: What difficult season are you walking through right now, and does it change anything to know that God is walking through it beside you?

text: For I will pour water on him who is thirsty, and streams on the dry ground. I will pour my Spirit on your descendants, and my blessing on your offspring.
reflection: God sees where your life feels dry and empty, and His answer is not a trickle. He promises to pour. He does not hold back from you when you are thirsty.
question: What part of your life needs God to bring refreshment right now?

text: 'Look to me, and be saved, all the ends of the earth; for I am God, and there is no other.'
reflection: God does not ask you to earn your way to Him. He does not say figure it out first and then come. He says look to me. That is it.
question: What is keeping you from simply turning your eyes toward God today and letting Him be enough?

text: Even to old age I am he, and even to gray hairs I will carry you. I have made, and I will bear. Yes, I will carry, and will deliver.
reflection: You will not always be as strong as you are today, but God will never stop carrying you. He made you, He knows you, and He will hold you through every season of your life.
question: Where do you need God to carry you right now?

text: For you have trusted in your wickedness. You have said, 'No one sees me.'
reflection: You cannot hide anything from God. Not the quiet compromises, not the pride that whispers you do not need Him. God sees it all, and He loves you too much to let you stay there.
question: What are you holding in the dark that God is asking you to bring into the light?

text: Yahweh, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel says: 'I am Yahweh your God, who teaches you to profit, who leads you by the way that you should go.'
reflection: God is not standing far away telling you to figure life out on your own. He is right beside you, teaching you what actually matters and leading you where you need to go.
question: Where do you need to trust His direction instead of relying on your own understanding?

text: Behold, I have engraved you on the palms of my hands. Your walls are continually before me.
reflection: God did not just write your name down somewhere He might check later. He engraved you on His hands. You are not a passing thought to God. You are always before Him.
question: How does knowing that God constantly holds you in His thoughts change the way you see yourself today?

text: For the Lord Yahweh will help me. Therefore I have not been confounded. Therefore I have set my face like a flint, and I know that I won't be disappointed.
reflection: When you know that God is on your side, you do not have to be afraid of what comes next. He has promised to help you, and He has never once failed.
question: What situation in your life do you need to face with the confidence that God is helping you through it?

text: Those ransomed by Yahweh will return, and come with singing to Zion. Everlasting joy will be on their heads. They will obtain gladness and joy.
reflection: God has promised that sorrow and sighing will flee your life forever. You are not stuck in the sadness you feel right now. He is bringing you to a place where joy stays and grief disappears.
question: What sorrow do you need to trust God to turn into lasting joy?

text: How beautiful on the mountains are the feet of him who brings good news, who publishes peace, who brings good news, who proclaims salvation.
reflection: God did not keep the good news to Himself. He sent someone to announce it right where you are, because He wanted you to hear it.
question: When was the last time you let the good news of what God has done truly fill your heart with joy?
hook: The Best News You Will Hear

text: But he was pierced for our transgressions. He was crushed for our iniquities. The punishment that brought our peace was on him; and by his wounds we are healed.
reflection: The pain that should have been yours was placed on someone who never deserved it. God did not look away from your sin. He looked straight at it and sent His Son to carry it for you.
question: What does it mean to you that Jesus was wounded so you could be healed?

text: 'For the mountains may depart, and the hills be removed; but my loving kindness will not depart from you, and my covenant of peace will not be removed.'
reflection: Everything around you can change, but God's love for you will not. Mountains may crumble and hills may disappear, but His kindness toward you is permanent.
question: What feels unstable in your life right now that you need to trust to a God whose love never moves?

text: 'Hey! Come, everyone who thirsts, to the waters! Come, he who has no money, buy, and eat!'
reflection: God is not waiting for you to have something to offer before He invites you in. He is calling you right now, empty-handed and thirsty, because everything you need is already His to give.
question: What are you thirsty for that only God can satisfy?

text: I will bring them to my holy mountain, and make them joyful in my house of prayer. Their burnt offerings and their sacrifices will be accepted on my altar.
reflection: God calls His house a place of joy for all who come.
question: Have you ever felt like you did not belong in His presence, and what would change if you believed He was talking directly to you?
hook: His House Is Open to You

text: For the high and lofty One who inhabits eternity, whose name is Holy, says: 'I dwell in the high and holy place, with him also who is of a contrite and humble spirit.'
reflection: The God who fills eternity chose to make His home with the humble.
question: What would it mean for you to stop trying to impress God and just come to Him with an honest heart?

text: Yahweh will guide you continually, satisfy your soul in dry places, and make your bones strong.
reflection: God promises to guide you continually, not just once.
question: Where in your life right now do you need to stop trying to find the way on your own and let God lead you?
hook: He Will Guide You Every Day

text: Behold, Yahweh's hand is not shortened, that it can't save; nor his ear heavy, that it can't hear.
reflection: God's power to save you has not weakened, and His willingness to hear you has not changed.
question: What is standing between you and Him today, and are you willing to let Him remove it?
hook: Nothing Stops God from Reaching You

text: Arise, shine; for your light has come, and Yahweh's glory has risen on you.
reflection: God says your light has already come, even before you can see it.
question: What area of darkness in your life is God asking you to believe He has already begun to shine through?

text: The Lord Yahweh's Spirit is on me; because Yahweh has anointed me to preach good news to the humble.
reflection: God sent Jesus on a specific mission to find the brokenhearted and set them free.
question: What part of your heart has been waiting for someone to come and bind it up?

text: You will also be a crown of beauty in Yahweh's hand, and a royal diadem in your God's hand.
reflection: God does not see you as damaged or discarded. He holds you in His hand like a crown of beauty.
question: What name have you been calling yourself that God wants to replace with the one He has chosen for you?
hook: A Crown of Beauty in His Hand

text: In all their affliction he was afflicted, and the angel of his presence saved them. In his love and in his pity he redeemed them.
reflection: God was not distant when you suffered. He was afflicted right alongside you.
question: What pain in your life do you need to believe God is carrying with you right now?

text: But now, Yahweh, you are our Father. We are the clay, and you our potter. We all are the work of your hand.
reflection: God is your Father, and you are the work of His hands.
question: What part of your life do you need to stop resisting and let the Potter shape as He sees fit?
hook: You Are in the Potter's Hands

text: 'For, behold, I create new heavens and a new earth; and the former things will not be remembered, nor come into mind.'
reflection: God promises that the old sorrows you carry will not even come to mind when He makes all things new.
question: What past pain do you need to trust God is already working to redeem?

text: 'For my hand has made all these things, and so all these things came to be,' says Yahweh: 'but I will look to this man, even to he who is poor and of a contrite spirit, and who trembles at my word.'
reflection: God made the heavens and the earth, yet He turns His attention to the one who comes to Him with a humble heart.
question: What would change in your life today if you believed that the God of all creation is looking directly at you with love?

text: 'Before I formed you in the womb, I knew you. Before you were born, I sanctified you. I have appointed you a prophet to the nations.'
reflection: God knew you before you took your first breath. He did not wait for you to become something before he loved you. He chose you on purpose, before you could choose him.
question: How does knowing that God has always known you change the way you see yourself today?
hook: What if God had a plan for your life before you ever took your first breath?

text: 'For my people have committed two evils: they have forsaken me, the spring of living waters, and cut out cisterns for themselves, broken cisterns that can hold no water.'
reflection: You have tried filling the empty place inside you with things that cannot hold. God is not one option among many. He is the only spring that will not run dry.
question: What have you been reaching for instead of him?
hook: You keep digging wells that leak while a spring of living water is right behind you.

text: Return, you backsliding children, I will heal your backsliding. Behold, we have come to you; for you are Yahweh our God.
reflection: God is not standing at the end of a long road waiting for you to earn your way back. He says return, and promises healing in the same breath.
question: What part of your life have you been holding from God because you feel too far gone?
hook: God does not wait for you to clean yourself up. He says come back, and the healing starts.

text: For Yahweh says to the men of Judah and to Jerusalem, 'Break up your fallow ground, and don't sow among thorns.'
reflection: God is asking you to tend the ground of your own heart. The weeds and thorns did not show up overnight, and they will not clear themselves. He is not looking for perfection from you. He is looking for an honest start.
question: What part of your heart has gone untended for too long?
hook: Nothing new can grow in a heart that has never been turned over.

text: Don't you fear me?' says Yahweh. 'Won't you tremble at my presence?'
reflection: God set a boundary for the ocean, and every wave obeys it. Not one has ever crossed the line he drew. That same God is asking you a simple question today. You have seen his power. You have heard his voice.
question: What would it take for you to listen the way the waves do?
hook: The ocean has never once crossed the line God drew. What about you?

text: Yahweh says, 'Stand in the ways and see, and ask for the ancient paths, where the good way is; and walk in it, and you will find rest for your souls.'
reflection: God is not hiding the good road from you. He is standing at the crossroads pointing to it. The path that leads to real rest has always been there. You just have to be willing to walk it.
question: What old road has God been inviting you back to that you have been avoiding?
hook: What if the rest you have been searching for has been waiting on a road you stopped walking?

text: But this thing I commanded them, saying, 'Listen to my voice, and I will be your God, and you shall be my people.'
reflection: God is not asking for complicated things. He is asking you to listen. Just listen. He says if you do, he will be your God and you will be his. That is the whole offer.
question: What would change if you stopped overcomplicating your walk with God and just listened to his voice today?
hook: God made the simplest deal there has ever been. Listen to him and he will be your God.

text: Is there no balm in Gilead? Is there no physician there? Why then isn't the health of the daughter of my people recovered?
reflection: God is not wondering whether a cure exists. He already has it. The question he is asking is why you have not come to him yet. The healing for everything broken in your life is standing right in front of you.
question: What wound are you still carrying that you have never brought to God?
hook: There is a cure. There is a healer. So why are you still carrying the wound?

text: Yahweh says, 'Don't let the wise man glory in his wisdom. Don't let the mighty man glory in his might. Don't let the rich man glory in his riches.'
reflection: God is not impressed by the things the world measures you by. Not your intelligence, not your strength, not your bank account. The only thing worth building your identity on is knowing him.
question: What have you been measuring your worth by that God says is not the point?
hook: God says the things you are most proud of are the wrong things to build your life on.

text: Yahweh, I know that the way of man is not in himself. It is not in man who walks to direct his steps.
reflection: You plan, you decide, you choose your direction. But God is the one who actually moves your feet. Every step you have ever taken happened because he allowed it.
question: What would change in your life if you stopped pretending you were in control and let God lead?
hook: You think you know where you are going. God says the ability to get there was never yours.

text: But, Yahweh of Armies, who judges righteously, who tests the heart and the mind, I will see your vengeance on them; for I have revealed my cause to you.
reflection: God sees every hidden thing that has been done against you and every wound you carry in silence.
question: What hurt are you still holding onto that you have not yet placed in his hands?
hook: When everyone turns against you, there is still one place to bring your pain.

text: 'If you have run with the footmen, and they have wearied you, then how can you contend with horses?'
reflection: God sees the small struggles that have already left you exhausted, and he knows bigger ones are ahead.
question: What if he is using this season not to break you, but to prepare you for something greater?
hook: If the easy things have already worn you out, what will you do when life gets truly hard?

text: Can the Ethiopian change his skin, or the leopard his spots? Then may you also do good, who are accustomed to do evil.
reflection: You know the habits you cannot seem to break, the patterns you keep falling back into. God is not waiting for you to fix yourself.
question: What would happen if you stopped trying to change on your own and let him do what only he can?
hook: You have tried to change yourself and failed. What if that was the point all along?

text: 'Why should you be like a scared man, like a mighty man who can't save? Yet you, Yahweh, are in our midst.'
reflection: God has not left you. Even when everything feels uncertain and you wonder if he is paying attention, he is right in the middle of your situation.
question: What fear are you carrying that would lose its grip if you really believed God was standing with you right now?
hook: You cry out to God wondering if he can hear you. He has been right beside you the whole time.

text: Your words were found, and I ate them. Your words were to me a joy and the rejoicing of my heart.
reflection: God knows the seasons when your faithfulness costs you everything. He also knows that one word from him can feed your soul in ways nothing else can. You are called by his name, and that joy will outlast every empty seat.
question: What word from God has kept you standing when everyone else walked away?
hook: When everything else goes silent, God's word feeds your soul in ways nothing else can.

text: Yahweh, my strength and my stronghold, and my refuge in the day of affliction.
reflection: Every other refuge you have built will eventually fall apart. God is not one shelter among many. He is the only stronghold that has never collapsed under the weight of your worst day.
question: What have you been leaning on that was never meant to hold you?
hook: Every refuge you have built will eventually fall apart. God is the only stronghold that never will.

text: Blessed is the man who trusts in Yahweh, and whose confidence is in Yahweh.
reflection: When the heat comes and everything around you dries up, God is the one thing that will not fail you. He is not just giving you advice to hold on. He is the ground your whole life is rooted in.
question: What are you trusting right now that will not survive the next drought?
hook: The difference between thriving and withering is not what happens to you. It is where your roots are.

text: 'House of Israel, can't I do with you as this potter?' says Yahweh. 'Behold, as the clay in the potter's hand, so are you in my hand.'
reflection: God has never looked at you and seen something to throw away. When his plans for your life hit something broken, he does not start over with someone else. He starts over with you.
question: What broken place in your life might God be reshaping right now?
hook: God has never looked at you and seen something to throw away.

text: 'Even so I will break this people and this city, as one breaks a potter's vessel, that can't be made whole again.'
reflection: God does not give warnings to scare you. He gives them because he can see the path you are on and he loves you too much to let you walk it without a word.
question: What warning has God been giving you that you have been choosing to ignore?
hook: God does not give warnings to scare you. He gives them because he loves you.

text: If I say, 'I will not make mention of him, or speak any more in his name,' then there is in my heart as it were a burning fire shut up in my bones.
reflection: Have you ever tried to hold back what God placed on your heart, only to feel it burning inside you until you could not keep silent any longer? God does not give you words that are meant to stay hidden. When his truth fills you, it becomes a fire that no fear or weariness can smother.
question: What is God asking you to speak today that you have been holding back?
hook: The fire you cannot put out

text: To this people you shall say, 'Yahweh says: Behold, I set before you the way of life and the way of death.'
reflection: God is not hiding the way from you. He has set life and death before you and asked you to choose. There is no secret path and no riddle to solve. The question is not whether you know which road leads to life. The question is whether you are willing to walk it today.
