question: What is keeping you from choosing the way God has already made clear?

text: Yahweh says: Execute justice and righteousness, and deliver him who is robbed out of the hand of the oppressor.
reflection: God is watching how you treat the people around you. He does not measure you by your position, your title, or your reputation. He measures you by whether you protect the vulnerable and do what is right even when no one is watching.
question: Where in your life has God asked you to stand up for someone who cannot stand up for themselves?

text: 'Am I a God at hand,' says Yahweh, 'and not a God afar off?'
reflection: You may feel like God is far away, but he is asking you a question right now. Am I only nearby, or do I fill every corner of your life? There is no room you walk into and no thought you carry that God does not already see. That is not a threat. That is the promise that you are never out of his reach, even when you feel most alone.
question: Where in your life have you been acting as if God could not see?

text: 'I will give them a heart to know me, that I am Yahweh. They will be my people, and I will be their God.'
reflection: You did not choose to know God on your own. He gave you the heart to recognize Him. That desire you feel to draw close to Him, that restlessness when you drift away, that is God keeping His promise to never let you go.
question: When you feel far from God today, will you remember that He already placed the longing for Him inside your heart?

text: 'Return now everyone from his evil way, and from the evil of your doings, and dwell in the land that Yahweh has given to you and to your fathers.'
reflection: God is not standing far away waiting for you to earn your way back. He is calling you right now, the same way He has been calling for longer than you know. When you hear that voice asking you to turn around, that is not anger. That is love refusing to let you go.
question: What would it look like for you to stop walking and turn toward God today?

text: 'Now therefore amend your ways and your doings, and obey Yahweh your God.'
reflection: God does not warn you because He wants to punish you. He warns you because He wants to save you. Every correction from God is an open door, not a closed fist. He is giving you the chance right now to turn things around before the weight of your choices catches up.
question: What is one thing God has been asking you to change, and what would it take to obey Him today?
hook: It is not too late to change

text: 'I have made the earth, the men, and the animals that are on the surface of the earth by my great power and by my outstretched arm.'
reflection: Everything you have came from the hand of God. Your breath, your home, the ground you walk on. He made it all and He gives it to whom He chooses. That is not a reason to be afraid. That is a reason to trust Him completely, because the God who made you knows exactly what you need.
question: Are you trying to control what only God has the right to give?
hook: The God who made it all

text: The prophet who prophesies of peace, when the word of the prophet comes to pass, then the prophet will be known, that Yahweh has truly sent him.
reflection: Not everything that sounds good comes from God. Some words comfort you without changing you. They tell you what you want to hear instead of what you need to hear. God's real voice always calls you closer to Him, even when the truth is uncomfortable. He loves you too much to leave you where you are.
question: Are you listening for the voice that tells you the truth, or the one that just tells you everything is fine?
hook: The truth is not always what you want to hear

text: 'For I know the thoughts that I think toward you,' says Yahweh, 'thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you hope and a future.'
reflection: When everything feels uncertain, God is not confused. He already knows the thoughts He thinks toward you, and they are not thoughts of harm. They are thoughts of peace, designed to carry you toward a future He has already prepared. You do not have to figure it all out on your own. God is already working ahead of you.
question: Will you trust that His plan for your life is better than the one you are trying to hold together?
hook: God already knows what comes next for you

text: 'For I will restore health to you, and I will heal you of your wounds,' says Yahweh.
reflection: You may be carrying a wound right now that feels like it will never close. Something that broke inside you and no amount of time or effort has been able to fix. God sees it. He is not looking away. He says He will restore your health and heal your wounds, and He means it. The same God who let you feel the sting of what went wrong is the one reaching toward you now.
question: Will you let Him touch the place that still hurts?
hook: The God who heals what no one else can

text: Yahweh appeared of old to me, saying, 'Yes, I have loved you with an everlasting love. Therefore I have drawn you with loving kindness.'
reflection: God did not start loving you the day you believed. His love for you has no beginning you can point to. It reaches further back than your worst mistake and stretches further forward than your biggest fear. He drew you to Himself not because you earned it, but because His love simply would not let you go.
question: Are you still trying to earn a love that was already yours before you took your first breath?
hook: A love that started before you were born

text: 'Behold, I am Yahweh, the God of all flesh. Is there anything too hard for me?'
reflection: You are looking at something in your life right now that feels impossible. Maybe it is a relationship you cannot fix, a habit you cannot break, or a future you cannot imagine. God is asking you one question. Is there anything too hard for me? He is not asking because He does not know the answer. He is asking because you need to hear it. Nothing in your life is beyond His reach.
question: Do you believe that?
hook: Nothing in your life is too hard for God

text: 'Call to me, and I will answer you, and will show you great and difficult things, which you don't know.'
reflection: You might wonder if God really hears you when you pray. He does more than hear you. He is inviting you right now to come to Him with everything on your heart. He promises not just to listen but to answer, and to show you things you never expected.
question: What would change in your prayer life if you truly believed God was waiting to answer you?
hook: God is not waiting for you to stop asking

text: 'I will give the men who have transgressed my covenant, who have not performed the words of the covenant which they made before me, when they cut the calf in two and passed between its parts,'
reflection: God does not forget the promises you make to Him, even the ones you made when you were afraid. He takes your word seriously because He takes you seriously. He is not looking for perfect obedience. He is looking for honest commitment.
question: What promise have you quietly taken back from God, and what would it look like to mean it this time?
hook: God remembers the promises you made

text: 'The words of Jonadab the son of Rechab, that he commanded his sons, not to drink wine, are performed; and to this day they drink none, but obey their father's commandment.'
reflection: God has spoken to you more times than you can count. Through His word, through people, through the quiet pull on your heart. He is not asking you for something impossible. He is asking you to simply listen and follow.
question: Where in your life have you been deciding which of God's words to follow and which to ignore?
hook: God has spoken to you more times than you know

text: 'Perhaps the house of Judah will hear all the evil which I intend to do to them, that they may each return from his evil way; that I may forgive their iniquity and their sin.'
reflection: Every word God sends your way is proof that He has not given up on you. He is not recording your failures. He is reaching for you. He warns because He loves, and every warning carries the same offer it always has. Come back, and I will forgive you.
question: What is God's word saying to you right now that you have been trying not to hear?
hook: Every warning from God is really an invitation

text: Then Zedekiah the king sent and took him out; and the king asked him secretly in his house, 'Is there any word from Yahweh?'
reflection: Every plan you have trusted besides God will eventually run out of answers. And when it does, this question rises from the deepest part of you. Is there a word from God for me? The answer is always yes. God has not gone silent because you went away. His word is waiting for you right now.
question: What have you been trusting instead of God, and what would it look like to finally bring your question to Him?
hook: The question your soul will always eventually ask

text: But Jeremiah said, 'They will not deliver you. Please obey Yahweh's voice, in that which I speak to you; so it will be well with you, and your soul will live.'
reflection: God is not trying to make your life harder. When He tells you what to do, it is because He wants things to go well with you. Every good thing God has for you is waiting on the other side of the step you are afraid to take.
question: What step has God been asking you to take that you have been putting off because you are afraid?
hook: God is pleading with you to take the path that leads to life

text: 'For I will surely save you. You won't fall by the sword, but your life will be a prize for you, because you have put your trust in me,' says Yahweh.
reflection: God sees every quiet act of trust you have ever made. Even when nobody else notices what you did for Him, He notices. The promise is personal. You trusted me and I will not let you fall. Your faith in God is never wasted.
question: Where in your life right now is God asking you to trust Him, even when it looks like everything around you is falling apart?
hook: Your trust in God is never wasted

text: Now, behold, I release you today from the chains which are on your hand. If it seems good to you to come with me into Babylon, come, and I will take care of you.
reflection: God does not always set you free the way you expect. Sometimes the person who unlocks your chains is the last one you would have chosen. But that is how God works. He is not limited by your expectations, and His freedom always comes at exactly the right moment.
question: What chains in your life is God ready to remove, and are you willing to accept freedom even if it looks different from what you imagined?
hook: God does not always set you free the way you expect

text: They departed and lived in Geruth Chimham, which is near Bethlehem, to go to enter into Egypt.
reflection: God sees when fear makes you run. Maybe you are not heading to Egypt, but you know what it feels like to grab the first escape route you can find. God does not want you to outrun your problems. He wants you to bring them to Him.
question: Where are you running right now, and have you asked God if that is where He wants you to go?
hook: When everything falls apart, where do you run?

text: 'Whether it is good, or whether it is bad, we will obey Yahweh our God's voice, to whom we send you; that it may be well with us, when we obey Yahweh our God's voice.'
reflection: God already knows whether you mean it when you say "I will obey whatever you say." He hears the promise and He sees the reservation hiding behind it. Before you ask God for direction, check your heart.
question: Are you ready for an answer you did not choose? What part of your life have you been asking God about while secretly hoping He tells you what you already want to hear?
hook: True obedience starts before you hear the answer.

text: So they came into the land of Egypt; for they didn't obey Yahweh's voice; and they came to Tahpanhes.
reflection: Every step away from God's voice takes you further from His protection. God told them exactly what to do, and they walked the other way. If God has spoken clearly to you, the only safe response is to listen, even when it is hard.
question: Is there something God has told you clearly that you have been avoiding?
hook: Every step away costs more than the last.

text: 'They are not humbled even to this day, neither have they feared, nor walked in my law, nor in my statutes.'
reflection: God sees whether your heart has truly been changed by what He has shown you. Being humbled is not just feeling bad for a moment. It means letting God's truth reshape the way you live. If you are still walking the same path, ask Him to soften what you have been holding back.
question: What has God been trying to show you that you have not yet let change you?
hook: Repeated correction is not punishment. It is love refusing to give up.

text: 'Do you seek great things for yourself? Don't seek them; for, behold, I will bring evil on all flesh,' says Yahweh; 'but I will let you escape with your life.'
reflection: When everything around you is falling apart, God's promise may not be what you hoped for, but it is exactly what you need. He does not promise comfort or success. He promises you Himself, and that is more than enough.
question: What are you clinging to that God is asking you to release?
hook: Sometimes the greatest gift is simply being kept alive.

text: 'Don't be afraid, O Jacob my servant,' says Yahweh; 'for I am with you. For I will make a full end of all the nations where I have driven you.'
reflection: When God disciplines you, He is not trying to destroy you. He is drawing you closer. The same hand that corrects you is the hand that holds you. You are never beyond His reach, and you are never outside His care.
question: Is there an area where you have been mistaking God's correction for His rejection?
hook: Not a full end, but a full presence.

text: 'How can you be quiet, since Yahweh has given you a command?'
reflection: If God has spoken to your heart and you know what He is asking, staying quiet is not caution. It is disobedience. Whatever He has told you to do, today is the day to stop waiting and start moving toward it.
question: What has God asked you to do that you have been putting off?
hook: God does not give optional suggestions.

text: 'Cursed is he who does the work of Yahweh negligently; and cursed is he who keeps back his sword from blood.'
reflection: If God has spoken to your heart and you know what He is asking, going through the motions is not enough. He wants your whole heart in everything He has given you to do. Whatever God has placed in front of you, give it everything you have, because He gave everything for you.
question: Where have you been giving God your leftovers instead of your best?

text: 'Leave your fatherless children. I will preserve them alive. Let your widows trust in me.'
reflection: If there is someone you love that you cannot keep safe, God is asking you to trust Him with them. He sees the people in your life who have no voice, no strength, no protection, and He is saying, I have them. You do not have to carry this alone.
question: Who in your life do you need to trust God with today?

text: 'In those days, and in that time,' says Yahweh, 'the children of Israel will come, they and the children of Judah together; they will go on their way weeping, and will seek Yahweh their God.'
reflection: When you finally turn back to God, you may feel like you have wandered too far. But God has been searching for you the whole time. He does not wait for you to earn your way back. He comes to find you, even when all you bring is your tears.
question: What has kept you from turning back to God with your whole heart?
hook: You have not wandered too far for God.

text: 'Yahweh has produced our righteousness. Come, and let's declare in Zion the work of Yahweh our God.'
reflection: The work of making you right with God was never yours to begin with. God has already produced everything you need to stand before Him. You do not have to earn it, build it, or prove it. You just have to receive it.
question: What are you still trying to earn from God that He has already given you?
hook: Your righteousness comes from God, not from you.

text: In the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-fifth day of the month, that Evilmerodach king of Babylon, in the first year of his reign, lifted up the head of Jehoiachin.
reflection: No matter how long you have been waiting, God has not moved on. He sees you. He knows exactly where you are and exactly when your season will change. If you feel forgotten, hold on. Your day is coming.
question: What are you still waiting on God for?
hook: God has not moved on from you.

text: 'For these things I weep. My eye, my eye runs down with water, because the comforter who should refresh my soul is far from me.'
Arise, cry out in the night, at the beginning of the watches! Pour out your heart like water before the face of the Lord.
It is because of Yahweh's loving kindnesses that we are not consumed, because his compassions don't fail.
The punishment of your iniquity is accomplished, daughter of Zion. He will no more carry you away into captivity.
You, Yahweh, remain forever. Your throne is from generation to generation.
As the appearance of the rainbow that is in the cloud in the day of rain, so was the appearance of the brightness all around. This was the appearance of the likeness of Yahweh's glory.
'You, son of man, don't be afraid of them, neither be afraid of their words, though briers and thorns are with you, and you dwell among scorpions.'
'Son of man, I have made you a watchman to the house of Israel. Whenever you hear a word from my mouth, warn them from me.'
Take for yourself an iron pan, and set it for a wall of iron between yourself and the city. Then set your face toward it.
'Therefore as I live,' says the Lord Yahweh, 'surely, because you have defiled my sanctuary with all your detestable things...'
The slain will fall among you, and you will know that I am Yahweh.
They will cast their silver in the streets, and their gold will be as an unclean thing.
'Therefore I will also deal in wrath. My eye won't spare, neither will I have pity. Though they cry in my ears with a loud voice, yet I will not hear them.'
Yahweh said to him, 'Go through the middle of the city, and set a mark on the foreheads of the men who sigh and cry over all the abominations.'
Yahweh's glory mounted up from the cherub, and stood over the threshold of the house; and the house was filled with the cloud.
'I will give them one heart, and I will put a new spirit within you. I will take the stony heart out of their flesh, and will give them a heart of flesh.'
'Son of man, you dwell in the middle of the rebellious house, who have eyes to see, and don't see, who have ears to hear, and don't hear; for they are a rebellious house.'
Because, even because they have seduced my people, saying, 'Peace;' and there is no peace.
'Therefore tell the house of Israel, Repent, and turn yourselves from your idols! Turn away your faces from all your abominations!'
'Therefore the Lord Yahweh says: As the vine tree among the trees of the forest, which I have given to the fire for fuel, so I will give the inhabitants of Jerusalem.'
'Your fame went out among the nations for your beauty, for it was perfect, through my majesty which I had put on you,' says the Lord Yahweh.
'All the trees of the field will know that I, Yahweh, have brought down the high tree, have exalted the low tree.'
'For I have no pleasure in the death of him who dies,' says the Lord Yahweh. 'Therefore turn yourselves, and live!'
Fire has gone out of the rods of its branches. It has devoured its fruit, so that there is in it no strong rod to be a scepter to rule.'
'I will accept you as a pleasant aroma when I bring you out from the peoples, and gather you out of the countries in which you have been scattered.'
'I will overturn, overturn, overturn it. This also shall be no more, until he comes whose right it is; and I will give it to him.'
'I sought for a man among them who would build up the wall, and stand in the gap before me for the land, that I should not destroy it; but I found no one.'
They will recompense your lewdness on you, and you will bear the sins of your idols. Then you will know that I am the Lord Yahweh.
'I, Yahweh, have spoken it. It will happen, and I will do it. I will not go back. I will not spare. I will not relent.'
'I will execute great vengeance on them with wrathful rebukes. They will know that I am Yahweh, when I lay my vengeance on them.'
'Therefore the Lord Yahweh says: Behold, I am against you, Tyre, and will cause many nations to come up against you, as the sea causes its waves to come up.'
'Your riches, your wares, your merchandise, your mariners, your pilots, your ship repairers, your dealers in merchandise, and all your men of war... will fall into the heart of the seas.'
'Your heart was lifted up because of your beauty. You have corrupted your wisdom by reason of your brightness.'
'It will be no more the confidence of the house of Israel, bringing iniquity to memory, when they turn to look after them. Then they will know that I am the Lord Yahweh.'
For the day is near, even the day of Yahweh is near. It will be a day of clouds, a time of the nations.
'Therefore the Lord Yahweh says: Because he is exalted in stature, and has set his top among the thick boughs, and his heart is lifted up in his height,'
'Pharaoh will see them, and will be comforted over all his multitude, even Pharaoh and all his army, slain by the sword,' says the Lord Yahweh.
Tell them, 'As I live,' says the Lord Yahweh, 'I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked; but that the wicked turn from his way and live.'
'I myself will be the shepherd of my sheep, and I will cause them to lie down,' says the Lord Yahweh.
'As you rejoiced over the inheritance of the house of Israel because it was desolate, so I will do to you.'
'I will also give you a new heart, and I will put a new spirit within you. I will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and I will give you a heart of flesh.'
The Lord Yahweh says to these bones: 'Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and you will live.'
'I will magnify myself and sanctify myself, and I will make myself known in the eyes of many nations.'
'I won't hide my face from them any more, for I have poured out my Spirit on the house of Israel,' says the Lord Yahweh.
The man said to me, 'Son of man, see with your eyes, hear with your ears, and set your heart on all that I show you.'
He measured its length, twenty cubits, and the width, twenty cubits, before the temple. He said to me, 'This is the most holy place.'
He measured it on the four sides. It had a wall around it, the length five hundred, and the width five hundred, to make a separation between what is holy and what is common.
The Spirit took me up and brought me into the inner court; and behold, Yahweh's glory filled the house.
'They shall teach my people the difference between the holy and the common, and cause them to discern between the unclean and the clean.'
'The Lord Yahweh says: Let it suffice you, princes of Israel! Remove violence and plundering, and execute justice and righteousness!'
'When the people of the land come before Yahweh in the appointed feasts, he who enters by the way of the north gate to worship shall go out by the way of the south gate.'
'It will happen that every living creature which swarms, in every place where the rivers go, will live.'
It shall be eighteen thousand reeds in circumference. The name of the city from that day shall be, 'Yahweh is there.'
But Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the king's dainties, nor with the wine which he drank...
reflection: There are moments when everyone around you is going one way and God is asking you to go another. He does not expect you to be fearless. He just wants you to choose him before the pressure starts.
question: What area of your life needs that decision today?
hook: What if the hardest battle you face today is one nobody else can see?

text: In the days of those kings the God of heaven will set up a kingdom which will never be destroyed, nor will its sovereignty be left to another people; but it will break in pieces and consume all these kingdoms, and it will stand forever.
reflection: Every power you have ever trusted in will eventually come to an end. Governments shift, leaders fail, and systems break down. But God has set up a kingdom that nothing can shake, and he invites you to build your life on that.
question: Where are you placing your security in something that will not last?
hook: Empires rise and fall. Only one kingdom survives them all.

text: But if not, be it known to you, O king, that we will not serve your gods, nor worship the golden image which you have set up.
reflection: God never promised that following him would be easy. But he did promise that he would be with you in every fire you walk through.
question: What would your faith look like if you chose to obey God even when you had no guarantee that things would turn out the way you want?
hook: What if you trusted God even when he did not promise to rescue you?

text: All the inhabitants of the earth are reputed as nothing; and he does according to his will in the army of heaven, and among the inhabitants of the earth. No one can stop his hand, or ask him, 'What are you doing?'
reflection: God does not need your permission to work in your life. When everything feels out of control, that is God reminding you that he is the one in charge.
question: Where do you need to stop fighting for control and let God lead?
hook: No one in heaven or on earth can stop what God decides to do.

text: TEKEL: you are weighed in the balances, and are found wanting.
reflection: God is not fooled by anything you hide behind. He sees who you really are, and he still calls you to come to him.
question: What would change if you stopped pretending and let God see the parts of your life you have been covering up?

text: When Daniel knew that the writing was signed, he went into his house (now his windows were open in his chamber toward Jerusalem) and he kneeled on his knees three times a day, and prayed, and gave thanks before his God, as he did before.
reflection: God sees every moment you choose to talk to him, especially when it costs you something.
question: Where in your life has it become easier to stay quiet than to bring your heart to God, and what would it look like to pray anyway?

text: Dominion was given to him, and glory, and a kingdom, that all peoples, nations, and languages should serve him. His dominion is an everlasting dominion, which will not pass away...
reflection: God has given you a place in a kingdom that will never fall.
question: When the things of this world feel shaky and uncertain, how does knowing you belong to something eternal change the way you face today?

text: He said, 'Behold, I will make you know what will be in the latter time of the indignation; for it belongs to the appointed time of the end.'
reflection: God does not owe you a preview of the future, but he promises to walk you through every part of it.
question: What are you anxious about right now that you could release to God, trusting that he already knows how it ends?

text: Lord, hear! Lord, forgive! Lord, listen and act! Don't delay, for your own sake, my God, because your city and your people are called by your name.
reflection: God does not wait for you to clean yourself up before He listens. Daniel came with nothing but honesty and urgency, and God heard him before he even finished speaking.
question: When you feel unworthy of being heard, what would it look like to bring that feeling straight to God instead of hiding from Him?

text: ...Don't be afraid, Daniel; for from the first day that you set your heart to understand, and to humble yourself before your God, your words were heard...
reflection: God heard Daniel the moment he started praying, even though the answer took twenty-one days to arrive. He hears you the same way. The silence does not mean God is ignoring you. He is already moving on your behalf.
question: What prayer have you been waiting on that God may already be answering?

text: He will flatter and win over those who have violated the covenant. But the people who know their God will be strong and take action.
reflection: Knowing about God and knowing God are not the same thing. God wants you to know Him so deeply that when everything around you shakes, you do not.
question: Where in your life is God asking you to move past information and truly know Him?

text: Those who are wise will shine as the brightness of the expanse. Those who turn many to righteousness will shine as the stars forever and ever.
reflection: God measures your life differently than the world does. Every quiet moment you spent pointing someone toward truth, every time you lived faithfully when nobody was watching, that is what lasts.
question: What would it look like for you to live today as someone whose life is meant to shine forever?

text: When Yahweh spoke at first by Hosea, Yahweh said to Hosea, 'Go, take for yourself a wife of prostitution...'
I will betroth you to me forever. Yes, I will betroth you to me in righteousness, in justice, in loving kindness, and in compassion.
Yahweh said to me, 'Go again, love a woman loved by another, and an adulteress, even as Yahweh loves the children of Israel, though they turn to other gods...'
My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge. Because you have rejected knowledge, I will also reject you... you have forgotten the law of your God...
Their deeds won't allow them to turn to their God; for the spirit of prostitution is within them, and they don't know Yahweh.
For I desire mercy, and not sacrifice; and the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings.
Ephraim mixes himself among the peoples. Ephraim is a pancake not turned over.
For they sow the wind, and they will reap the whirlwind...
I found Israel like grapes in the wilderness. I saw your fathers as the first ripe in the fig tree at its first season...
Sow to yourselves in righteousness, reap according to kindness. Break up your fallow ground; for it is time to seek Yahweh, until he comes and rains righteousness on you.
'How can I give you up, Ephraim? How can I hand you over, Israel?... My heart is turned within me, my compassion is aroused.'
Therefore turn to your God. Keep kindness and justice, and wait continually for your God.
Yet I am Yahweh your God from the land of Egypt; and you shall acknowledge no god but me, for there is no savior besides me.
'I will heal their waywardness. I will love them freely; for my anger is turned away from him.'
Sanctify a fast! Call a solemn assembly! Gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land to the house of Yahweh, your God, and cry to Yahweh.
It will happen afterward, that I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh; and your sons and your daughters will prophesy. Your old men will dream dreams. Your young men will see visions.
Multitudes, multitudes in the valley of decision! For the day of Yahweh is near in the valley of decision.
He said: 'Yahweh will roar from Zion, and thunder from Jerusalem... the pastures of the shepherds will mourn, and the top of Carmel will wither.'
Yahweh says: 'For three transgressions of Israel, yes, for four, I will not turn away its punishment; because they have sold the righteous for silver, and the needy for a pair of sandals.'
Do two walk together, unless they have agreed?
Therefore I will do this to you, Israel; because I will do this to you, prepare to meet your God, Israel.
But let justice roll on like rivers, and righteousness like a mighty stream.
Woe to those who are at ease in Zion, and to those who are secure on the mountain of Samaria...
Yahweh said to me, 'Amos, what do you see?' I said, 'A plumb line.' Then the Lord said, 'Behold, I will set a plumb line in the middle of my people Israel...'
'Behold, the days come,' says the Lord Yahweh, 'that I will send a famine in the land, not a famine of bread, nor a thirst for water, but of hearing Yahweh's words.'
'I will bring my people Israel back from captivity, and they will rebuild the ruined cities, and inhabit them...'
The pride of your heart has deceived you, you who dwell in the clefts of the rock, whose habitation is high, who says in his heart, 'Who will bring me down to the ground?'

But Jonah rose up to flee to Tarshish from the presence of Yahweh. He went down to Joppa, and found a ship going to Tarshish; so he paid its fare, and went down into it, to go with them to Tarshish from the presence of Yahweh.
But I will sacrifice to you with the voice of thanksgiving. I will pay that which I have vowed. Salvation belongs to Yahweh.
God saw their works, that they turned from their evil way. God relented of the disaster which he said he would do to them, and he didn't do it.
'Shouldn't I be concerned for Nineveh, that great city, in which are more than one hundred twenty thousand persons who can't discern between their right hand and their left, and also much livestock?'
Hear, you peoples, all of you! Listen, O earth, and all that is therein. Let the Lord Yahweh be witness against you, the Lord from his holy temple.
Shall it be said, O house of Jacob, 'Is Yahweh's Spirit restricted? Are these his doings?' 'Don't my words do good to him who walks blamelessly?'
But as for me, I am full of power by Yahweh's Spirit, and of judgment, and of might, to declare to Jacob his transgression, and to Israel his sin.
Indeed all the nations may walk in the name of their gods, but we will walk in the name of Yahweh our God forever and ever.
But you, Bethlehem Ephrathah, being small among the clans of Judah, out of you one will come out to me that is to be ruler in Israel; whose goings out are from of old, from ancient times.
He has shown you, O man, what is good. What does Yahweh require of you, but to act justly, to love mercy, and to walk humbly with your God?
Who is a God like you, who pardons iniquity, and passes over the disobedience of the remnant of his heritage? He doesn't retain his anger forever, because he delights in loving kindness.
Yahweh is good, a stronghold in the day of trouble; and he knows those who take refuge in him.
For Yahweh restores the excellency of Jacob as the excellency of Israel, although the destroyers have destroyed them and ruined their vine branches.
There is no healing your wound, for your injury is fatal. All who hear the report of you clap their hands over you, for who hasn't felt your endless cruelty?
Behold among the nations, watch, and wonder marvelously; for I am working a work in your days which you will not believe though it is told you.
Behold, his soul is puffed up. It is not upright in him, but the righteous will live by his faith.

For even though the fig tree doesn't flourish, nor fruit be in the vines, the labor of the olive fails, the fields yield no food, the flocks are cut off from the fold, and there is no herd in the stalls,
The great day of Yahweh is near. It is near and hurries greatly, the voice of the day of Yahweh. The mighty man cries there bitterly.
Seek Yahweh, all you humble of the land, who have kept his ordinances. Seek righteousness. Seek humility. Perhaps you will be hidden in the day of Yahweh's anger.
Yahweh your God is within you, a mighty one who will save. He will rejoice over you with joy. He will calm you in his love. He will rejoice over you with singing.
Now therefore this is what Yahweh of Armies says: 'Consider your ways.'
'The latter glory of this house will be greater than the former,' says Yahweh of Armies, 'and in this place I will give peace,' says Yahweh of Armies.
Therefore tell them, Yahweh of Armies says: 'Return to me,' says Yahweh of Armies, 'and I will return to you...'
'Sing and rejoice, daughter of Zion! For behold, I come and I will dwell within you,' says Yahweh.

He answered and spoke to those who stood before him, saying, 'Take the filthy garments off him.' To him he said, 'Behold, I have caused your iniquity to pass from you, and I will clothe you with rich clothing.'
Then he answered and spoke to me, saying, 'This is Yahweh's word to Zerubbabel, saying, \"Not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit,\" says Yahweh of Armies.'
Then he said to me, 'This is the curse that goes out over the surface of the whole land...'
...he will bear the glory, and will sit and rule on his throne. He will be a priest on his throne, and the counsel of peace will be between them both.
Yahweh of Armies says, 'Execute true judgment, and show kindness and compassion every man to his brother.'
These are the things that you should do: speak every man the truth with his neighbor. Execute the judgment of truth and peace in your gates.

Rejoice greatly, daughter of Zion! Shout, daughter of Jerusalem! Behold, your King comes to you. He is righteous, and having salvation, lowly, and riding on a donkey, even on a colt, the foal of a donkey.
Ask of Yahweh rain in the spring time, Yahweh who makes storm clouds, and he gives rain showers to everyone for the plants in the field.
I said to them, 'If you think it best, give me my wages; and if not, keep them.' So they weighed for my wages thirty pieces of silver.
I will pour on David's house, and on the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the spirit of grace and of supplication; and they will look to me whom they have pierced...

I will bring the third part into the fire, and will refine them as silver is refined, and will test them as gold is tested. They will call on my name, and I will hear them. I will say, 'It is my people,' and they will say, 'Yahweh is my God.'
Yahweh will be King over all the earth. In that day Yahweh will be one, and his name one.
A son honors his father, and a servant his master. If I am a father, then where is my honor? And if I am a master, where is the respect due me?...
'For I hate divorce,' says Yahweh, the God of Israel, 'and him who covers his garment with violence...'

Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house, and test me now in this...if I will not open you the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that there will not be room enough for.
But to you who fear my name, the sun of righteousness will arise with healing in its wings. You will go out and leap like calves of the stall.
"Behold, the virgin shall be with child, and shall give birth to a son. They shall call his name Immanuel," which is, being interpreted, "God with us."
reflection: God did not stay in heaven and watch from a distance. He came to be with you. Immanuel means God with us, and that promise has never been canceled. He is with you right now, in whatever you are carrying.
question: Do you believe God is truly with you today?
hook: God Came to Be with You

text: Where is he who is born King of the Jews? For we saw his star in the east, and have come to worship him.
reflection: Have you ever felt a hunger for God that you could not explain? The wise men traveled a long and dangerous road because they believed a King had been born. They did not have a Bible or a church to guide them, but they had a star and a longing that would not let them stop. God honored that hunger and led them all the way to His Son. He will do the same for you when you seek Him with everything you have.
question: Are you chasing Him with that kind of urgency?

text: Behold, a voice out of the heavens said, "This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased."
reflection: God spoke from heaven and called Jesus His beloved Son before He had preached a single sermon or healed a single person. That love was declared before any work was done. If God delights in His Son that way, imagine what it means that He has brought you into that same family.
question: What would change in your life if you truly believed God's love for you was already settled?
hook: Loved Before He Did a Thing

text: But he answered, "It is written, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds out of God's mouth.'"
reflection: When the devil came to Jesus in the wilderness, Jesus did not argue or try to outsmart him. He answered every lie with the truth of Scripture. God has given you the same weapon.
question: When temptation speaks to you, do you know the word of God well enough to answer it back?

text: Even so, let your light shine before men, that they may see your good works and glorify your Father who is in heaven.
reflection: Jesus did not say you might become light someday. He said you are the light of the world right now. Not because of what you have done, but because of who lives in you.
question: What would change in your daily life if you truly believed that?

text: But seek first God's Kingdom and his righteousness; and all these things will be given to you as well.
reflection: Jesus told you not to worry about tomorrow because your Father already knows what you need. He feeds the birds. He clothes the flowers. And you are worth far more to Him than either of those.
question: What worry are you carrying today that God is asking you to hand over to Him?

text: Ask, and it will be given you. Seek, and you will find. Knock, and it will be opened to you.
reflection: Jesus gave you three words for prayer, ask, seek, knock. Each one shows more urgency than the last. God is not hiding from you. He is waiting for you to come.
question: What have you stopped asking Him for because you thought He would not answer?
hook: God Is Waiting for You to Ask

text: The men marveled, saying, "What kind of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?"
reflection: You have probably had Jesus right there with you and still been afraid. The disciples had Him in the boat, and they were still terrified by the storm. The waves were real, but so was His presence.
question: When fear rises in your life, do you look at the waves or at the One who commands them?

text: But you go and learn what this means: 'I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,' for I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
reflection: Jesus did not come for people who thought they had it all together. He came for the broken, the guilty, and the lost. He is not waiting for you to clean yourself up before you come to Him.
question: Have you been holding back from God because you feel too far gone?

text: He who finds his life will lose it; and he who loses his life for my sake will find it.
reflection: Jesus said holding on to your life too tightly is the surest way to lose it. But giving it up for His sake is the only way to find what your heart is really looking for.
question: What part of your life are you gripping so tightly that it keeps you from following Him fully?
hook: Lose Your Life to Find It

text: Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest.
reflection: Jesus did not say, Come to me when you have it figured out. He said, Come to me when you are tired and carrying too much. His invitation is for the worn out and the weighed down.
question: What burden have you been carrying that He is asking you to lay at His feet today?

text: I tell you that every idle word that men speak, they will give account of it in the day of judgment.
reflection: Jesus said every careless word will be accounted for on the day of judgment. Your words reveal what is stored up in your heart.
question: If someone listened to everything you said this past week, what would they learn about what you truly treasure?

text: But blessed are your eyes, for they see; and your ears, for they hear.
reflection: Jesus said many prophets and righteous people longed to see what the disciples were seeing and never did. You hold God's word in your hands. You can read the teachings of Jesus any time you choose.
question: Do you realize what a gift that is, or have you started to take it for granted?
hook: A Gift You Might Be Missing

text: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, "Cheer up! It is I! Don't be afraid."
reflection: The disciples saw a figure walking toward them on the water and they were terrified. But Jesus spoke immediately. It is I. Do not be afraid. In the middle of your darkest moment, He is not far away. He is walking toward you.
question: Can you hear His voice telling you not to be afraid?

text: Then Jesus answered her, "Woman, great is your faith! Be it done to you even as you desire." And her daughter was healed from that hour.
reflection: This woman had no claim on Jesus by birth or religion. She came because her daughter was suffering and she had nowhere else to turn. Jesus called her faith great. God does not measure your faith by your background. He measures it by whether you keep coming to Him.
question: Are you still coming?
hook: Faith That Would Not Give Up

text: For what will it profit a man if he gains the whole world and forfeits his life? Or what will a man give in exchange for his soul?
reflection: Jesus asked the most sobering question anyone will ever hear. What good is it to win everything the world offers and lose your own soul? Nothing you can earn or build or collect will matter on that day.
question: Is there anything you are chasing right now that is costing you what matters most to God?

text: He said to them, "Because of your unbelief. For most certainly I tell you, if you have faith as a grain of mustard seed, you will tell this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you."
reflection: You do not need giant faith to see God move. Jesus told His disciples they failed because of unbelief, not because the task was too hard. Even a mustard seed of trust in God can move what seems impossible in your life.
question: What have you stopped believing God could do for you?

text: For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there I am in the middle of them.
reflection: Jesus promised that wherever two or three gather in His name, He is there. You do not need a big crowd for God to show up. He is present wherever His people call on Him together.
question: When was the last time you gathered with someone to pray and trusted God to meet you there?
hook: He Shows Up When You Gather

text: Looking at them, Jesus said, "With men this is impossible, but with God all things are possible."
reflection: You may have looked at your life and thought some things are just too broken to fix. The disciples felt the same way when they asked, who then can be saved? Jesus answered, with God all things are possible. No one is too lost for God to reach, and no situation in your life is too broken for Him to restore.
question: What impossible thing do you need to trust God with today?

text: even as the Son of Man came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.
reflection: Jesus did not come to be served. He came to serve and give His life for you. The God of the universe lowered Himself to wash feet and die on a cross.
question: If the King of glory serves, how much more should you? Where is God calling you to serve someone else?
hook: The King Who Chose to Serve

text: All things, whatever you ask in prayer, believing, you will receive.
reflection: Jesus promised that whatever you ask in prayer with real faith, you will receive. This is not about getting everything you want. It is about trusting a God who is generous and close and listening.
question: What are you afraid to bring to God because you doubt He will answer?

text: Jesus said to him, "'You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.'"
reflection: When someone asked Jesus which commandment mattered most, He did not list a rule. He said to love God with everything you have. Your heart, your soul, your mind. God is not looking for perfect obedience. He is looking for all of you.
question: What part of your life have you been holding back from God?
hook: The One Thing God Wants Most

text: Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
reflection: Jesus warned that whoever lifts himself up will be brought low, and whoever humbles himself will be lifted up. God does not measure your greatness the way the world does. He honors the humble heart that depends on Him completely.
question: Where in your life is pride keeping you from the place God wants to lift you?

text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
reflection: Everything around you will eventually pass away. But the words of Jesus will never fade. They are the one thing you can build your life on that will never crumble.
question: Are you anchoring your life to what lasts, or to what is passing away?

text: The King will answer them, 'Most certainly I tell you, because you did it to one of the least of these my brothers, you did it to me.'
reflection: Jesus said that when you feed the hungry, welcome the stranger, or visit the sick, you are doing it for Him. Every act of love toward someone in need is an act of love toward God Himself.
question: Who around you is hurting that God might be asking you to reach?
hook: Every Act of Love Reaches God

text: Watch and pray, that you don't enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
reflection: Jesus told His disciples to watch and pray because the spirit is willing but the flesh is weak. He knew they wanted to stand firm, but He also knew they could not do it alone. Neither can you.
question: What temptation do you need to bring to God in prayer before it overtakes you?

text: Now the centurion, and those who were with him watching Jesus, when they saw the earthquake and the things that were done, were terrified, saying, "Truly this was the Son of God!"
reflection: A Roman soldier watched Jesus die and could not deny what he had just seen. Truly this was the Son of God. The cross was not an accident. It was the plan of a God who would do anything to reach you.
question: Have you stopped lately to consider what Jesus went through for you?

text: Go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
reflection: The last words Jesus spoke in Matthew were a promise. I am with you always, even to the end of the age. He did not leave you to figure life out alone. He is with you right now, in every moment.
question: Do you believe Jesus is with you today, even in what feels impossible?

text: Jesus said to them, "Come after me, and I will make you into fishers of men."
reflection: Jesus walked up to ordinary fishermen and invited them to follow Him. He did not ask for their credentials or wait for them to be ready. He simply said, "Come." That same voice calls to you today.
question: Will you follow where Jesus leads, even before you feel qualified?

text: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, "Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance."
reflection: Jesus did not come for people who have it all together. He came for people who know they are broken. He is not waiting for you to clean up before He will sit with you. He wants to meet you right where you are.
question: Do you believe Jesus came for someone like you?

text: For whoever does the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.
reflection: Jesus said that anyone who does the will of God belongs to His family. That means you are not an outsider looking in. If you trust Him and follow Him, you are His brother, His sister, His own.
question: Does your heart believe that you truly belong in the family of God?

text: They were greatly afraid, and said to one another, "Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?"
reflection: The disciples had Jesus in the boat with them, and they were still terrified. Sometimes you will face storms even when God is close. But the One who calmed the sea with a word has not lost His power.
question: Is there a storm in your life that you need to trust Him with today?

text: However, Jesus did not allow him, but said to him, "Go to your house, to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you, and how he had mercy on you."
reflection: After Jesus set this man free, He told him to go home and tell everyone what God had done. Your story of what God has done in your life is one of the most powerful things you carry.
question: Who in your life needs to hear what the Lord has done for you?
hook: A Story Only You Can Tell

text: He said to them, "Come away into a deserted place, and rest awhile." For there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
reflection: Jesus saw that His disciples were exhausted and told them to come away and rest. He sees your weariness too. He is not a taskmaster who drives you until you break. He is a Shepherd who leads you to quiet places.
question: Will you accept the rest He is offering you today?

text: They were astonished beyond measure, saying, "He has done all things well. He makes even the deaf hear and the mute speak!"
reflection: The people watched Jesus heal a man who could not hear or speak, and they said, He has done all things well. That is still true today. Whatever God is doing in your life, even what you do not understand, He is doing well.
question: Can you trust that today?

text: He called the multitude to himself with his disciples, and said to them, "Whoever wants to come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me."
reflection: Jesus did not hide the cost of following Him. He said it plainly. Deny yourself, take up your cross, and follow me. That sounds hard, and it is. But the life He calls you into is better than anything you leave behind.
question: What are you holding onto that Jesus is asking you to lay down?
hook: What It Really Means to Follow

text: Jesus said to him, "If you can believe, all things are possible to him who believes."
reflection: A desperate father said, if you can do anything, help us. Jesus turned those words around. If you can believe, all things are possible. God does not ask for perfect faith. He asks for honest faith.
question: Will you bring your doubt to Him today?

text: For the Son of Man also came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.
reflection: You live in a world that tells you greatness means being served. But Jesus flipped that upside down. He came not to be served, but to give His life as a ransom for you. If the King of kings chose the path of a servant, then God is calling you to a completely different kind of greatness.
question: How is God asking you to serve someone today?

text: Therefore I tell you, all things whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you have received them, and you shall have them.
reflection: When you pray, do you actually believe God is going to answer? Jesus told you to believe you have already received what you ask for. That is not a trick or a formula. It is trust in a God who has never broken a promise to you.
question: When you bring your next request to God, will you pray like you believe He hears you?
hook: Pray Like You Already Have It

text: You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your mind, and with all your strength. This is the first commandment.
reflection: Jesus said the greatest commandment is to love God with everything you have. Not part of your heart, but all of it. That kind of love is something God grows in you as you stay close to Him.
question: Is there a part of your heart you have been holding back from God?
hook: All of You for All of Him

text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
reflection: Everything you see around you will one day pass away. But the words Jesus spoke will stand forever. When the ground beneath your feet feels uncertain, His promises are the one thing that will never move.
question: Are you building your life on things that last, or things that will fade?

text: He said, "Abba, Father, all things are possible to you. Please remove this cup from me. However, not what I desire, but what you desire."
reflection: In the darkest hour of His life, Jesus prayed with complete honesty. He told the Father exactly how He felt and then surrendered everything. When life presses in and you cannot see the way forward, God invites you to do the same thing. Bring your honest pain to Him.
question: Can you trust God with the outcome even when everything in you wants to run?

text: When the centurion, who stood by opposite him, saw that he cried out like this and breathed his last, he said, "Truly this man was the Son of God!"
reflection: A Roman soldier watched Jesus die and said what you need to say too. Truly this man was the Son of God. He came to believe by watching how Jesus suffered. The cross reveals who Jesus really is, and it reveals how far God was willing to go for you.
question: Have you looked closely at the cross and let it change the way you see God?

text: He said to them, "Go into all the world and preach the Good News to the whole creation."
reflection: The last command Jesus gave before He ascended was to go and tell. The good news is not meant to be kept to yourself. God did not save you just for your own sake. He saved you to be part of His mission.
question: Who needs to hear the good news from you?

text: For nothing spoken by God is impossible.
reflection: The angel spoke these words to a young woman who had every reason to doubt. God told her something that made no sense, and she believed it. Nothing God has spoken to you is impossible for Him to do.
question: What promise from God are you struggling to believe right now?

text: For there is born to you today, in David's city, a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
reflection: God announced the birth of His Son to shepherds in a field, not to kings in a palace. He wanted you to know that He brings His best news to the people the world passes by.
question: If you feel overlooked or forgotten, does it comfort you to know that God sees you right where you are?

text: The Holy Spirit descended in a bodily form as a dove on him. A voice came out of the sky, saying "You are my beloved Son. In you I am well pleased."
reflection: Before Jesus did a single miracle or preached a single sermon, the Father spoke over Him. You are my beloved Son. God declared who Jesus was before He did anything public. If you belong to God through Christ, He speaks that same love over you.
question: Do you believe what He says about you?

text: The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to heal the broken hearted, to proclaim release to the captives, recovering of sight to the blind, to deliver those who are crushed,
reflection: Jesus read these words as His own mission statement. He came to bring good news to the poor, heal the broken, and set captives free. If your heart is heavy today, He is speaking to you. God sent Jesus for the very person you are right now.
question: What would change if you believed that?

text: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
reflection: Jesus did not come looking for people who had it all together. He came for the ones who know they are broken. If you feel too far gone or too messed up to deserve God's attention, that is exactly why He is calling you.
question: Can you hear Him?

text: Give, and it will be given to you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, will be given to you. For with the same measure you measure it will be measured back to you.
reflection: God is not keeping score. He is being generous. When you give freely, whether it is your time, your kindness, or your forgiveness, God promises to pour it back into your life beyond what you can hold.
question: Are you holding back because you are afraid there will not be enough?
hook: God Gives More Than You Expect

text: He said to the woman, "Your faith has saved you. Go in peace."
reflection: Jesus spoke these words to a woman everyone had written off. She came to Him broken, and He sent her away whole. Your faith does not need to be perfect. God responds to even your smallest reach toward Him.
question: What is keeping you from reaching out right now?

text: But Jesus hearing it, answered him, "Don't be afraid. Only believe, and she will be healed."
reflection: Jesus spoke these words to a father whose daughter had just died. Everyone told him it was over. But Jesus said, Do not be afraid. Only believe. When everything feels hopeless, God is not done with you. He speaks those same words over your life today.
question: What are you afraid of that God cannot handle?
hook: Do Not Be Afraid, Just Believe

text: He said to all, "If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross daily, and follow me."
reflection: Jesus did not offer an easy road. He said to deny yourself, take up your cross every day, and follow Him. This is not punishment. This is the path to real life.
question: If you are holding on to something God is asking you to release, what would it take for you to let go?
hook: A Cross You Carry Every Day

text: Nevertheless, don't rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.
reflection: The disciples were amazed at what they could do in Jesus' name. But Jesus redirected their joy. Do not celebrate your power. Celebrate that God knows you. What matters most is not what you do for Him, but that He has written your name in heaven.
question: Does that change how you see yourself?
hook: Your Name Is Written in Heaven

text: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him?
reflection: Jesus used the simplest picture to make this point. Even flawed parents give good things to their children.
question: How much more will God, your perfect Father, give you what you truly need? If you have been afraid to ask God for something, what is stopping you from coming to Him right now?
hook: A Father Who Gives Even More

text: Don't be afraid, little flock, for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the Kingdom.
reflection: Jesus calls His followers a little flock. That name is full of warmth. You are not lost in a crowd. God sees you, knows you, and delights in giving you His kingdom.
question: If the Creator takes pleasure in caring for you, what do you have to fear?
hook: God Delights in Giving to You

text: Strive to enter in by the narrow door, for many, I tell you, will seek to enter in, and will not be able.
reflection: Jesus did not soften His answer. The door to life is narrow, and many will try to enter but fail. This is not because God is unwilling. He is holding the door open for you right now. But you have to walk through it, not just stand near it.
question: Are you stepping through today?
hook: The Door That Many Will Miss

text: Whoever doesn't bear his own cross, and come after me, can't be my disciple.
reflection: Jesus was not trying to scare anyone away. He was being honest. Following Him means carrying a cross. It means choosing His way even when it costs you something. God does not ask for perfection, but He does ask for your whole heart.
question: What in your life are you holding back from Him?

text: I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance.
reflection: Heaven throws a celebration when one person turns back to God. Not an entire nation. Not a perfect church. Just one sinner who says, I need you, Lord. God is not counting your failures. He is watching for you to come home.
question: Do you know how much joy your return would bring Him?
hook: Heaven Throws a Party for You

text: He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.
reflection: God is not waiting for you to do something great before He trusts you. He is watching how you treat the small things right in front of you.
question: Are you being faithful with what He has already placed in your hands?

text: Neither will they say, 'Look, here!' or, 'Look, there!' for behold, God's Kingdom is within you.
reflection: Everyone was looking for a kingdom they could see. A throne. A new government. But Jesus said the kingdom of God is already here, inside you. It does not announce itself with trumpets. It grows quietly in your heart.
question: Have you been looking for God in the wrong places?

text: But he said, "The things which are impossible with men are possible with God."
reflection: If a good man could not earn his way to heaven, who can? Jesus said what is impossible for you is possible with God. Salvation is not something you earn. It is a gift God gives freely.
question: Have you stopped trying to earn what only God can give?

text: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost.
reflection: Jesus did not wait for Zacchaeus to clean up his life. He walked right to his tree and called him by name. God does the same with you. He does not wait for you to be ready. He comes to find you exactly where you are.
question: Do you believe He is calling your name today?

text: Now he is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all are alive to him.
reflection: Death does not have the last word over you. God told Moses, I am the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Not I was, but I am. They are alive to Him. Every person you have loved and lost in faith is alive with God right now.
question: Does that truth change how you face each day?

text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will by no means pass away.
reflection: Everything you see will one day be gone. The sky, the mountains, the oceans. But the words of Jesus will never fade. They are more permanent than the ground you stand on. When everything else fails, His promises hold.
question: Are you building your life on something that lasts?

text: Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.
reflection: Jesus did not pretend the cross was easy. He asked His Father to take it away. But then He prayed, Not my will, but yours be done. God does not ask you to pretend that hard things do not hurt. He asks you to trust Him through them.
question: Will you surrender your will and trust God with what hurts the most?

text: Jesus said to him, "Assuredly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise."
reflection: A criminal hanging on a cross, minutes from death, turned to Jesus and asked to be remembered. Jesus answered, Today you will be with me in Paradise. It was not too late for that man.
question: If God saved him in his final breath, is it ever too late for you?

text: He is not here, but is risen. Remember what he told you when he was still in Galilee,
reflection: The women came expecting a dead body, but found an empty tomb. Two angels asked a question that still echoes. Why do you look for the living among the dead? Jesus had already told you this would happen. He keeps every promise He makes.
question: Are you living as if He is truly alive?
hook: He Is Not Here, He Is Risen

text: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
reflection: Before anything existed, Jesus was already there. He is God, and He has always been God. The one who created everything also sees you right now and knows you by name. He is not far away. He is closer to you than your next breath.
question: Are you living today as though the eternal God truly knows you?
hook: The God Who Was Always There

text: This beginning of his signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.
reflection: Jesus revealed His glory at a wedding feast, in the middle of an ordinary problem. He did not wait for something dramatic. He stepped into a simple need and showed who He really was. God does the same thing in your life every day if you have eyes to see it.
question: Are you watching for the ways He is showing Himself in your ordinary moments?

text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
reflection: This is the most famous verse in all of Scripture, and for good reason. God did not wait for you to be good enough. He loved you while you were still lost. He gave His only Son so that you could live.
question: Do you believe that His love is truly for you?

text: but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him will never thirst again; but the water that I will give him will become in him a well of water springing up to eternal life.
reflection: Everything this world offers leaves you thirsty again. Money, success, approval, comfort, none of it lasts. But Jesus offers something different, a satisfaction that never runs dry.
question: Have you tasted the living water He is offering you today?

text: Most certainly I tell you, he who hears my word, and believes him who sent me, has eternal life, and doesn't come into judgment, but has passed out of death into life.
reflection: Jesus says the person who hears His word and believes already has eternal life. Not someday. Right now. You have passed from death to life the moment you trust Him.
question: Do you believe that your salvation is already secure in His hands?

text: Jesus said to them, "I am the bread of life. He who comes to me will not be hungry, and he who believes in me will never be thirsty."
reflection: Jesus called Himself the bread of life. Not a luxury, not an extra, but the thing you cannot live without. Every other source of satisfaction will leave you hungry again.
question: Is Jesus the one thing you are feeding on every day?

text: Now on the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, "If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink!"
reflection: On the greatest day of the feast, in front of everyone, Jesus stood up and called out to anyone who was thirsty. He did not whisper it. He did not hold back. That same invitation is still open to you right now. If you are thirsty for something more, He is calling you.
question: Are you ready to come to Him and drink?

text: Again, therefore, Jesus spoke to them, saying, "I am the light of the world. He who follows me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life."
reflection: Jesus did not say He would show you the light. He said He is the light. When you follow Him, you stop stumbling in the dark. You stop guessing your way through life.
question: Is He the one leading your steps today, or are you still walking on your own?
hook: No More Walking in the Dark

text: He therefore answered, "Whether he is a sinner, I don't know. One thing I do know: that though I was blind, now I see."
reflection: The blind man could not explain everything about what God had done. He did not have all the answers. But he knew one thing for certain. God had changed him. You may not be able to explain every detail of how God has worked in your life. But you know what He did.
question: Can you say, "I was lost, and now I am found"?
hook: One Thing You Know for Sure

text: The thief only comes to steal, kill, and destroy. I came that they may have life, and may have it abundantly.
reflection: Jesus draws a sharp line between the thief and the shepherd. The thief comes to take everything from you. Jesus came to give you everything you need. He offers a life that overflows, not a life barely hanging on.
question: Are you receiving the life He came to give?
hook: The Life He Came to Give You

text: Jesus said to her, "I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will still live, even if he dies."
reflection: Jesus did not promise Martha that her brother would be raised someday. He told her that He Himself is the resurrection and the life. The hope you are looking for is not an event in the future. It is a person standing in front of you right now.
question: Do you believe this?

text: I have come as a light into the world, that whoever believes in me may not remain in the darkness.
reflection: Jesus did not come into a world that was almost fine. He came into darkness. And He did not come to lecture the darkness. He came to light it up so you could find your way out. If you believe in Him, you never have to walk blind again.
question: Are you following His light?

text: A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another. Just as I have loved you, you also love one another.
reflection: Jesus gave His followers one command that would outlast every other mark of religion. He said the world would know you belong to Him not by your words but by your love.
question: If someone watched your life this week, would they see a love that could only come from God?

text: Jesus said to him, "I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father, except through me."
reflection: Jesus did not say He would show you one of the ways to God. He said He is the way. The only way. There is no door to the Father that does not go through the Son.
question: Have you stopped searching for another path and started walking with Him?
hook: The Only Way to the Father

text: I am the vine. You are the branches. He who remains in me, and I in him, the same bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing.
reflection: Jesus did not say you might need Him sometimes. He said apart from Him you can do nothing. Every good thing in your life grows from staying connected to Him. When you try to do it on your own, you dry up.
question: Are you staying close to the vine today?

text: I have told you these things, that in me you may have peace. In the world you have trouble; but cheer up! I have overcome the world.
reflection: Jesus never promised you an easy life. He promised you peace in Him. The trouble is real, but so is the victory. He has already conquered the world that tries to overwhelm you.
question: When the weight of this world presses down on you, can you hear Him say, take heart, I have overcome?

text: This is eternal life, that they should know you, the only true God, and him whom you sent, Jesus Christ.
reflection: Eternal life is not just living forever in heaven. Jesus said it is knowing God personally and knowing Jesus Christ whom He sent. Not knowing about Him, but knowing Him the way you know someone who loves you.
question: Do you know God that way, or are you still keeping your distance?

text: Pilate therefore said to him, "Are you a king then?" Jesus answered, "You say that I am a king. For this reason I have been born, and for this reason I have come into the world, that I should testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice."
reflection: Pilate asked the most important question you will ever hear. What is truth? The answer was standing right in front of him, and it is standing right in front of you now. Jesus came to testify to the truth, and everyone who belongs to Him listens to His voice.
question: Are you listening?

text: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, "It is finished!" Then he bowed his head, and gave up his spirit.
reflection: It is finished. The debt you owed has been paid. The work is done. Jesus did not die as a victim. He completed the greatest rescue in history and then chose to lay down His life for you. Nothing is left for you to add.
question: Will you rest in what He has already done?
hook: Nothing Left for You to Add

text: Jesus said to him, "Because you have seen me, you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen, and have believed."
reflection: Thomas needed to see and touch before he could believe. Jesus did not reject him for it. But then He said something meant for you. Blessed are those who have not seen and have believed. You have never seen His wounds, but you can still say, my Lord and my God.
question: Will you trust Him even though you have never seen Him?

text: So when they had eaten their breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, "Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me more than these?" He said to him, "Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you." He said to him, "Feed my lambs."
reflection: Three times Peter denied Jesus. Three times Jesus asked him, do you love me? For every time Peter said no, Jesus gave him the chance to say yes. That is what God does for you when you fall. He does not leave you in your failure.
question: Have you failed Him and wondered if He still wants you?

text: But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you. You will be witnesses to me in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the uttermost parts of the earth.
reflection: Jesus did not send you out empty. He promised you His Spirit and His power. You are not alone in telling others about Him.
question: Are you depending on the Holy Spirit today, or are you trying to do it all in your own strength?
hook: Power You Were Not Born With

text: Peter said to them, "Repent, and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit."
reflection: Peter did not soften the call or explain it away. Repent. Be baptized. Receive the Holy Spirit. God offers you complete forgiveness and the gift of His presence. That offer is as real for you today as it was at Pentecost.
question: Have you received what God is offering you?

text: Repent therefore, and turn again, that your sins may be blotted out, so that there may come times of refreshing from the presence of the Lord,
reflection: God does not just forgive you. He refreshes you. When you repent and turn back to Him, He blots out your sins and pours out His presence over you. That is not punishment. That is a Father welcoming you home.
question: Are you ready to turn back and be refreshed by God today?

text: There is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven that is given among men, by which we must be saved!
reflection: There is no other name that can save you. Jesus is not one option among many. He is the only way to God. Peter stood before the most powerful men in Israel and said it plainly. That same truth stands over your life today.
question: Do you believe that about Jesus?

text: But Peter and the apostles answered, "We must obey God rather than men."
reflection: The apostles had already been warned once. They were arrested again and told to stop. But Peter answered, We must obey God rather than men. When the world tells you to stay quiet about Jesus, God says speak.
question: Whose voice are you following today?

text: The word of God increased, and the number of the disciples in Jerusalem multiplied greatly. A great company of the priests were obedient to the faith.
reflection: God has given you a gift, and He has placed you somewhere for a reason. When you serve faithfully, He does more through you than you could do on your own.
question: Are you using what God gave you to serve where He has placed you?

text: He kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, "Lord, don't hold this sin against them!" When he had said this, he fell asleep.
reflection: Stephen's last words were a prayer for the men killing him. He asked God to forgive them. That kind of love only comes from being close to God, and He offers it to you.
question: Is there someone God is asking you to forgive today?
