text: Philip opened his mouth, and beginning from this Scripture, preached to him about Jesus.
reflection: The Ethiopian was reading Isaiah but could not understand it. God sent Philip to him on a desert road. Philip opened the Scripture and the man saw Jesus. God does the same for you. When you seek Him, He makes sure you find Him.
question: Are you asking God to open His Word to you?

text: But the Lord said to him, "Go your way, for he is my chosen vessel to bear my name before the nations and kings, and the children of Israel."
reflection: Ananias was afraid of Saul. But God said, He is my chosen vessel. The person you have written off might be the one God is about to transform. No one is beyond the reach of His grace.
question: Is there someone you have given up on that God might still be working in?

text: Peter opened his mouth and said, "Truly I perceive that God doesn't show favoritism;
reflection: Peter believed the gospel was only for the Jews until God sent a vision that changed his mind. God does not show favoritism, and His grace reaches further than you expect. He reached you the same way.
question: Are there walls in your thinking about who God's love can reach?

text: When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, "Then God has also granted to the Gentiles repentance to life!"
reflection: The believers in Jerusalem were upset that Peter went to the Gentiles. But when they heard what God had done, they fell silent and praised Him. God gives repentance to people you might never expect.
question: Have you been holding back praise because God is working in someone you did not choose?

text: But the word of God grew and multiplied.
reflection: Herod accepted worship that belonged to God alone. God struck him down, but His Word kept growing. Your life is held by a God whose power never fades.
question: Where is your trust placed today?

text: Be it known to you therefore, brothers, that through this man is proclaimed to you remission of sins,
reflection: Paul stood before people who had spent their whole lives trying to earn God's approval through the law. He told them the truth. The forgiveness of sins comes through Jesus alone. What the law could never do, Christ has done for you.
question: Are you still trying to earn what God is offering you for free?

text: confirming the souls of the disciples, exhorting them to continue in the faith, and that through many afflictions we must enter into God's Kingdom.
reflection: Paul was stoned and left for dead. He got up and went right back to work. Then he told the new believers what God wanted you to hear too. Through many afflictions we must enter God's kingdom. He did not promise you an easy road.
question: Are you prepared for a faith that costs you something?

text: But we believe that we are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus, just as they are.
reflection: The debate cut to the heart of the gospel. Must you keep the law to be saved? Peter settled the matter. You are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus, not by anything you add to it.
question: Is your assurance built on grace alone?
hook: Saved by Grace, Just Like Them

text: They said, "Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ, and you will be saved, you and your household."
reflection: At midnight in a jail cell, Paul and Silas sang to God. An earthquake broke the prison open. The jailer cried out the question God wants you to ask. What must I do to be saved? The answer for you is the same. Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ.
question: Have you believed?

text: For in him we live, move, and have our being.' As some of your own poets have said, 'For we are also his offspring.'
reflection: Paul said these words to the wisest men in Athens. In him we live, move, and have our being. Every breath you take depends on God, whether you acknowledge Him or not.
question: How aware are you of your total dependence on the One who holds all things together?

text: For I am with you, and no one will attack you to harm you, for I have many people in this city.
reflection: Corinth was one of the most corrupt cities in the ancient world. Yet God told Paul He already had many people there. God sees who belongs to Him before they know it, and He sees the people around you the same way.
question: Have you given up on someone that God has not given up on?
hook: God Has People in Your City

text: So the word of the Lord was growing mightily and prevailing.
reflection: The believers in Ephesus did not negotiate with what held them back from God. They confessed openly and destroyed what had kept them in darkness. God already knows what you are holding onto, and He wants you free.
question: Is there something He is asking you to let go of today?

text: In all things I gave you an example, that so laboring you ought to help the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, 'It is more blessed to give than to receive.'
reflection: God did not design your life to be spent collecting for yourself. Paul worked with his own hands so he could help the weak, proving that it truly is more blessed to give than to receive.
question: Is your life shaped more by what you are getting, or by what you are giving in God's name?

text: Then Paul answered, "What are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be bound, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus."
reflection: Paul's friends wept and begged him not to go to Jerusalem. But his heart was settled. He was ready to die for the name of Jesus. That kind of resolve only comes from knowing God has called you.
question: When He calls you somewhere costly, is your heart settled enough to follow?
hook: Ready to Die for His Name

text: Now why do you wait? Arise, be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord.
reflection: Ananias asked Paul one question. Why do you wait? God had already spoken. His invitation to you is clear. Nothing stands between you and God's mercy but your own hesitation.
question: Is there something keeping you from the God who is ready to wash every sin away?

text: The following night, the Lord stood by him, and said, "Cheer up, Paul, for as you have testified about me at Jerusalem, so you must also testify at Rome."
reflection: Paul was beaten and alone in a cell. That night the Lord stood beside him and said, take courage. God did not remove the danger for you. He did something better. He showed up for you.
question: When your night is darkest, do you believe God Himself is standing right beside you?
hook: The Lord Stood by Him at Night

text: Herein I also practice always having a conscience void of offense toward God and men.
reflection: Paul told a Roman governor that he practiced having a clear conscience before God and all people. His words challenge you today.
question: Are you living with that kind of honesty before God? Is your conscience clear before Him right now, or is there something you need to bring to God today?

text: For if I have done wrong, and have committed anything worthy of death, I don't refuse to die; but if none of those things is true that they accuse me of, no one can give me up to them. I appeal to Caesar!
reflection: Paul said, if I have done wrong, I do not refuse to die. But if I am innocent, no one can hand me over. He trusted God, not human opinion. When you are falsely accused, God sees the truth about you.
question: Can you trust Him with the outcome, even when no one takes your side?

text: to open their eyes, that they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, that they may receive remission of sins and an inheritance among those who are sanctified by faith in me.
reflection: Jesus gave Paul a mission. Open their eyes and turn them from darkness to light, from the power of Satan to God. That same mission now rests on you. God has placed people in your life who need to hear what He has done for you.
question: Who will you point toward the light today?

text: Therefore, sirs, cheer up! For I believe God, that it will be just as it has been spoken to me.
reflection: Paul stood in a raging storm and told everyone to cheer up. He said, I believe God. He had no proof the storm would end, but he trusted what God had spoken. That kind of faith is available to you.
question: When your storm feels endless, do you believe what God has spoken over your life?

text: preaching God's Kingdom, and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness, without hindrance.
reflection: The book of Acts ends with Paul still preaching and still bold. Nothing could stop him. God gave him a message, and he delivered it until the end. That same God has given you a message too. The question for you is simple.
question: Are you sharing it boldly, the way God created you to?

text: For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ, because it is the power of God for salvation for everyone who believes, for the Jew first, and also for the Greek.
reflection: The gospel is not an idea for you to consider. It is the power of God sent to rescue you. God is not ashamed to save you, and He does not want you to be ashamed of Him.
question: Are you living boldly in what God has done for you?

text: Or do you despise the riches of his goodness, forbearance, and patience, not knowing that the goodness of God leads you to repentance?
reflection: God has been patient with you, not because He ignores your sin, but because He loves you and is drawing you back to Himself. His goodness is not weakness. It is an invitation.
question: Have you received that invitation today?

text: for all have sinned, and fall short of the glory of God;
reflection: You have sinned. Every person has. But God did not leave you in that place. He provided a way back to Himself through Jesus. The question is not whether you are good enough. You are not, and neither is anyone else.
question: The question is whether you will accept what God has done for you?

text: Yet, looking to the promise of God, he didn't waver through unbelief, but grew strong through faith, giving glory to God,
reflection: Abraham looked at what God promised, not at what seemed impossible. God asks you to do the same thing.
question: Will you trust what He has said to you even when everything around you says it cannot happen?

text: But God commends his own love toward us, in that while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
reflection: You did not clean yourself up before God reached for you. He looked at your worst and still chose to love you. That is not a love you earn. That is a love that changes everything.
question: How does knowing God loved you at your worst change the way you talk to Him today?

text: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
reflection: You have a choice in front of you every day. Sin offers you something, but it always ends the same way. God offers you something too, and what He gives never runs out.
question: Are you reaching for what will last, or settling for what will fade?
hook: The Gift You Did Not Deserve

text: What a wretched man I am! Who will deliver me out of the body of this death?
reflection: Paul did not pretend he had it all together. He cried out because the struggle was real. If you feel that same war inside you, take heart. God does not expect you to fix yourself. He sent Jesus to deliver you.
question: Have you asked Him for that help today?

text: We know that all things work together for good for those who love God, to those who are called according to his purpose.
reflection: God is not watching your life from a distance. He is weaving every moment together for your good. Even the hard things you cannot explain are being used by a God who loves you.
question: Do you trust that He is working even when you cannot see it?
hook: God Is Working in All of It

text: As it is written, "Behold, I lay in Zion a stumbling stone and a rock of offense; and no one who believes in him will be disappointed."
reflection: God placed Jesus right in front of you. He is either the rock you build your life on or the stone you stumble over. There is no middle ground. Everyone who believes in Him will not be disappointed.
question: Are you trusting Him, or are you still trying to earn what only faith can receive?

text: that if you will confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord, and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
reflection: God made salvation as clear as it could possibly be. Believe in your heart. Say it with your mouth. He is not asking you to climb a mountain or cross an ocean. He is asking you to trust His Son.
question: Have you said yes to Jesus with your whole heart?

text: For of him, and through him, and to him are all things. To him be the glory forever! Amen.
reflection: Everything you have comes from God. Everything you experience passes through His hands. And everything that exists points back to Him. When you see your life that way, worship is the only response that makes sense.
question: Are you living today with that kind of awe toward the God who holds all things together?

text: Therefore I urge you, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable to God, which is your spiritual service.
reflection: God is not asking you for a perfect performance. He is asking for you. All of you. Your time, your body, your choices offered back to the One who gave you everything.
question: What would it look like for you to live today as a living sacrifice to God?

text: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, for its lusts.
reflection: God is not asking you to fight temptation alone. He is telling you to put on Jesus like armor. When you clothe yourself with Christ, you stop feeding what pulls you away from God.
question: What area of your life needs less provision for the flesh and more of Jesus?

text: For if we live, we live to the Lord. If we die, we die to the Lord. Whether then we live or we die, we are the Lord's.
reflection: You belong to God in life and in death. Nothing can change that. Whether your day is full of joy or full of grief, you are His.
question: Does that truth change the way you face what is in front of you today?
hook: You Belong to God Either Way

text: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that you may abound in hope, in the power of the Holy Spirit.
reflection: God wants to fill you with joy and peace right now. Not someday when things are fixed. Right now, in the middle of everything. He is the God of hope, and His Spirit makes you overflow.
question: When was the last time you asked God to fill you with real, present hope?

text: And the God of peace will quickly crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
reflection: God promises to crush the enemy under your feet. Not by your strength, but by His peace. Every battle you face with sin and temptation already has an outcome decided by God. He will win it for you.
question: Are you trusting God's power to defeat what you cannot defeat on your own?

text: For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are dying, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
reflection: The world may call it foolishness, but God chose the cross to rescue you. The very thing that looks weak to the world around you is the power of God working for your salvation.
question: Are you trusting in God's power today, even when the world says it makes no sense?

text: But as it is written, "Things which an eye didn't see, and an ear didn't hear, which didn't enter into the heart of man, these God has prepared for those who love him."
reflection: You have never seen anything as beautiful as what God has waiting for you. No experience you have had even comes close to what He is preparing for those who love Him.
question: What would change in your life today if you truly believed God has something beyond anything you can imagine waiting for you?
hook: What No Eye Has Ever Seen

text: For no one can lay any other foundation than that which has been laid, which is Jesus Christ.
reflection: Everything you build your life on will be tested. God is telling you right now that only one foundation will hold. Jesus Christ is that foundation.
question: Are you building your hopes, your future, and your trust on Him alone?

text: For God's Kingdom is not in word, but in power.
reflection: God is not impressed by what you say about your faith. He is looking at how His power is working in your life right now.
question: Are you living with real, God-given power, or are you settling for words without change?

text: Purge out the old yeast, that you may be a new lump, even as you are unleavened. For indeed Christ, our Passover, has been sacrificed in our place.
reflection: Christ already gave His life for you. He is your Passover Lamb, sacrificed in your place. God is asking you to let go of the old things that do not belong in your new life with Him.
question: What are you still holding onto that He is calling you to release?

text: Or don't you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God? You are not your own,
reflection: God lives in you. The Holy Spirit has made your body His home. You are not your own anymore because God paid for you with the life of His Son.
question: How does knowing that God Himself dwells in you change the way you treat your body and your choices today?
hook: Your Body Is Already a Temple

text: You were bought with a price. Don't become bondservants of men.
reflection: God paid the highest price imaginable for you. You belong to Him now, not to the opinions and demands of other people.
question: Are you living as someone who belongs to God, or are you still letting others define your worth?

text: But if anyone loves God, the same is known by him.
reflection: You do not have to impress God with how much you know. He already knows everything about you, and what matters most to Him is that you love Him.
question: Are you pursuing knowledge about God, or are you pursuing God Himself?

text: Don't you know that those who run in a race all run, but one receives the prize? Run like that, that you may win.
reflection: God has set a race before you, and He wants you to run it with everything you have. You are not running for a prize that fades away. You are running for something eternal that God Himself is keeping for you.
question: Are you running your life with that kind of purpose?
hook: Run Like You Mean to Win

text: No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.
reflection: Whatever temptation you are facing right now, God already knows about it. He has promised that He will not let it be too much for you. He will always give you a way through.
question: Do you believe God is faithful enough to carry you through this moment?
hook: God Always Gives You a Way Out

text: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until he comes.
reflection: Every time you come to the Lord's table, you are telling the world that Jesus died for you and that He is coming back. This is not just a tradition. It is your declaration to God and to everyone around you.
question: When you remember what Christ did for you, does it still move your heart?

text: Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.
reflection: God did not leave you on your own after saving you. He placed you into the body of Christ, and He gave you a specific part to play.
question: What role has God given you in His family, and are you living it out for Him today?

text: But now faith, hope, and love remain—these three. The greatest of these is love.
reflection: God gave you three things that will never be taken from you. Faith to trust Him, hope to wait for Him, and love to know Him. But love is the greatest of all, because love is who God is.
question: Are you living today in the love God has already poured into your heart?
hook: The Greatest of These Is Love

text: for God is not a God of confusion, but of peace, as in all the assemblies of the saints.
reflection: God is not the author of confusion in your life. He is the God of peace. When things around you feel chaotic, remember that God is still in control and He is bringing order to your heart.
question: Are you trusting His peace today, even in the middle of the mess?
hook: God Is Not the Author of Chaos

text: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
reflection: The victory over death is not something you earn. It is something God gives you through Jesus Christ. Every fear you carry about the future has already been answered by what He did for you.
question: Do you believe today that the God who conquered death is fighting for you?

text: Watch! Stand firm in the faith! Be courageous! Be strong!
reflection: God gave you four commands that cover every season of your life. Watch. Stand firm. Be courageous. Be strong. These are not suggestions for someone else. They are for you, right now, in whatever you are facing.
question: Which of these four does God want you to practice most this week?

text: who comforts us in all our affliction, that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, through the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
reflection: God comforts you in your pain, not so the comfort stops with you, but so it flows through you into someone else's life. He is close to you right now, especially in your hardest moments.
question: How has God's comfort already changed you into someone who can sit with others in their hurt?

text: Now thanks be to God, who always leads us in triumph in Christ, and reveals through us the sweet aroma of his knowledge in every place.
reflection: God is leading you in triumph through Christ right now. Even when the day feels like a fight, He is out ahead of you. Everywhere you go, your life carries the fragrance of knowing Him.
question: What would change about today if you truly believed God already won this battle for you?

text: But we all, with unveiled face seeing the glory of the Lord as in a mirror, are transformed into the same image from glory to glory, even as from the Lord, the Spirit.
reflection: God is transforming you every time you turn your face toward Him. You become more like Christ not by striving, but by seeing Him. He lifts the veil and does what you never could on your own.
question: What does it look like for you to simply look at God today and let Him shape you?

text: For our light affliction, which is for the moment, works for us more and more exceedingly an eternal weight of glory,
reflection: Your pain feels heavy right now, but it is not the final word. God calls it light and momentary because He sees what it is producing in you. An eternal weight of glory is on the other side.
question: What would change if you trusted God is turning your suffering into something that lasts forever?

text: Therefore if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old things have passed away. Behold, all things have become new.
reflection: God does not patch the old version of you. He creates someone entirely new. In Christ, the old guilt and the old shame have passed away. You are not defined by what you used to be.
question: How does knowing God has already made you new change the way you see yourself today?
hook: You Are a Brand New Creation

text: (for he says, "At an acceptable time I listened to you. In a day of salvation I helped you." Behold, now is the acceptable time. Behold, now is the day of salvation.)
reflection: God is not telling you to get ready first. He is telling you that right now is when He is ready to meet you. Today is the day of salvation, not tomorrow, not when you feel worthy.
question: What is holding you back from responding to Him right now?

text: For godly sorrow produces repentance leading to salvation, which brings no regret. But the sorrow of the world produces death.
reflection: There is a sorrow that pushes you from God and a sorrow that pulls you into His arms. Godly sorrow leads to repentance with no regret. It draws you closer to Him, not further away.
question: What is God pressing on in your life right now that He wants you to turn from?

text: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that you through his poverty might become rich.
reflection: Jesus gave up the riches of heaven so you could receive everything you need from God. He chose poverty so you could be rich in grace. His sacrifice is the pattern for your own giving.
question: How does knowing what Jesus surrendered for you shape the way you open your hands toward others?

text: Let each man give according as he has determined in his heart, not grudgingly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
reflection: God is not measuring the size of your gift. He is watching the joy in your heart when you give it. Giving from guilt drains you, but giving from gladness connects you to who God is.
question: What would change in your heart if you gave this week with real joy?

text: throwing down imaginations and every high thing that is exalted against the knowledge of God, and bringing every thought into captivity to the obedience of Christ,
reflection: God wants more than your outward behavior. He wants the hidden rooms of your mind under the care of Christ. When you bring your thought life to Him, He replaces the noise with His peace.
question: What thoughts has God been asking you to hand over to Him?

text: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that concern my weakness.
reflection: Most people hide weakness like a shameful secret. But God does His best work right where you feel the most helpless. He is not asking you to perform strength for Him. He already knows where you are fragile.
question: What would change if you stopped hiding your weakness and let God meet you there?

text: He has said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness." Most gladly therefore I will rather glory in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may rest on me.
reflection: God's power shows up most clearly in the places where you are weakest. You do not need to have everything figured out for Him to work in you. His grace is enough for whatever you face right now.
question: What weakness in your life might God be using to reveal His strength?
hook: My Grace Is Enough for You

text: Test yourselves, whether you are in the faith. Examine yourselves. Or don't you know about yourselves, that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless indeed you are disqualified.
reflection: God invites you to look honestly at your own heart. He is not trying to condemn you. He wants you to know for certain that Jesus Christ lives in you. That knowledge changes everything about the way you face today.
question: How would deeper honesty with God change the way you see your own faith?

text: For am I now seeking the favor of men, or of God? Or am I seeking to please men? For if I were still pleasing men, I wouldn't be a servant of Christ.
reflection: Other people's opinions can drown out the voice of God if you let them. Following Christ will cost you some applause. But His approval carries more weight than every room you will ever walk into.
question: Whose opinion are you chasing right now that is pulling you away from what God has asked of you?
hook: Who Are You Trying to Please

text: I have been crucified with Christ, and it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me. That life which I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself up for me.
reflection: The Christian life is not you struggling to fix yourself for God. It is Christ living in you. He does not stand far off and judge your effort. He gives you His own life as your strength.
question: What part of your day needs to be powered by trusting Christ in you instead of pushing harder on your own?

text: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free man, there is neither male nor female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
reflection: In Christ, the labels people use to rank each other lose their grip. God did not accept you because of your background, status, or strength. He accepted you by grace alone.
question: Who in your life might God be asking you to stop measuring and start treating like family?

text: And because you are children, God sent out the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, "Abba, Father!"
reflection: God does not keep you at arm's length. He brings you close as His child and gives you the Spirit of His Son. You can come to Him as Father, not as a stranger begging for permission.
question: How would your prayer life change if you came to God the way a loved child comes home?

text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith,
reflection: The life God is growing in you does not come from pressure or performance. It comes from His Spirit. Love, joy, peace, patience, these are not things you manufacture. They are what God produces in you as you stay near Him.
question: Which fruit do you need His Spirit to grow in you most right now?

text: Let's not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season, if we don't give up.
reflection: Doing good feels pointless some days, especially when nobody seems to notice. But God sees every quiet act of faithfulness you offer Him. He is not wasting any of it. He promises a harvest in due season if you do not quit.
question: What area of faithfulness is God asking you to keep walking in even when you cannot see the fruit?

text: in whom we have our redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace,
reflection: Your forgiveness is not thin or barely enough. It was purchased by the blood of Christ and given from the deep riches of God's grace. He is not holding your past over you.
question: How would today feel different if you stopped dragging old guilt and received God's mercy as it really is?

text: for by grace you have been saved through faith, and that not of yourselves; it is the gift of God,
reflection: Your salvation did not start with you. It started with God's grace, given as a gift through faith. You do not have to wake up each morning proving you deserve what Christ already purchased.
question: How would today change if you stopped performing and received what God has freely given?

text: Now to him who is able to do exceedingly abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that works in us,
reflection: God can do more than you have thought to ask Him for. Your fears are real, but they are not the measure of His power. The same God who saved you is at work in you right now.
question: What prayer have you been holding back because your fear feels bigger than your faith?

text: And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.
reflection: God is not telling you to forgive by pretending the wound was small. He is asking you to forgive from the mercy He has already poured into you through Christ. His kindness toward you is the pattern for your kindness toward others.
question: Who comes to mind that God is asking you to release today?

text: Walk in love, even as Christ also loved you and gave himself up for us, an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweet-smelling fragrance.
reflection: Christ did not love you from a distance. He stepped close and gave His life for you. That sacrifice is the model God is holding up for the way you live now.
question: What would it look like for you to love someone this week the way Christ loves you, with real cost and real presence?

text: Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.
reflection: You cannot fight today's battles in your own strength and expect to stand. God has given you real armor for a real fight. He has not left you exposed.
question: What battle in your life right now needs God's strength instead of your own?
hook: Put On the Armor of God

text: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
reflection: Paul could say that life meant Christ and death meant gain because Jesus held the center of everything. God wants to grow that same love in you.
question: What would have to shift in your heart for Jesus to hold that place?

text: Have this in your mind, which was also in Christ Jesus,
reflection: God is calling you to look at Jesus and let His humility reshape how you treat people. He grows that kind of heart in you through surrender and love, not pride. He is ready to meet you right where you are.
question: Where do you most need God to replace pride with the mind of Christ?

text: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.
reflection: God is not calling you to drift. He is calling you forward in Christ. Your past does not have to own you, and your failures do not define your future. He has already taken hold of you.
question: What would it look like for you to stop looking back and press forward with Him today?

text: I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.
reflection: Philippians 4:13 is not God promising you that you can do whatever you want. It is Christ meeting you in what He calls you to face. His strength can hold you steady in weakness and need. God is not asking you to be strong.
question: Where do you need Christ's strength instead of your own today?

text: He is before all things, and in him all things are held together.
reflection: Jesus is not one name on a long list of things that matter. He is before all things, and in Him all things hold together. Your life is not hanging in empty space. God's Son is holding you right now.
question: Where do you need to trust Him with what feels unstable?

text: and in him you are made full, who is the head of all principality and power;
reflection: God is not asking you to fill your emptiness with temporary things. In Christ you are made full. The life and standing you need are not found in yourself, but in Him. He keeps drawing you back to His Son.
question: Where are you still looking for fullness outside of Christ?

text: And whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord and not for men,
reflection: God sees your work when no one else notices. He does not measure you by what impresses others. When you give your effort to Him, even the smallest task carries weight. You are serving the Lord who already loves you.
question: What would change if you worked today as though only God were watching?

text: Let your speech always be with grace, seasoned with salt, that you may know how you ought to answer each one.
reflection: God cares about the words you speak. He does not want your voice used for empty talk. He wants your speech to carry His grace into every conversation. When you speak with kindness and truth, you reflect the God who first spoke life into you.
question: How are your words carrying His grace today?

text: remembering without ceasing your work of faith and labor of love and patience of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, before our God and Father.
reflection: God sees three things at work in you when your faith is real. Faith that labors, love that serves, and hope that endures. These are not things you produce on your own. They grow because God is alive in you.
question: Which of these three is God strengthening in your life right now?

text: For this cause we also thank God without ceasing, that, when you received from us the word of the message of God, you accepted it not as the word of men, but, as it is in truth, the word of God, which also works in you who believe.
reflection: God's word is not just good advice from a wise teacher. It is the living voice of the God who made you. When you receive it as truth, it works in you and changes you from the inside.
question: How are you receiving God's word, as information or as something that reshapes your life?
hook: The Word That Works Inside You

text: and the Lord make you to increase and abound in love toward one another and toward all men, even as we also do toward you,
reflection: God is not asking you to manufacture love on your own. He is asking you to let Him increase it in you until it overflows. His love for you is the source, and when you stay close to Him, it spills into every relationship.
question: Where is God growing your love for others right now?

text: then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air. So we will be with the Lord forever.
reflection: God has not left you without hope. The same Jesus who died and rose again will come back for you. Every believer who has died in Christ will rise, and you will be with the Lord forever.
question: How does that promise change the way you face today?
hook: Caught Up to Meet the Lord

text: Rejoice always.
reflection: God did not say rejoice when life is easy. He said rejoice always. That kind of joy comes from knowing the God who loves you is still in control and still with you.
question: What would change if you chose to rejoice in Him today no matter what you are facing?

text: To this end we also pray always for you, that our God may count you worthy of your calling, and fulfill every desire of goodness and work of faith with power,
reflection: God is the one who counts you worthy. He is the one who fulfills every good desire in your heart. You do not have to earn His calling. He gives you the power to walk in it.
question: How does knowing God is working in you change the way you face today?

text: So then, brothers, stand firm, and hold the traditions which you were taught by us, whether by word, or by letter.
reflection: God is not asking you to stand on your own. He already loved you and gave you an everlasting comfort through His grace. When everything around you shifts, the truth you have received from God does not change.
question: What truth from God is holding you steady today?
hook: Hold On to What You Learned

text: But the Lord is faithful, who will establish you and guard you from the evil one.
reflection: You do not have to protect yourself from every attack. God is faithful, and He has promised to establish you and guard you from the evil one. His faithfulness does not depend on yours. He holds you because He loves you.
question: What would change if you truly trusted that God is guarding you right now?

text: This saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am chief.
reflection: Christ Jesus came for sinners, and Paul called himself the worst of them. That is good news for you. No matter where you have been, God's grace can still reach you.
question: What have you been holding back from God, thinking it is too broken for Him to touch?

text: For there is one God and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus,
reflection: There is one God and one way to reach Him, through Christ Jesus. You do not need anyone else to stand between you and God. Jesus already did that for you. He gave Himself so you could come to the Father freely.
question: How does knowing Jesus is your mediator change the way you pray?
hook: Only One Way to Reach God

text: Without controversy, the mystery of godliness is great: God was revealed in the flesh, justified in the spirit, seen by angels, preached among the nations, believed on in the world, and received up in glory.
reflection: The mystery of godliness is great because God Himself entered into human flesh. He came where you are so you could be brought to where He is. The God who made everything became a man for you.
question: How does knowing that God came to you in human form shape the way you worship Him?

text: Let no man despise your youth; but be an example to those who believe, in word, in your way of life, in love, in spirit, in faith, and in purity.
reflection: God does not measure your impact by your age. He calls you to be an example in the way you speak, the way you live, and the way you love. Your faith and purity are visible to the people around you.
question: Where is God calling you to lead by example right now?

text: But if anyone doesn't provide for his own, and especially his own household, he has denied the faith, and is worse than an unbeliever.
reflection: God connects your faith to the way you care for the people closest to you. Providing for your household is not just a practical duty. It is an expression of the faith God placed in your heart.
question: Who in your life is God asking you to care for more intentionally right now?
hook: Take Care of Your Own Family

text: Fight the good fight of faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called, and you confessed the good confession in the sight of many witnesses.
reflection: God calls you to fight for the faith He placed in your heart. He tells you to take hold of eternal life, because it is yours through Christ. This fight is not one you lose. God wins it through you.
question: What are you fighting for, and is it the fight God called you to?

text: For God didn't give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and self-control.
reflection: God did not give you a spirit of fear. He gave you power, love, and a sound mind. When anxiety rises or doubt creeps in, you can stand firm because the Spirit living in you is stronger than anything you face.
question: How is God's spirit of power showing up in your life today?
hook: God Did Not Give You Fear

text: Give diligence to present yourself approved by God, a workman who doesn't need to be ashamed, properly handling the word of truth.
reflection: God calls you to be a worker who does not need to be ashamed. He wants you to handle His truth with care and honesty. When you study His word and live by it, you present yourself approved before God.
question: How are you handling the word of truth that God has given you?
hook: A Worker Who Is Not Ashamed

text: Every Scripture is God-breathed and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for instruction in righteousness,
reflection: Every word of Scripture carries the breath of God. He breathed it out for you, to teach you, to correct you, and to train you in the way you should go. His word is alive and always working in your life.
question: How is God using Scripture to shape you right now?

text: I have fought the good fight. I have finished the course. I have kept the faith.
reflection: Paul could say he fought the good fight, finished the course, and kept the faith. Those words were not boasting. They were the testimony of a life spent following God with everything he had.
question: When your life is over, will you be able to say the same? What fight is God calling you to finish?

text: in hope of eternal life, which God, who can't lie, promised before time began,
reflection: God cannot lie. That is not just a description of His character. It is your guarantee. Before time began, He promised you eternal life, and nothing can undo that promise. Your hope rests on the faithfulness of a God who has never broken His word.
question: What promise from God are you holding onto today?

text: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men,
reflection: The grace of God has appeared and it reaches every person, including you. God's grace does not just save you from what you were. It trains you to live in a way that honors Him. His grace is at work in you right now.
question: How is God's grace teaching you to live differently today?

text: not by works of righteousness which we did ourselves, but according to his mercy, he saved us, through the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit,
reflection: God did not save you because of anything you did right. He saved you because of His mercy. Through the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit, God made you new from the inside out.
question: What area of your life is God still renewing by His Spirit right now?

text: that the sharing of your faith may become effective, in the knowledge of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus.
reflection: God wants your faith to bear fruit in the lives of others. When you see every good thing He has placed in you through Christ, your faith overflows to the people around you. It is not just for you.
question: What good thing has God given you that someone close to you needs to see?

text: His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, who, when he had by himself purified us of our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high,
reflection: God spoke His final word through Jesus. Everything you need to know about who God is and what He thinks of you is found in His Son.
question: Are you listening to Jesus, or are you still searching for God's voice in places He has already moved past?

text: For in that he himself has suffered being tempted, he is able to help those who are tempted.
reflection: Jesus became like you so He could reach you where you actually are. He stepped into your weakness and your pain so He could carry you through it.
question: Are you bringing your struggles to Him, or are you still pretending you can handle them alone?
hook: He Was Tempted Just Like You

text: but exhort one another day by day, so long as it is called "today," lest any one of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.
reflection: A hard heart does not happen overnight. It happens one ignored day at a time. Sin whispers lies so gently that you do not notice your heart getting harder.
question: Are you encouraging someone in your life today, or are you letting another day pass without the honesty God says you need?

text: Let's therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and may find grace for timely help.
reflection: God invites you to come to His throne boldly, not after you clean up, but right now in the middle of your mess. Jesus opened the door and He left it open.
question: Are you coming to God with confidence, or are you staying away because you think you need to fix yourself first?

text: though he was a Son, yet learned obedience by the things which he suffered.
reflection: Suffering is not a sign that God forgot about you. It is how He shaped even His own Son. Jesus learned obedience through pain, and God may be teaching you the same way.
question: Are you trusting God in your suffering, or are you assuming He must not care because it hurts?

text: This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, a hope both sure and steadfast and entering into that which is within the veil,
reflection: God gave you a hope that is anchored to something that cannot move. When everything around you shakes, this anchor holds because it is fixed in the presence of God Himself.
question: Are you resting in that anchor, or are you still trying to find stability in things that will not last?

text: Therefore he is also able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him, seeing that he lives forever to make intercession for them.
reflection: Jesus never stops praying for you. Right now, this very moment, He is before the Father speaking your name. There is no gap in His coverage of your life.
question: Are you living like someone who has an advocate in heaven, or have you forgotten that He is there?

text: For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness. I will remember their sins and lawless deeds no more.
reflection: God does not keep a record of what He already forgave. When He looks at you, He does not see your past. He sees the new covenant written on your heart.
question: Are you living in the freedom of God's forgiveness, or are you still punishing yourself for sins He already forgot?

text: how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish to God, cleanse your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?
reflection: The blood of Jesus does not just forgive what you did. It frees your conscience so you can actually live for God without dragging guilt behind you.
question: Are you serving God with a clean heart, or is old guilt still holding you back from the life He already freed you to live?

text: not forsaking our own assembling together, as the custom of some is, but exhorting one another, and so much the more as you see the Day approaching.
reflection: You were not built to carry your faith alone. God placed you in a family of believers because He knows you need them and they need you. Gathering together is how God keeps your heart strong.
question: Are you leaning into the community God gave you, or are you trying to do this on your own?

text: Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen.
reflection: Faith is not seeing and then trusting. It is trusting before you see. God has given you promises that your eyes cannot verify yet, but your heart can hold right now.
question: Are you waiting for proof before you believe, or are you trusting God with what you cannot see?

text: looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising its shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.
reflection: The race God set before you is not about speed. It is about who you are looking at. Jesus endured the cross because He saw something worth the pain, and that something includes you.
question: Are you fixing your eyes on Jesus, or are you still staring at the weight you were told to drop?

text: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
reflection: Jesus has never changed and He never will. The same Jesus who healed the sick, welcomed sinners, and loved the broken is the same Jesus standing with you right now.
question: Are you building your confidence on the One who never changes, or are you still looking for stability in things that shift every day?

text: But be doers of the word, and not only hearers, deluding your own selves.
reflection: God gave you His word to change how you live, not just how you feel. Hearing truth and doing nothing about it is the definition of fooling yourself.
question: Are you a doer of what God has shown you, or are you still just a listener who nods and walks away unchanged?

text: For as the body apart from the spirit is dead, even so faith apart from works is dead.
reflection: Faith that never moves your hands is not alive. God did not save you so you could believe the right things and do nothing about them.
question: Are you letting your faith move you to action, or is it just something you carry in your head?

text: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceful, gentle, reasonable, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, without hypocrisy.
reflection: The wisdom you follow reveals whose voice you are listening to. God's wisdom is gentle, pure, and full of mercy. The world's wisdom is competitive and restless.
question: Are you following God's wisdom in your decisions, or are you calling the world's voice wise because it gets results?

text: Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
reflection: God promised that if you come closer to Him, He will come closer to you. The distance you feel is not because God walked away. He is right where He has always been.
question: Are you drawing near to God, or are you blaming Him for a distance that you created?

text: Confess your offenses to one another, and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The insistent prayer of a righteous person is powerfully effective.
reflection: God says your prayers are powerful. Not because you are impressive, but because the God who hears them is. Elijah was an ordinary person, and God shook the heavens when he prayed.
question: Are you praying like your prayers matter to God, or have you stopped believing He is listening?

text: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to his great mercy caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
reflection: God did not give you a hope that depends on how your week is going. He gave you a hope that is alive because He is alive.
question: Are you living like someone whose future is guaranteed by God, or are you still acting like everything depends on you?
hook: Born Again to a Living Hope

text: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God's own possession, that you may proclaim the excellence of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.
reflection: God says you are chosen, royal, and His own. That is not what you earned. That is what He declared.
question: Are you living from the identity God gave you, or are you still letting your failures tell you who you are?

text: But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts. Always be ready to give an answer to everyone who asks you a reason concerning the hope that is in you, with gentleness and respect,
reflection: God says to be ready to explain your hope with gentleness, not arguments. The way you handle trouble should make people curious about what you have.
question: Is your life making anyone ask where your peace comes from, or does your worry look just like everyone else's?
hook: Always Ready to Give an Answer

text: And above all things be earnest in your love among yourselves, for love covers a multitude of sins.
reflection: God says earnest love is the most urgent thing in your life right now. Not someday love, not when-it-is-easy love, but love that costs you something today.
question: Are you loving the people around you like time is running out, or are you waiting for a more convenient moment?

text: casting all your worries on him, because he cares for you.
reflection: God told you to hand Him every worry you are carrying. Not manage it better, not pray about it and keep holding it, but actually let go.
question: Are you casting your burdens on God, or are you praying about them and then picking them right back up?

text: by which he has granted to us his precious and exceedingly great promises; that through these you may become partakers of the divine nature, having escaped from the corruption that is in the world by lust.
reflection: God has already given you everything you need to live the life He is calling you to. His promises are not wishful thinking. They are the power that changes you from the inside out.
question: Are you building on what God has given you, or are you still living like something is missing?

text: the Lord knows how to deliver the godly out of temptation and to keep the unrighteous under punishment for the day of judgment,
reflection: God has been rescuing His people since the beginning, and He has never lost one. He is not confused about your situation. He sees exactly where you are.
question: Are you trusting Him to bring you through, or are you trying to find the exit on your own?
hook: God Knows How to Rescue You

text: The Lord is not slow concerning his promise, as some count slowness; but he is patient with us, not wishing that anyone should perish, but that all should come to repentance.
reflection: What looks like delay from God is actually patience. He is holding the door open so more people can come through. That patience saved you once, and right now it is holding for someone you love.
question: Are you using the time God is giving, or are you wasting it?

text: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive us the sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
reflection: God does not ask you to clean yourself up before you come to Him. He asks you to be honest. When you confess, He forgives and He cleanses.
question: Are you still pretending with God, or are you ready to bring Him the truth about where you really are?

text: The world is passing away with its lusts, but he who does God's will remains forever.
reflection: Everything you are chasing in this world has an expiration date. But when you give your life to what God wants, you are building on something that will never fade.
question: Are you investing your heart in what lasts, or are you pouring it into what is already passing away?

text: See how great a love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God! For this cause the world doesn't know us, because it didn't know him.
reflection: God calls you His child. Not because you earned it, but because His love is that wild and that generous. The world will never understand that.
question: Are you living like someone who belongs to God, or are you still trying to prove your worth on your own?

text: We love him, because he first loved us.
reflection: God loved you before you ever thought about loving Him. Everything good in your heart started with Him.
question: Are you letting that love flow through you to others, or are you keeping it to yourself?

text: This is the boldness which we have toward him, that if we ask anything according to his will, he listens to us.
reflection: God does not make you guess about whether He hears you. He told you plainly that when you ask according to His will, He listens.
question: Are you praying with that kind of boldness, or are you still tiptoeing around God like He might not answer?

text: This is love, that we should walk according to his commandments. This is the commandment, even as you heard from the beginning, that you should walk in it.
reflection: Love is not a feeling you keep to yourself. It is obedience you walk in every single day. God defined love for you clearly. It is walking according to His commandments.
question: Are you walking in love the way God defined it, or are you calling something love that looks nothing like obedience?

text: I have no greater joy than this: to hear about my children walking in truth.
reflection: The greatest joy you can bring someone who invested in your faith is to keep walking in truth. God placed people in your life who poured into you so you could walk this road faithfully.
question: Are you walking in the truth they taught you, or have you drifted from the path they set you on?

text: Now to him who is able to keep you from stumbling, and to present you faultless before the presence of his glory in great joy,
reflection: God is the One keeping you from falling. He is not watching from a distance hoping you make it. He is actively holding you up. One day He will present you faultless before His glory.
question: Are you resting in God's power to keep you, or are you exhausting yourself trying to keep yourself?
hook: He Will Keep You from Falling

text: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. He laid his right hand on me, saying, "Don't be afraid. I am the first and the last,"
reflection: Jesus is not waiting for you to clean yourself up before He loves you. He already loves you, and He already washed you. His blood covered your sin before you even knew how deep it went.
question: What would change in your life today if you truly believed you were already clean?

text: But I have this against you, that you left your first love.
reflection: Jesus does not promise your life will be easy. But He promises your suffering has a limit and that He sees every moment of it. He is right here with you in it.
question: What would it look like for you to trust Him with the hardest thing you are carrying today?

text: Behold, I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, then I will come in to him and will dine with him, and he with me.
reflection: When God corrects you, it can feel uncomfortable. But Jesus says His correction is proof that He loves you. He is not pushing you away. He is pulling you closer.
question: What is God correcting in your life right now, and how does it change things to know He is doing it because He loves you?
hook: He Corrects the Ones He Loves

text: "Worthy are you, our Lord and God, the Holy One, to receive the glory, the honor, and the power, for you created all things, and because of your desire they existed and were created!"
reflection: God did not create you because He needed something from you. He created you because He wanted you. Every breath you take exists because He desired it.
question: How does knowing that God wanted you here change the way you see yourself today?

text: They sang a new song, saying, "You are worthy to take the book and to open its seals, for you were killed, and bought us for God with your blood out of every tribe, language, people, and nation,"
reflection: Jesus did not just die to save people in general. He died to buy you specifically. Out of every tribe, language, and nation, God reached into your life and made you His.
question: How does knowing that your redemption was personal and deliberate change how you see your worth today?

text: For the great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand?
reflection: If you have ever cried out to God and asked how long you have to wait, you are in good company. The saints in heaven asked the same question. God heard them and gave them rest.
question: What would it mean for you to trust that He hears you too?

text: for the Lamb who is in the middle of the throne shepherds them and leads them to springs of life-giving waters. And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
reflection: The same Jesus who died for you is now your shepherd. He is leading you toward living water right now, even if you cannot see it yet.
question: What is one place in your life where you need God to lead you to rest today?

text: The smoke of the incense, with the prayers of the saints, went up before God out of the angel's hand.
reflection: Every prayer you have ever whispered has gone somewhere. It rose before the throne of God, mixed with the intercession of Jesus Himself.
question: How does it change the way you pray knowing that not a single word has been wasted?

text: The rest of mankind, who were not killed with these plagues, didn't repent of the works of their hands, that they wouldn't worship demons and the idols of gold, and of silver, and of brass, and of stone, and of wood, which can neither see, nor hear, nor walk.
reflection: God sends warning after warning, not to scare you but to get your attention. The question is never whether God is reaching out. The question is whether you are willing to respond.
question: What is God asking you to let go of today?

text: and swore by him who lives forever and ever, who created the heaven and the things that are in it, the earth and the things that are in it, and the sea and the things that are in it, that there will no longer be delay,
reflection: God's word is not always comfortable. Sometimes it shows you things about yourself you did not want to see. But that honesty is part of how He loves you.
question: What part of God's word has been sweetest to you, and what part has been hardest to accept?

text: The seventh angel sounded, and great voices in heaven followed, saying, "The kingdom of the world has become the Kingdom of our Lord and of his Christ. He will reign forever and ever!"
reflection: No matter how chaotic things look around you, God has not lost control. His kingdom is coming, and nothing in this world can stop it.
question: How does knowing that God will have the final word change the way you face your struggles today?

text: They overcame him because of the Lamb's blood, and because of the word of their testimony. They didn't love their life, even to death.
reflection: You do not overcome the enemy by being strong enough on your own. You overcome because of what Jesus already did for you.
question: Where in your life do you need to stop fighting in your own strength and start standing on what God has already accomplished?

text: If anyone is to go into captivity, he will go into captivity. If anyone is to be killed with the sword, he must be killed. Here is the endurance and the faith of the saints.
reflection: Before the world was made, God already knew your name. Your identity is not defined by what pressures you or what the world demands from you. It was settled by God before anything else existed.
question: How does knowing that God chose you before the foundation of the world change the way you see yourself today?

text: I heard a voice from heaven saying, "Write, 'Blessed are the dead who die in the Lord from now on.'" "Yes," says the Spirit, "that they may rest from their labors; for their works follow with them."
reflection: Death is not the end of your story if you belong to God. It is the beginning of rest that nothing in this world can take away from you.
question: What would change in your life today if you truly believed that your future with God is already secure?

text: Who wouldn't fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? For you only are holy. For all the nations will come and worship before you. For your righteous acts have been revealed.
reflection: God is not just powerful. He is holy in a way that nothing and no one else can be. When you worship, you are responding to the only One in the universe who is truly worthy.
question: What would it look like for you to give God the reverence He deserves today?

text: "Behold, I come like a thief. Blessed is he who watches, and keeps his clothes, so that he doesn't walk naked, and they see his shame."
reflection: Jesus says He is coming when you least expect it. That is not a threat. It is an invitation to live each day so close to Him that His return would not catch you off guard.
question: What would you change about today if you knew Jesus was coming back tomorrow?
hook: He Is Coming Like a Thief

text: These will war against the Lamb, and the Lamb will overcome them, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings; and those who are with him are called, chosen, and faithful.
reflection: Every power that has ever stood against God will fall. The Lamb wins, and if you are with Him, you share in that victory. Not because you earned it, but because He called you.
question: How does knowing that you are on the winning side change the way you face opposition today?
hook: The Lamb Will Win the War

text: I heard another voice from heaven, saying, "Come out of her, my people, that you have no participation in her sins, and that you don't receive of her plagues,"
reflection: Every wrong done against you is known by God. He does not forget, and He does not look away. His justice may not come on your timeline, but it will come.
question: How does trusting in God's justice help you release the need to make things right on your own?

text: He said to me, "Write, 'Blessed are those who are invited to the marriage supper of the Lamb.'" He said to me, "These are true words of God."
reflection: Heaven erupts in praise the moment God's justice is complete. That same God holds your life in His hands right now.
question: What would it look like for you to praise God today, not just for what He has done, but for who He is?

text: If anyone was not found written in the book of life, he was cast into the lake of fire.
reflection: Your life is an open book before God. Nothing you have done or thought is hidden from Him. One day every person will stand before His throne, and the books will be opened.
question: If God were to open the book of your life today, would you be ready?

text: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more; neither will there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain any more. The first things have passed away.
reflection: God is not far away from you. He is not watching from a distance. This passage promises that He will make His home with you, and every tear you have carried will be wiped away by His own hand.
question: What would it mean for you to believe that God is really that close?
hook: God Moves In with His People

text: He who testifies these things says, "Yes, I come quickly." Amen! Yes, come, Lord Jesus.
reflection: The very last invitation in the Bible is for you. God does not close His book with a list of conditions. He closes it with an open door. He says come, and He means you.
question: What is stopping you from accepting that invitation today?
